{"content":"1: name: linux-binary-build
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build_name:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: The build's name
10:       build_environment:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: The build environment
14:       runner_prefix:
15:         required: false
16:         default: ""
17:         type: string
18:         description: prefix for runner label
19:       runs_on:
20:         required: false
21:         default: linux.12xlarge.memory.ephemeral
22:         type: string
23:         description: Hardware to run this "build" job on, linux.12xlarge or linux.arm64.2xlarge.
24:       timeout-minutes:
25:         required: false
26:         default: 240
27:         type: number
28:         description: timeout for the job
29:       ALPINE_IMAGE:
30:         required: false
31:         type: string
32:         default: "308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine"
33:         description: Alpine image to use
34:       PYTORCH_ROOT:
35:         required: true
36:         type: string
37:         description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository
38:       PACKAGE_TYPE:
39:         required: true
40:         type: string
41:         description: Package type
42:       DESIRED_CUDA:
43:         required: true
44:         type: string
45:         description: Desired Cuda version
46:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION:
47:         required: false
48:         type: string
49:         description: GPU Arch version
50:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE:
51:         required: true
52:         type: string
53:         description: GPU Arch type
54:       DOCKER_IMAGE:
55:         required: true
56:         type: string
57:         description: Docker image to use
58:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:
59:         required: true
60:         type: string
61:         description: Docker image tag to use
62:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG:
63:         required: false
64:         type: string
65:         description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)
66:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT:
67:         required: false
68:         type: string
69:         description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)
70:       DESIRED_PYTHON:
71:         required: false
72:         type: string
73:         description: Desired python version
74:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS:
75:         required: false
76:         type: string
77:         description: Extra install requirements
78:         default: ""
79:     secrets:
80:       github-token:
81:         required: true
82:         description: Github Token
83: 
84: permissions:
85:   id-token: write
86: 
87: jobs:
88:   build:
89:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runs_on }}
90:     timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}
91:     env:
92:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_ROOT }}
93:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}
94:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in
95:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION
96:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}
97:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}
98:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}
99:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
100:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1
101:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}
102:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}
103:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}
104:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}
105:       ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
106:       AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
107:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env
108:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build_environment }}
109:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
110:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
111:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts
112:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
113:     steps:
114:       - name: Make the env permanent during this workflow (but not the secrets)
115:         shell: bash
116:         run: |
117:           {
118:             echo "PYTORCH_ROOT=${{ env.PYTORCH_ROOT }}"
119:             echo "PACKAGE_TYPE=${{ env.PACKAGE_TYPE }}"
120:             echo "DESIRED_CUDA=${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}"
121:             echo "GPU_ARCH_VERSION=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}"
122:             echo "GPU_ARCH_TYPE=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}"
123:             echo "DOCKER_IMAGE=${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}"
124:             echo "SKIP_ALL_TESTS=${{ env.SKIP_ALL_TESTS }}"
125:             echo "LIBTORCH_CONFIG=${{ env.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}"
126:             echo "LIBTORCH_VARIANT=${{ env.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}"
127:             echo "DESIRED_PYTHON=${{ env.DESIRED_PYTHON }}"
128:             echo "PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS=${{ env.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}"
129:             echo "ALPINE_IMAGE=${{ env.ALPINE_IMAGE }}"
130:             echo "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${{ env.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION }}"
131:             echo "BINARY_ENV_FILE=${{ env.BINARY_ENV_FILE }}"
132:             echo "BUILD_ENVIRONMENT=${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}"
133:             echo "BUILD_NAME=${{ env.BUILD_NAME }}"
134:             echo "PR_NUMBER=${{ env.PR_NUMBER }}"
135:             echo "PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}"
136:             echo "SHA1=${{ env.SHA1 }}"
137:           } >> "${GITHUB_ENV} }}"
138: 
139:       - name: List the env
140:         shell: bash
141:         run: env
142: 
143:       - name: "[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)"
144:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
145:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10
146:         continue-on-error: true
147:         with:
148:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
149: 
150:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
151:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10
152:         with:
153:           no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
154: 
155:       - name: Setup Linux
156:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
157:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
158: 
159:       - name: Chown workspace
160:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
161:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace
162:         with:
163:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
164: 
165:       - name: Clean workspace
166:         shell: bash
167:         run: |
168:           set -eux
169: 
170:           rm -rf "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
171:           mkdir "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
172: 
173:           if [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' ]] || [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' ]] ; then
174:             rm -rf "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts"
175:             mkdir "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts"
176:           fi
177: 
178:       - name: Checkout PyTorch to pytorch dir
179:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
180:         with:
181:           submodules: recursive
182:           path: pytorch
183:           show-progress: false
184: 
185:       - name: Clean PyTorch checkout
186:         run: |
187:           # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts
188:           git clean -fxd
189:         working-directory: pytorch
190: 
191:       - name: Check if the job is disabled
192:         id: filter
193:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs
194:         with:
195:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
196:           # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the
197:           # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled
198:           test-matrix: |
199:             { include: [
200:               { config: "default" },
201:             ]}
202: 
203:       - name: configure aws credentials
204:         id: aws_creds
205:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}
206:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
207:         with:
208:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only
209:           aws-region: us-east-1
210:           role-duration-seconds: 18000
211: 
212:       - name: Calculate docker image
213:         id: calculate-docker-image
214:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
215:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10
216:         with:
217:           # If doing this in release/2.10 or release branch, use docker.io. Otherwise
218:           # use ECR
219:           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}
220:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
221:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}
222:           # The build.sh script in this folder is not actually the correct one,
223:           # this is just needed for sha calculation
224:           docker-build-dir: .ci/docker
225:           working-directory: pytorch
226: 
227:       - name: Pull Docker image
228:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
229:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10
230:         with:
231:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
232: 
233:       - name: Build PyTorch binary
234:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
235:         env:
236:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image || format('{0}:{1}', inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE, inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX) }}
237:         run: |
238:           set -x
239:           mkdir -p artifacts/
240:           container_name=$(docker run \
241:             -e BINARY_ENV_FILE \
242:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
243:             -e DESIRED_CUDA \
244:             -e DESIRED_PYTHON \
245:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
246:             -e GPU_ARCH_TYPE \
247:             -e GPU_ARCH_VERSION \
248:             -e LIBTORCH_VARIANT \
249:             -e PACKAGE_TYPE \
250:             -e PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR \
251:             -e PYTORCH_ROOT \
252:             -e SKIP_ALL_TESTS \
253:             -e PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS \
254:             --tty \
255:             --detach \
256:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/pytorch:/pytorch" \
257:             -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/artifacts" \
258:             -w / \
259:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
260:           )
261:           docker exec -t -w "${PYTORCH_ROOT}" "${container_name}" bash -c "bash .circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh"
262:           # Unified build script for all architectures (x86_64, aarch64, s390x)
263:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" bash -c "source ${BINARY_ENV_FILE} && bash /pytorch/.ci/${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}/build.sh"
264: 
265:       - name: Chown artifacts
266:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
267:         shell: bash
268:         run: |
269:           # Ensure the working directory gets chowned back to the current user
270:           docker run --rm -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/v" -w /v "${ALPINE_IMAGE}" chown -R "$(id -u):$(id -g)" .
271: 
272:       - uses: actions/upload-artifact@50769540e7f4bd5e21e526ee35c689e35e0d6874 # v4.4.0

274:         with:

276:           if-no-files-found: error

279: 
280:       - name: Teardown Linux
281:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
282:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10
283: 
284:       - name: Chown workspace
285:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
286:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/chown-workspace
287:         with:
288:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
289: 
290:       - name: Cleanup docker
291:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
292:         shell: bash
293:         run: |
294:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop
295:           # ignore expansion of "docker ps -q" since it could be empty
296:           # shellcheck disable=SC2046
297:           docker stop $(docker ps -q) || true

{"content":"1: #!/bin/bash
2: 
3: # Common util **functions** that can be sourced in other scripts.
4: 
5: # note: printf is used instead of echo to avoid backslash
6: # processing and to properly handle values that begin with a '-'.
7: 
8: log() { printf '%s\n' "$*"; }
9: error() { log "ERROR: $*" >&2; }
10: fatal() { error "$@"; exit 1; }
11: 
12: retry () {
13:     "$@" || (sleep 10 && "$@") || (sleep 20 && "$@") || (sleep 40 && "$@")
14: }
15: 
16: # compositional trap taken from https://stackoverflow.com/a/7287873/23845
17: # appends a command to a trap
18: #
19: # - 1st arg:  code to add
20: # - remaining args:  names of traps to modify
21: #
22: trap_add() {
23:     trap_add_cmd=$1; shift || fatal "${FUNCNAME[0]} usage error"
24:     for trap_add_name in "$@"; do
25:         trap -- "$(
26:             # helper fn to get existing trap command from output
27:             # of trap -p
28:             extract_trap_cmd() { printf '%s\n' "$3"; }
29:             # print existing trap command with newline
30:             eval "extract_trap_cmd $(trap -p "${trap_add_name}")"
31:             # print the new trap command
32:             printf '%s\n' "${trap_add_cmd}"
33:         )" "${trap_add_name}" \
34:             || fatal "unable to add to trap ${trap_add_name}"
35:     done
36: }
37: # set the trace attribute for the above function.  this is
38: # required to modify DEBUG or RETURN traps because functions don't
39: # inherit them unless the trace attribute is set
40: declare -f -t trap_add
41: 
42: function assert_git_not_dirty() {
43:     # TODO: we should add an option to `build_amd.py` that reverts the repo to
44:     #       an unmodified state.
45:     if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" != *rocm* ]] && [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" != *xla* ]] ; then
46:         git_status=$(git status --porcelain | grep -v '?? third_party' || true)
47:         if [[ $git_status ]]; then
48:             echo "Build left local git repository checkout dirty"
49:             echo "git status --porcelain:"
50:             echo "${git_status}"
51:             exit 1
52:         fi
53:     fi
54: }
55: 
56: function pip_install_whl() {
57:   # This is used to install PyTorch and other build artifacts wheel locally
58:   # without using any network connection
59: 
60:   # Convert the input arguments into an array
61:   local args=("$@")
62: 
63:   # Check if the first argument contains multiple paths separated by spaces
64:   if [[ "${args[0]}" == *" "* ]]; then
65:     # Split the string by spaces into an array
66:     IFS=' ' read -r -a paths <<< "${args[0]}"
67:     # Loop through each path and install individually
68:     for path in "${paths[@]}"; do
69:       echo "Installing $path"
70:       python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps "$path"
71:     done
72:   else
73:     # Loop through each argument and install individually
74:     for path in "${args[@]}"; do
75:       echo "Installing $path"
76:       python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps "$path"
77:     done
78:   fi
79: }
80: 
81: function pip_build_and_install() {
82:   local build_target=$1
83:   local wheel_dir=$2
84: 
85:   local found_whl=0
86:   for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
87:   do
88:     if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
89:       found_whl=1
90:       break
91:     fi
92:   done
93: 
94:   # Build the wheel if it doesn't exist
95:   if [ "${found_whl}" == "0" ]; then
96:     python3 -m pip wheel \
97:       --no-build-isolation \
98:       --no-deps \
99:       -w "${wheel_dir}" \
100:       "${build_target}"
101:   fi
102: 
103:   for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
104:   do
105:     pip_install_whl "${file}"
106:   done
107: }
108: 
109: function pip_install() {
110:   # retry 3 times
111:   pip_install_pkg="python3 -m pip install --progress-bar off"
112:   ${pip_install_pkg} "$@" || \
113:     ${pip_install_pkg} "$@" || \
114:     ${pip_install_pkg} "$@"
115: }
116: 
117: function pip_uninstall() {
118:   # uninstall 2 times
119:   pip3 uninstall -y "$@" || pip3 uninstall -y "$@"
120: }
121: 
122: function get_exit_code() {
123:   set +e
124:   "$@"
125:   retcode=$?
126:   set -e
127:   return $retcode
128: }
129: 
130: function get_bazel() {
131:   # Download and use the cross-platform, dependency-free Python
132:   # version of Bazelisk to fetch the platform specific version of
133:   # Bazel to use from .bazelversion.
134:   retry curl --location --output tools/bazel \
135:     https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bazelbuild/bazelisk/v1.23.0/bazelisk.py
136:   shasum --algorithm=1 --check \
137:     <(echo '01df9cf7f08dd80d83979ed0d0666a99349ae93c  tools/bazel')
138:   chmod u+x tools/bazel
139: }
140: 
141: function install_monkeytype {
142:   # Install MonkeyType
143:   pip_install MonkeyType
144: }
145: 
146: 
147: function get_pinned_commit() {
148:   cat .github/ci_commit_pins/"${1}".txt
149: }
150: 
151: function detect_cuda_arch() {
152:   if [[ "${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}" == *cuda* ]]; then
153:     if command -v nvidia-smi; then
154:       TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=compute_cap --format=csv | tail -n 1)
155:     elif [[ "${TEST_CONFIG}" == *nogpu* ]]; then
156:       # There won't be nvidia-smi in nogpu tests, so just set TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST to the default
157:       # minimum supported value here
158:       TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=8.0
159:     fi
160:     export TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST
161:   fi
162: }
163: 
164: function install_torchaudio() {
165:   local commit
166:   commit=$(get_pinned_commit audio)
167:   pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/audio.git@${commit}" dist/audio
168: }
169: 
170: function install_torchtext() {
171:   local data_commit
172:   local text_commit
173:   data_commit=$(get_pinned_commit data)
174:   text_commit=$(get_pinned_commit text)
175:   pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/data.git@${data_commit}" dist/data
176:   pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/text.git@${text_commit}" dist/text
177: }
178: 
179: function install_torchvision() {
180:   local orig_preload
181:   local commit
182:   commit=$(get_pinned_commit vision)
183:   orig_preload=${LD_PRELOAD}
184:   if [ -n "${LD_PRELOAD}" ]; then
185:     # Silence dlerror to work-around glibc ASAN bug, see https://sourceware.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=27653#c9
186:     echo 'char* dlerror(void) { return "";}'|gcc -fpic -shared -o "${HOME}/dlerror.so" -x c -
187:     LD_PRELOAD=${orig_preload}:${HOME}/dlerror.so
188:   fi
189: 
190:   if [[ "${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}" == *cuda* ]]; then
191:     # Not sure if both are needed, but why not
192:     export FORCE_CUDA=1
193:     export WITH_CUDA=1
194:   fi
195:   pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/vision.git@${commit}" dist/vision
196: 
197:   if [ -n "${LD_PRELOAD}" ]; then
198:     LD_PRELOAD=${orig_preload}
199:   fi
200: }
201: 
202: function install_torchrec_and_fbgemm() {
203:   local torchrec_commit
204:   torchrec_commit=$(get_pinned_commit torchrec)
205:   local fbgemm_commit
206:   fbgemm_commit=$(get_pinned_commit fbgemm)
207:   if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" == *rocm* ]] ; then
208:     fbgemm_commit=$(get_pinned_commit fbgemm_rocm)
209:   fi
210:   pip_uninstall torchrec-nightly
211:   pip_uninstall fbgemm-gpu-nightly
212:   pip_install setuptools-git-versioning scikit-build pyre-extensions
213: 
214:   if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" == *rocm* ]] ; then
215:     # install torchrec first because it installs fbgemm nightly on top of rocm fbgemm
216:     pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/torchrec.git@${torchrec_commit}" dist/torchrec
217:     pip_uninstall fbgemm-gpu-nightly
218: 
219:     # Set ROCM_HOME isn't available, use ROCM_PATH if set or /opt/rocm
220:     ROCM_HOME="${ROCM_HOME:-${ROCM_PATH:-/opt/rocm}}"
221: 
222:     # Find rocm_version.h header file for ROCm version extract
223:     rocm_version_h="${ROCM_HOME}/include/rocm-core/rocm_version.h"
224:     if [ ! -f "$rocm_version_h" ]; then
225:         rocm_version_h="${ROCM_HOME}/include/rocm_version.h"
226:     fi
227: 
228:     # Error out if rocm_version.h not found
229:     if [ ! -f "$rocm_version_h" ]; then
230:         echo "Error: rocm_version.h not found in expected locations." >&2
231:         exit 1
232:     fi
233: 
234:     # Extract major, minor and patch ROCm version numbers
235:     MAJOR_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_MAJOR' "$rocm_version_h" | awk '{print $3}')
236:     MINOR_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_MINOR' "$rocm_version_h" | awk '{print $3}')
237:     PATCH_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_PATCH' "$rocm_version_h" | awk '{print $3}')
238:     ROCM_INT=$((MAJOR_VERSION * 10000 + MINOR_VERSION * 100 + PATCH_VERSION))
239:     echo "ROCm version: $ROCM_INT"
240:     export BUILD_ROCM_VERSION="$MAJOR_VERSION.$MINOR_VERSION"
241: 
242:     pip_install tabulate  # needed for newer fbgemm
243:     pip_install patchelf  # needed for rocm fbgemm
244: 
245:     local wheel_dir=dist/fbgemm_gpu
246:     local found_whl=0
247:     for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
248:     do
249:       if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
250:         found_whl=1
251:         break
252:       fi
253:     done
254: 
255:     # Build the wheel if it doesn't exist
256:     if [ "${found_whl}" == "0" ]; then
257:       git clone --recursive https://github.com/pytorch/fbgemm
258:       pushd fbgemm/fbgemm_gpu
259:       git checkout "${fbgemm_commit}" --recurse-submodules
260:       # until the fbgemm_commit includes the tbb patch
261:       patch <<'EOF'
262: --- a/FbgemmGpu.cmake
263: +++ b/FbgemmGpu.cmake
264: @@ -184,5 +184,6 @@ gpu_cpp_library(
265:      fbgemm_gpu_tbe_cache
266:      fbgemm_gpu_tbe_optimizers
267:      fbgemm_gpu_tbe_utils
268: +    tbb
269:    DESTINATION
270:      fbgemm_gpu)
271: EOF
272:       python setup.py bdist_wheel --build-variant=rocm
273:       popd
274: 
275:       # Save the wheel before cleaning up
276:       mkdir -p dist/fbgemm_gpu
277:       cp fbgemm/fbgemm_gpu/dist/*.whl dist/fbgemm_gpu
278:     fi
279: 
280:     for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
281:     do
282:       pip_install_whl "${file}"
283:     done
284: 
285:     rm -rf fbgemm
286:   else
287:     pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/torchrec.git@${torchrec_commit}" dist/torchrec
288:     # Skip fbgemm for CUDA 13 as it's not compatible yet
289:     if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" != *cuda13* ]]; then
290:       pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/FBGEMM.git@${fbgemm_commit}#subdirectory=fbgemm_gpu" dist/fbgemm_gpu
291:     fi
292:   fi
293: }
294: 
295: function clone_pytorch_xla() {
296:   if [[ ! -d ./xla ]]; then
297:     git clone --recursive --quiet https://github.com/pytorch/xla.git
298:     pushd xla
299:     # pin the xla hash so that we don't get broken by changes to xla
300:     git checkout "$(cat ../.github/ci_commit_pins/xla.txt)"
301:     git submodule sync
302:     git submodule update --init --recursive
303:     popd
304:   fi
305: }
306: 
307: function install_torchao() {
308:   local commit
309:   commit=$(get_pinned_commit torchao)
310:   pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/ao.git@${commit}" dist/ao
311: }
312: 
313: function install_flash_attn_cute() {
314:   echo "Installing FlashAttention CuTe from GitHub..."
315:   # Grab latest main til we have a pinned commit
316:   local flash_attn_commit
317:   flash_attn_commit=$(git ls-remote https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention.git HEAD | cut -f1)
318: 
319:   # Clone the repo to a temporary directory
320:   rm -rf flash-attention-build
321:   git clone --depth 1 --recursive https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention.git flash-attention-build
322: 
323:   pushd flash-attention-build
324:   git checkout "${flash_attn_commit}"
325: 
326:   # Install only the 'cute' sub-directory
327:   pip_install -e flash_attn/cute/
328:   popd
329: 
330:   # remove the local repo
331:   rm -rf flash-attention-build
332:   echo "FlashAttention CuTe installation complete."
333: }
334: 
335: function print_sccache_stats() {
336:   echo 'PyTorch Build Statistics'
337:   sccache --show-stats
338: 
339:   if [[ -n "${OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID}" ]]; then
340:     sccache --show-stats --stats-format json | jq .stats \
341:       > "sccache-stats-${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}-${OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID}.json"
342:   else
343:     echo "env var OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID not set, will not write sccache stats to json"
344:   fi
345: }

{"content":"1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: # ruff: noqa: LOG015
3: 
4: import json
5: import logging
6: import os
7: import re
8: import subprocess
9: import sys
10: import warnings
11: from collections.abc import Callable
12: from enum import Enum
13: from functools import cache
14: from logging import info
15: from typing import Any, Optional
16: from urllib.request import Request, urlopen
17: 
18: import yaml
19: 
20: 
21: REENABLE_TEST_REGEX = "(?i)(Close(d|s)?|Resolve(d|s)?|Fix(ed|es)?) (#|https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/)([0-9]+)"
22: MAIN_BRANCH = "main"
23: 
24: PREFIX = "test-config/"
25: 
26: logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
27: 
28: 
29: def is_cuda_or_rocm_job(job_name: Optional[str]) -> bool:
30:     if not job_name:
31:         return False
32: 
33:     return "cuda" in job_name or "rocm" in job_name
34: 
35: 
36: # Supported modes when running periodically. Only applying the mode when
37: # its lambda condition returns true
38: SUPPORTED_PERIODICAL_MODES: dict[str, Callable[[Optional[str]], bool]] = {
39:     # Memory leak check is only needed for CUDA and ROCm jobs which utilize GPU memory
40:     "mem_leak_check": is_cuda_or_rocm_job,
41:     "rerun_disabled_tests": lambda job_name: True,
42: }
43: 
44: # The link to the published list of disabled jobs
45: DISABLED_JOBS_URL = "https://ossci-metrics.s3.amazonaws.com/disabled-jobs.json"
46: # and unstable jobs
47: UNSTABLE_JOBS_URL = "https://ossci-metrics.s3.amazonaws.com/unstable-jobs.json"
48: 
49: # Some constants used to handle disabled and unstable jobs
50: JOB_NAME_SEP = "/"
51: BUILD_JOB_NAME = "build"
52: TEST_JOB_NAME = "test"
53: BUILD_AND_TEST_JOB_NAME = "build-and-test"
54: JOB_NAME_CFG_REGEX = re.compile(r"(?P<job>[\w-]+)\s+\((?P<cfg>[\w-]+)\)")
55: EXCLUDED_BRANCHES = ["nightly"]
56: MEM_LEAK_LABEL = "enable-mem-leak-check"
57: 
58: 
59: class IssueType(Enum):
60:     DISABLED = "disabled"
61:     UNSTABLE = "unstable"
62: 
63: 
64: def parse_args() -> Any:
65:     from argparse import ArgumentParser
66: 
67:     parser = ArgumentParser(
68:         "Filter all test configurations and keep only requested ones"
69:     )
70:     parser.add_argument(
71:         "--test-matrix", type=str, required=True, help="the original test matrix"
72:     )
73:     parser.add_argument(
74:         "--selected-test-configs",
75:         type=str,
76:         default="",
77:         help="a comma-separated list of test configurations from the test matrix to keep",
78:     )
79:     parser.add_argument(
80:         "--workflow", type=str, help="the name of the current workflow, i.e. pull"
81:     )
82:     parser.add_argument(
83:         "--job-name",
84:         type=str,
85:         help="the name of the current job, i.e. linux-jammy-py3.8-gcc7 / build",
86:     )
87:     parser.add_argument("--pr-number", type=str, help="the pull request number")
88:     parser.add_argument("--tag", type=str, help="the associated tag if it exists")
89:     parser.add_argument(
90:         "--event-name",
91:         type=str,
92:         help="name of the event that triggered the job (pull, schedule, etc)",
93:     )
94:     parser.add_argument(
95:         "--schedule",
96:         type=str,
97:         help="cron schedule that triggered the job",
98:     )
99:     parser.add_argument(
100:         "--branch",
101:         type=str,
102:         default=MAIN_BRANCH,
103:         help="the branch name",
104:     )
105:     return parser.parse_args()
106: 
107: 
108: @cache
109: def get_pr_info(pr_number: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
110:     """
111:     Dynamically get PR information
112:     """
113:     # From https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/environment-variables
114:     pytorch_repo = os.environ.get("GITHUB_REPOSITORY", "pytorch/pytorch")
115:     pytorch_github_api = f"https://api.github.com/repos/{pytorch_repo}"
116:     github_token = os.environ["GITHUB_TOKEN"]
117: 
118:     headers = {
119:         "Accept": "application/vnd.github.v3+json",
120:         "Authorization": f"token {github_token}",
121:     }
122:     json_response: dict[str, Any] = download_json(
123:         url=f"{pytorch_github_api}/issues/{pr_number}",
124:         headers=headers,
125:     )
126: 
127:     if not json_response:
128:         warnings.warn(f"Failed to get the labels for #{pr_number}")
129:         return {}
130: 
131:     return json_response
132: 
133: 
134: def get_labels(pr_number: int) -> set[str]:
135:     """
136:     Dynamically get the latest list of labels from the pull request
137:     """
138:     pr_info = get_pr_info(pr_number)
139:     return {
140:         label.get("name") for label in pr_info.get("labels", []) if label.get("name")
141:     }
142: 
143: 
144: def filter_labels(labels: set[str], label_regex: Any) -> set[str]:
145:     """
146:     Return the list of matching labels
147:     """
148:     return {l for l in labels if re.match(label_regex, l)}
149: 
150: 
151: def filter(test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]], labels: set[str]) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:
152:     """
153:     Select the list of test config to run from the test matrix. The logic works
154:     as follows:
155: 
156:     If the PR has one or more test-config labels as specified, only these test configs
157:     will be selected.  This also works with ciflow labels, for example, if a PR has both
158:     ciflow/trunk and test-config/functorch, only trunk functorch builds and tests will
159:     be run.
160: 
161:     If the PR has none of the test-config label, all tests are run as usual.
162:     """
163:     filtered_test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]] = {"include": []}
164: 
165:     for entry in test_matrix.get("include", []):
166:         config_name = entry.get("config", "")
167:         if not config_name:
168:             continue
169: 
170:         label = f"{PREFIX}{config_name.strip()}"
171:         if label in labels:
172:             msg = f"Select {config_name} because label {label} is present in the pull request by the time the test starts"
173:             info(msg)
174:             filtered_test_matrix["include"].append(entry)
175: 
176:     test_config_labels = filter_labels(labels, re.compile(f"{PREFIX}.+"))
177:     if not filtered_test_matrix["include"] and not test_config_labels:
178:         info("Found no test-config label on the PR, so all test configs are included")
179:         # Found no test-config label and the filtered test matrix is empty, return the same
180:         # test matrix as before so that all tests can be run normally
181:         return test_matrix
182:     else:
183:         msg = f"Found {test_config_labels} on the PR so only these test configs are run"
184:         info(msg)
185:         # When the filter test matrix contain matches or if a valid test config label
186:         # is found in the PR, return the filtered test matrix
187:         return filtered_test_matrix
188: 
189: 
190: def filter_selected_test_configs(
191:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]], selected_test_configs: set[str]
192: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:
193:     """
194:     Keep only the selected configs if the list if not empty. Otherwise, keep all test configs.
195:     This filter is used when the workflow is dispatched manually.
196:     """
197:     if not selected_test_configs:
198:         return test_matrix
199: 
200:     filtered_test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]] = {"include": []}
201:     for entry in test_matrix.get("include", []):
202:         config_name = entry.get("config", "")
203:         if not config_name:
204:             continue
205: 
206:         if config_name in selected_test_configs:
207:             filtered_test_matrix["include"].append(entry)
208: 
209:     return filtered_test_matrix
210: 
211: 
212: def set_periodic_modes(
213:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]], job_name: Optional[str]
214: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:
215:     """
216:     Apply all periodic modes when running under a schedule
217:     """
218:     scheduled_test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]] = {
219:         "include": [],
220:     }
221: 
222:     for config in test_matrix.get("include", []):
223:         for mode, cond in SUPPORTED_PERIODICAL_MODES.items():
224:             if not cond(job_name):
225:                 continue
226: 
227:             cfg = config.copy()
228:             cfg[mode] = mode
229:             scheduled_test_matrix["include"].append(cfg)
230: 
231:     return scheduled_test_matrix
232: 
233: 
234: def mark_unstable_jobs(
235:     workflow: str, job_name: str, test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]]
236: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:
237:     """
238:     Check the list of unstable jobs and mark them accordingly. Note that if a job
239:     is unstable, all its dependents will also be marked accordingly
240:     """
241:     return process_jobs(
242:         workflow=workflow,
243:         job_name=job_name,
244:         test_matrix=test_matrix,
245:         issue_type=IssueType.UNSTABLE,
246:         url=UNSTABLE_JOBS_URL,
247:     )
248: 
249: 
250: def remove_disabled_jobs(
251:     workflow: str, job_name: str, test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]]
252: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:
253:     """
254:     Check the list of disabled jobs, remove the current job and all its dependents
255:     if it exists in the list
256:     """
257:     return process_jobs(
258:         workflow=workflow,
259:         job_name=job_name,
260:         test_matrix=test_matrix,
261:         issue_type=IssueType.DISABLED,
262:         url=DISABLED_JOBS_URL,
263:     )
264: 
265: 
266: def _filter_jobs(
267:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]],
268:     issue_type: IssueType,
269:     target_cfg: Optional[str] = None,
270: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:
271:     """
272:     An utility function used to actually apply the job filter
273:     """
274:     # The result will be stored here
275:     filtered_test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]] = {"include": []}
276: 
277:     # This is an issue to disable a CI job
278:     if issue_type == IssueType.DISABLED:
279:         # If there is a target config, disable (remove) only that
280:         if target_cfg:
281:             # Remove the target config from the test matrix
282:             filtered_test_matrix["include"] = [
283:                 r for r in test_matrix["include"] if r.get("config", "") != target_cfg
284:             ]
285: 
286:         return filtered_test_matrix
287: 
288:     if issue_type == IssueType.UNSTABLE:
289:         for r in test_matrix["include"]:
290:             cpy = r.copy()
291: 
292:             if (target_cfg and r.get("config", "") == target_cfg) or not target_cfg:
293:                 # If there is a target config, only mark that as unstable, otherwise,
294:                 # mark everything as unstable
295:                 cpy[IssueType.UNSTABLE.value] = IssueType.UNSTABLE.value
296: 
297:             filtered_test_matrix["include"].append(cpy)
298: 
299:         return filtered_test_matrix
300: 
301:     # No matching issue, return everything
302:     return test_matrix
303: 
304: 
305: def process_jobs(
306:     workflow: str,
307:     job_name: str,
308:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]],
309:     issue_type: IssueType,
310:     url: str,
311: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:
312:     """
313:     Both disabled and unstable jobs are in the following format:
314: 
315:     {
316:         "WORKFLOW / PLATFORM / JOB (CONFIG)": [
317:             AUTHOR,
318:             ISSUE_NUMBER,
319:             ISSUE_URL,
320:             WORKFLOW,
321:             PLATFORM,
322:             JOB (CONFIG),
323:         ],
324:         "pull / linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9 / test (dynamo)": [
325:             "pytorchbot",
326:             "94861",
327:             "https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/94861",
328:             "pull",
329:             "linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9",
330:             "test (dynamo)",
331:         ],
332:     }
333:     """
334:     try:
335:         # The job name from github is in the PLATFORM / JOB (CONFIG) format, so breaking
336:         # it into its two components first
337:         current_platform, _ = (n.strip() for n in job_name.split(JOB_NAME_SEP, 1) if n)
338:     except ValueError:
339:         warnings.warn(f"Invalid job name {job_name}, returning")
340:         return test_matrix
341: 
342:     for record in download_json(url=url, headers={}).values():
343:         (
344:             author,
345:             _,
346:             target_url,
347:             target_workflow,
348:             target_platform,
349:             target_job_cfg,
350:         ) = record
351: 
352:         if target_workflow != workflow:
353:             # The current workflow doesn't match this record
354:             continue
355: 
356:         cleanup_regex = rf"(-{BUILD_JOB_NAME}|-{TEST_JOB_NAME})$"
357:         # There is an exception here for binary build workflows in which the platform
358:         # names have the build and test suffix. For example, we have a build job called
359:         # manywheel-py3-cuda11_8-build / build and its subsequent test job called
360:         # manywheel-py3-cuda11_8-test / test. So they are linked, but their suffixes
361:         # are different
362:         target_platform_no_suffix = re.sub(cleanup_regex, "", target_platform)
363:         current_platform_no_suffix = re.sub(cleanup_regex, "", current_platform)
364: 
365:         if (
366:             target_platform != current_platform
367:             and target_platform_no_suffix != current_platform_no_suffix
368:         ):
369:             # The current platform doesn't match this record
370:             continue
371: 
372:         # The logic after this is fairly complicated:
373:         #
374:         # - If the target record doesn't have the optional job (config) name,
375:         #   i.e. pull / linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9, all build and test jobs will
376:         #   be skipped if it's a disabled record or marked as unstable if it's
377:         #   an unstable record
378:         #
379:         # - If the target record has the job name and it's a build job, i.e.
380:         #   pull / linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9 / build, all build and test jobs
381:         #   will be skipped if it's a disabled record or marked as unstable if
382:         #   it's an unstable record, because the latter requires the former
383:         #
384:         # - If the target record has the job name and it's a test job without
385:         #   the config part, i.e. pull / linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9 / test, all
386:         #   test jobs will be skipped if it's a disabled record or marked as
387:         #   unstable if it's an unstable record
388:         #
389:         # - If the target record has the job (config) name, only that test config
390:         #   will be skipped or marked as unstable
391:         if not target_job_cfg:
392:             msg = (
393:                 f"Issue {target_url} created by {author} has {issue_type.value} "
394:                 + f"all CI jobs for {workflow} / {job_name}"
395:             )
396:             info(msg)
397:             return _filter_jobs(
398:                 test_matrix=test_matrix,
399:                 issue_type=issue_type,
400:             )
401: 
402:         if target_job_cfg == BUILD_JOB_NAME:
403:             msg = (
404:                 f"Issue {target_url} created by {author} has {issue_type.value} "
405:                 + f"the build job for {workflow} / {job_name}"
406:             )
407:             info(msg)
408:             return _filter_jobs(
409:                 test_matrix=test_matrix,
410:                 issue_type=issue_type,
411:             )
412: 
413:         if target_job_cfg in (TEST_JOB_NAME, BUILD_AND_TEST_JOB_NAME):
414:             msg = (
415:                 f"Issue {target_url} created by {author} has {issue_type.value} "
416:                 + f"all the test jobs for {workflow} / {job_name}"
417:             )
418:             info(msg)
419:             return _filter_jobs(
420:                 test_matrix=test_matrix,
421:                 issue_type=issue_type,
422:             )
423: 
424:         m = JOB_NAME_CFG_REGEX.match(target_job_cfg)
425:         if m:
426:             target_job = m.group("job")
427:             # Make sure that the job name is a valid test job name first before checking the config
428:             if target_job in (TEST_JOB_NAME, BUILD_AND_TEST_JOB_NAME):
429:                 target_cfg = m.group("cfg")
430: 
431:                 # NB: There can be multiple unstable configurations, i.e. inductor, inductor_huggingface
432:                 test_matrix = _filter_jobs(
433:                     test_matrix=test_matrix,
434:                     issue_type=issue_type,
435:                     target_cfg=target_cfg,
436:                 )
437:         else:
438:             warnings.warn(
439:                 f"Found a matching {issue_type.value} issue {target_url} for {workflow} / {job_name}, "
440:                 + f"but the name {target_job_cfg} is invalid"
441:             )
442: 
443:     # Found no matching target, return the same input test matrix
444:     return test_matrix
445: 
446: 
447: def download_json(url: str, headers: dict[str, str], num_retries: int = 3) -> Any:
448:     for _ in range(num_retries):
449:         try:
450:             req = Request(url=url, headers=headers)
451:             content = urlopen(req, timeout=5).read().decode("utf-8")
452:             return json.loads(content)
453:         except Exception as e:
454:             warnings.warn(f"Could not download {url}: {e}")
455: 
456:     warnings.warn(f"All {num_retries} retries exhausted, downloading {url} failed")
457:     return {}
458: 
459: 
460: def set_output(name: str, val: Any) -> None:
461:     print(f"Setting output {name}={val}")
462:     if os.getenv("GITHUB_OUTPUT"):
463:         with open(str(os.getenv("GITHUB_OUTPUT")), "a") as env:
464:             print(f"{name}={val}", file=env)
465:     else:
466:         print(f"::set-output name={name}::{val}")
467: 
468: 
469: def parse_reenabled_issues(s: Optional[str]) -> list[str]:
470:     # NB: When the PR body is empty, GitHub API returns a None value, which is
471:     # passed into this function
472:     if not s:
473:         return []
474: 
475:     # The regex is meant to match all *case-insensitive* keywords that
476:     # GitHub has delineated would link PRs to issues, more details here:
477:     # https://docs.github.com/en/issues/tracking-your-work-with-issues/linking-a-pull-request-to-an-issue.
478:     # E.g., "Close #62851", "fixES #62851" and "RESOLVED #62851" would all match, but not
479:     # "closes  #62851" --> extra space, "fixing #62851" --> not a keyword, nor "fix 62851" --> no #
480:     issue_numbers = [x[5] for x in re.findall(REENABLE_TEST_REGEX, s)]
481:     return issue_numbers
482: 
483: 
484: def get_reenabled_issues(pr_body: str = "") -> list[str]:
485:     default_branch = f"origin/{os.environ.get('GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH', 'main')}"
486:     try:
487:         commit_messages = subprocess.check_output(
488:             f"git cherry -v {default_branch}".split(" ")
489:         ).decode("utf-8")
490:     except Exception as e:
491:         warnings.warn(f"failed to get commit messages: {e}")
492:         commit_messages = ""
493:     return parse_reenabled_issues(pr_body) + parse_reenabled_issues(commit_messages)
494: 
495: 
496: def check_for_setting(labels: set[str], body: str, setting: str) -> bool:
497:     return setting in labels or f"[{setting}]" in body
498: 
499: 
500: def perform_misc_tasks(
501:     labels: set[str],
502:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]],
503:     job_name: str,
504:     pr_body: str,
505:     branch: Optional[str] = None,
506:     tag: Optional[str] = None,
507: ) -> None:
508:     """
509:     In addition to apply the filter logic, the script also does the following
510:     misc tasks to set keep-going and is-unstable variables
511:     """
512:     set_output(
513:         "keep-going",
514:         branch == MAIN_BRANCH
515:         or bool(tag and re.match(r"^trunk/[a-f0-9]{40}$", tag))
516:         # Pattern for tags created via manual run on HUD
517:         or bool(tag and re.match(r"^ciflow/[^/]+/[a-f0-9]{40}$", tag))
518:         or check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, "keep-going"),
519:     )
520:     set_output(
521:         "ci-verbose-test-logs",
522:         check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, "ci-verbose-test-logs"),
523:     )
524:     set_output(
525:         "ci-test-showlocals", check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, "ci-test-showlocals")
526:     )
527:     set_output(
528:         "ci-no-test-timeout", check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, "ci-no-test-timeout")
529:     )
530:     set_output("ci-no-td", check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, "ci-no-td"))
531:     # Only relevant for the one linux distributed cuda job, delete this when TD
532:     # is rolled out completely
533:     set_output(
534:         "ci-td-distributed", check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, "ci-td-distributed")
535:     )
536: 
537:     # Obviously, if the job name includes unstable, then this is an unstable job
538:     is_unstable = job_name and IssueType.UNSTABLE.value in job_name
539:     if not is_unstable and test_matrix and test_matrix.get("include"):
540:         # Even when the job name doesn't mention unstable, we will also mark it as
541:         # unstable when the test matrix only includes unstable jobs. Basically, this
542:         # logic allows build or build-and-test jobs to be marked as unstable too.
543:         #
544:         # Basically, when a build job is unstable, all the subsequent test jobs are
545:         # also unstable. And when all test jobs are unstable, we will also treat the
546:         # build job as unstable. It's simpler this way
547:         is_unstable = all(IssueType.UNSTABLE.value in r for r in test_matrix["include"])
548: 
549:     set_output(
550:         "is-unstable",
551:         is_unstable,
552:     )
553: 
554:     set_output("reenabled-issues", ",".join(get_reenabled_issues(pr_body=pr_body)))
555: 
556:     if MEM_LEAK_LABEL in labels:
557:         # Enable mem leak check if label is added
558:         for config in test_matrix.get("include", []):
559:             if is_cuda_or_rocm_job(job_name):
560:                 config["mem_leak_check"] = "mem_leak_check"
561: 
562: 
563: def main() -> None:
564:     args = parse_args()
565:     # Load the original test matrix set by the workflow. Its format, however,
566:     # doesn't follow the strict JSON format, so we load it using yaml here for
567:     # its more relaxed syntax
568:     test_matrix = yaml.safe_load(args.test_matrix)
569: 
570:     if test_matrix is None:
571:         warnings.warn(f"Invalid test matrix input '{args.test_matrix}', exiting")
572:         # We handle invalid test matrix gracefully by marking it as empty
573:         set_output("is-test-matrix-empty", True)
574:         sys.exit(0)
575: 
576:     pr_number = args.pr_number
577:     tag = args.tag
578: 
579:     # If the tag matches, we can get the PR number from it, this is from ciflow
580:     # workflow dispatcher
581:     tag_regex = re.compile(r"^ciflow/[\w\-]+/(?P<pr_number>\d+)$")
582: 
583:     labels = set()
584:     if pr_number:
585:         # If a PR number is set, query all the labels from that PR
586:         labels = get_labels(int(pr_number))
587:         # Then filter the test matrix and keep only the selected ones
588:         filtered_test_matrix = filter(test_matrix, labels)
589: 
590:     elif tag:
591:         m = tag_regex.match(tag)
592: 
593:         if m:
594:             pr_number = m.group("pr_number")
595: 
596:             # The PR number can also come from the tag in ciflow tag event
597:             labels = get_labels(int(pr_number))
598:             # Filter the test matrix and keep only the selected ones
599:             filtered_test_matrix = filter(test_matrix, labels)
600: 
601:         else:
602:             # There is a tag but it isn't ciflow, so there is nothing left to do
603:             filtered_test_matrix = test_matrix
604: 
605:     else:
606:         # No PR number, no tag, we can just return the test matrix as it is
607:         filtered_test_matrix = test_matrix
608: 
609:     if args.selected_test_configs:
610:         selected_test_configs = {
611:             v.strip().lower()
612:             for v in args.selected_test_configs.split(",")
613:             if v.strip()
614:         }
615:         filtered_test_matrix = filter_selected_test_configs(
616:             filtered_test_matrix, selected_test_configs
617:         )
618: 
619:     if args.event_name == "schedule" and args.schedule == "29 8 * * *":
620:         # we don't want to run the mem leak check or disabled tests on normal
621:         # periodically scheduled jobs, only the ones at this time
622:         filtered_test_matrix = set_periodic_modes(filtered_test_matrix, args.job_name)
623: 
624:     if args.workflow and args.job_name and args.branch not in EXCLUDED_BRANCHES:
625:         # If both workflow and job name are available, we will check if the current job
626:         # is disabled and remove it and all its dependants from the test matrix
627:         filtered_test_matrix = remove_disabled_jobs(
628:             args.workflow, args.job_name, filtered_test_matrix
629:         )
630: 
631:         filtered_test_matrix = mark_unstable_jobs(
632:             args.workflow, args.job_name, filtered_test_matrix
633:         )
634: 
635:     pr_body = get_pr_info(int(pr_number)).get("body", "") if pr_number else ""
636: 
637:     perform_misc_tasks(
638:         labels=labels,
639:         test_matrix=filtered_test_matrix,
640:         job_name=args.job_name,
641:         pr_body=pr_body if pr_body else "",
642:         branch=args.branch,
643:         tag=tag,
644:     )
645: 
646:     # Set the filtered test matrix as the output
647:     set_output("test-matrix", json.dumps(filtered_test_matrix))
648: 
649:     filtered_test_matrix_len = len(filtered_test_matrix.get("include", []))
650:     # and also put a flag if the test matrix is empty, so subsequent jobs can
651:     # quickly check it without the need to parse the JSON string
652:     set_output("is-test-matrix-empty", filtered_test_matrix_len == 0)
653: 
654: 
655: if __name__ == "__main__":
656:     main()

{"content":"1: {% import 'common.yml.j2' as common %}

4: {%- block name -%}
5: # Template is at:    .github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2
6: # Generation script: .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py
7: name: !{{ build_environment }}
8: {%- endblock %}
9: 
10: {%- macro set_runner_specific_vars() -%}
11:       # NOTE: These environment variables are put here so that they can be applied on every job equally
12:       #       They are also here because setting them at a workflow level doesn't give us access to the
13:       #       runner.temp variable, which we need.
14:       - name: Populate binary env
15:         shell: bash
16:         run: |
17:           # shellcheck disable=SC2129
18:           echo "BINARY_ENV_FILE=${RUNNER_TEMP}/env" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
19:           # shellcheck disable=SC2129
20:           echo "PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
21:           # shellcheck disable=SC2129
22:           echo "MAC_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
23: {%- endmacro %}
24: 
25: {%- macro setup_python(py_ver) -%}
26:       - name: Setup Python
27:         uses: actions/setup-python@v6
28:         with:
29:           # .4 version is min minor for 3.10, and also no-gil version of 3.13 needs at least 3.13.3
30:           python-version: "!{{ py_ver.strip('t') + ('.4' if '3.14' not in py_ver else '.0') }}"
31:           freethreaded: !{{ "true" if py_ver.endswith('t') else "false" }}
32: {%- endmacro %}
33: 
34: on:
35: # TODO: Migrate to new ciflow trigger, reference https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/70321
36:   push:
37:     # NOTE: Meta Employees can trigger new nightlies using: https://fburl.com/trigger_pytorch_nightly_build
38:     branches:
39:       - nightly
40:     tags:
41:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate builds
42:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1
43:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+
44: {%- for label in ciflow_config.labels | sort %}
45:     {%- if loop.first and branches != "nightly" %}
46:     tags:
47:     {%- endif %}
48:       - '!{{ label }}/*'
49: {%- endfor %}
50:   workflow_dispatch:
51: 
52: env:
53:   ALPINE_IMAGE: "308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine"
54:   AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
55:   BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: !{{ build_environment }}
56:   GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
57:   PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
58:   SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 0
59: !{{ common.concurrency(build_environment) }}
60:

22:     reenabled_issues = os.getenv("REENABLED_ISSUES", "")
23:     issue_numbers = reenabled_issues.split(",")
24:     print("Ignoring disabled issues: ", issue_numbers)
25:     return issue_numbers

28: DISABLED_TESTS_FILE = ".pytorch-disabled-tests.json"
29: ADDITIONAL_CI_FILES_FOLDER = Path(".additional_ci_files")
30: TEST_TIMES_FILE = "test-times.json"
31: TEST_CLASS_TIMES_FILE = "test-class-times.json"
32: TEST_FILE_RATINGS_FILE = "test-file-ratings.json"
33: TEST_CLASS_RATINGS_FILE = "test-class-ratings.json"
34: TD_HEURISTIC_PROFILING_FILE = "td_heuristic_profiling.json"
35: TD_HEURISTIC_HISTORICAL_EDITED_FILES = "td_heuristic_historical_edited_files.json"
36: TD_HEURISTIC_PREVIOUSLY_FAILED = "previous_failures.json"
37: TD_HEURISTIC_PREVIOUSLY_FAILED_ADDITIONAL = "previous_failures_additional.json"
38: 
39: FILE_CACHE_LIFESPAN_SECONDS = datetime.timedelta(hours=3).seconds
40: 
41: 
42: def fetch_and_cache(
43:     dirpath: str | Path,
44:     name: str,
45:     url: str,
46:     process_fn: Callable[[dict[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]],
47: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
48:     """
49:     This fetch and cache utils allows sharing between different process.
50:     """
51:     Path(dirpath).mkdir(exist_ok=True)
52: 
53:     path = os.path.join(dirpath, name)
54:     print(f"Downloading {url} to {path}")
55: 
56:     def is_cached_file_valid() -> bool:
57:         # Check if the file is new enough (see: FILE_CACHE_LIFESPAN_SECONDS). A real check
58:         # could make a HEAD request and check/store the file's ETag
59:         fname = Path(path)
60:         now = datetime.datetime.now()
61:         mtime = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(fname.stat().st_mtime)
62:         diff = now - mtime
63:         return diff.total_seconds() < FILE_CACHE_LIFESPAN_SECONDS
64: 
65:     if os.path.exists(path) and is_cached_file_valid():
66:         # Another test process already download the file, so don't re-do it
67:         with open(path) as f:
68:             return cast(dict[str, Any], json.load(f))
69: 
70:     for _ in range(3):
71:         try:
72:             contents = urlopen(url, timeout=5).read().decode("utf-8")
73:             processed_contents = process_fn(json.loads(contents))
74:             with open(path, "w") as f:
75:                 f.write(json.dumps(processed_contents))
76:             return processed_contents
77:         except Exception as e:
78:             print(f"Could not download {url} because: {e}.")
79:     print(f"All retries exhausted, downloading {url} failed.")
80:     return {}

#!/bin/bash

# Common util **functions** that can be sourced in other scripts.

# note: printf is used instead of echo to avoid backslash
# processing and to properly handle values that begin with a '-'.

log() { printf '%s\n' "$*"; }
error() { log "ERROR: $*" >&2; }
fatal() { error "$@"; exit 1; }

retry () {
    "$@" || (sleep 10 && "$@") || (sleep 20 && "$@") || (sleep 40 && "$@")
}

# compositional trap taken from https://stackoverflow.com/a/7287873/23845
# appends a command to a trap
#
# - 1st arg:  code to add
# - remaining args:  names of traps to modify
#
trap_add() {
    trap_add_cmd=$1; shift || fatal "${FUNCNAME[0]} usage error"
    for trap_add_name in "$@"; do
        trap -- "$(
            # helper fn to get existing trap command from output
            # of trap -p
            extract_trap_cmd() { printf '%s\n' "$3"; }
            # print existing trap command with newline
            eval "extract_trap_cmd $(trap -p "${trap_add_name}")"
            # print the new trap command
            printf '%s\n' "${trap_add_cmd}"
        )" "${trap_add_name}" \
            || fatal "unable to add to trap ${trap_add_name}"
    done
}
# set the trace attribute for the above function.  this is
# required to modify DEBUG or RETURN traps because functions don't
# inherit them unless the trace attribute is set
declare -f -t trap_add

function assert_git_not_dirty() {
    # TODO: we should add an option to `build_amd.py` that reverts the repo to
    #       an unmodified state.
    if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" != *rocm* ]] && [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" != *xla* ]] ; then
        git_status=$(git status --porcelain | grep -v '?? third_party' || true)
        if [[ $git_status ]]; then
            echo "Build left local git repository checkout dirty"
            echo "git status --porcelain:"
            echo "${git_status}"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
}

function pip_install_whl() {
  # This is used to install PyTorch and other build artifacts wheel locally
  # without using any network connection

  # Convert the input arguments into an array
  local args=("$@")

  # Check if the first argument contains multiple paths separated by spaces
  if [[ "${args[0]}" == *" "* ]]; then
    # Split the string by spaces into an array
    IFS=' ' read -r -a paths <<< "${args[0]}"
    # Loop through each path and install individually
    for path in "${paths[@]}"; do
      echo "Installing $path"
      python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps "$path"
    done
  else
    # Loop through each argument and install individually
    for path in "${args[@]}"; do
      echo "Installing $path"
      python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps "$path"
    done
  fi
}

function pip_build_and_install() {
  local build_target=$1
  local wheel_dir=$2

  local found_whl=0
  for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
  do
    if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
      found_whl=1
      break
    fi
  done

  # Build the wheel if it doesn't exist
  if [ "${found_whl}" == "0" ]; then
    python3 -m pip wheel \
      --no-build-isolation \
      --no-deps \
      -w "${wheel_dir}" \
      "${build_target}"
  fi

  for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
  do
    pip_install_whl "${file}"
  done
}

function pip_install() {
  # retry 3 times
  pip_install_pkg="python3 -m pip install --progress-bar off"
  ${pip_install_pkg} "$@" || \
    ${pip_install_pkg} "$@" || \
    ${pip_install_pkg} "$@"
}

function pip_uninstall() {
  # uninstall 2 times
  pip3 uninstall -y "$@" || pip3 uninstall -y "$@"
}

function get_exit_code() {
  set +e
  "$@"
  retcode=$?
  set -e
  return $retcode
}

function get_bazel() {
  # Download and use the cross-platform, dependency-free Python
  # version of Bazelisk to fetch the platform specific version of
  # Bazel to use from .bazelversion.
  retry curl --location --output tools/bazel \
    https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bazelbuild/bazelisk/v1.23.0/bazelisk.py
  shasum --algorithm=1 --check \
    <(echo '01df9cf7f08dd80d83979ed0d0666a99349ae93c  tools/bazel')
  chmod u+x tools/bazel
}

function install_monkeytype {
  # Install MonkeyType
  pip_install MonkeyType
}


function get_pinned_commit() {
  cat .github/ci_commit_pins/"${1}".txt
}

function detect_cuda_arch() {
  if [[ "${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}" == *cuda* ]]; then
    if command -v nvidia-smi; then
      TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=compute_cap --format=csv | tail -n 1)
    elif [[ "${TEST_CONFIG}" == *nogpu* ]]; then
      # There won't be nvidia-smi in nogpu tests, so just set TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST to the default
      # minimum supported value here
      TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=8.0
    fi
    export TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST
  fi
}

function install_torchaudio() {
  local commit
  commit=$(get_pinned_commit audio)
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/audio.git@${commit}" dist/audio
}

function install_torchtext() {
  local data_commit
  local text_commit
  data_commit=$(get_pinned_commit data)
  text_commit=$(get_pinned_commit text)
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/data.git@${data_commit}" dist/data
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/text.git@${text_commit}" dist/text
}

function install_torchvision() {
  local orig_preload
  local commit
  commit=$(get_pinned_commit vision)
  orig_preload=${LD_PRELOAD}
  if [ -n "${LD_PRELOAD}" ]; then
    # Silence dlerror to work-around glibc ASAN bug, see https://sourceware.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=27653#c9
    echo 'char* dlerror(void) { return "";}'|gcc -fpic -shared -o "${HOME}/dlerror.so" -x c -
    LD_PRELOAD=${orig_preload}:${HOME}/dlerror.so
  fi

  if [[ "${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}" == *cuda* ]]; then
    # Not sure if both are needed, but why not
    export FORCE_CUDA=1
    export WITH_CUDA=1
  fi
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/vision.git@${commit}" dist/vision

  if [ -n "${LD_PRELOAD}" ]; then
    LD_PRELOAD=${orig_preload}
  fi
}

function install_torchrec_and_fbgemm() {
  local torchrec_commit
  torchrec_commit=$(get_pinned_commit torchrec)
  local fbgemm_commit
  fbgemm_commit=$(get_pinned_commit fbgemm)
  if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" == *rocm* ]] ; then
    fbgemm_commit=$(get_pinned_commit fbgemm_rocm)
  fi
  pip_uninstall torchrec-nightly
  pip_uninstall fbgemm-gpu-nightly
  pip_install setuptools-git-versioning scikit-build pyre-extensions

  if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" == *rocm* ]] ; then
    # install torchrec first because it installs fbgemm nightly on top of rocm fbgemm
    pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/torchrec.git@${torchrec_commit}" dist/torchrec
    pip_uninstall fbgemm-gpu-nightly

    # Set ROCM_HOME isn't available, use ROCM_PATH if set or /opt/rocm
    ROCM_HOME="${ROCM_HOME:-${ROCM_PATH:-/opt/rocm}}"

    # Find rocm_version.h header file for ROCm version extract
    rocm_version_h="${ROCM_HOME}/include/rocm-core/rocm_version.h"
    if [ ! -f "$rocm_version_h" ]; then
        rocm_version_h="${ROCM_HOME}/include/rocm_version.h"
    fi

    # Error out if rocm_version.h not found
    if [ ! -f "$rocm_version_h" ]; then
        echo "Error: rocm_version.h not found in expected locations." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract major, minor and patch ROCm version numbers
    MAJOR_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_MAJOR' "$rocm_version_h" | awk '{print $3}')
    MINOR_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_MINOR' "$rocm_version_h" | awk '{print $3}')
    PATCH_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_PATCH' "$rocm_version_h" | awk '{print $3}')
    ROCM_INT=$((MAJOR_VERSION * 10000 + MINOR_VERSION * 100 + PATCH_VERSION))
    echo "ROCm version: $ROCM_INT"
    export BUILD_ROCM_VERSION="$MAJOR_VERSION.$MINOR_VERSION"

    pip_install tabulate  # needed for newer fbgemm
    pip_install patchelf  # needed for rocm fbgemm

    local wheel_dir=dist/fbgemm_gpu
    local found_whl=0
    for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
    do
      if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
        found_whl=1
        break
      fi
    done

    # Build the wheel if it doesn't exist
    if [ "${found_whl}" == "0" ]; then
      git clone --recursive https://github.com/pytorch/fbgemm
      pushd fbgemm/fbgemm_gpu
      git checkout "${fbgemm_commit}" --recurse-submodules
      # until the fbgemm_commit includes the tbb patch
      patch <<'EOF'
--- a/FbgemmGpu.cmake
+++ b/FbgemmGpu.cmake
@@ -184,5 +184,6 @@ gpu_cpp_library(
     fbgemm_gpu_tbe_cache
     fbgemm_gpu_tbe_optimizers
     fbgemm_gpu_tbe_utils
+    tbb
   DESTINATION
     fbgemm_gpu)
EOF
      python setup.py bdist_wheel --build-variant=rocm
      popd

      # Save the wheel before cleaning up
      mkdir -p dist/fbgemm_gpu
      cp fbgemm/fbgemm_gpu/dist/*.whl dist/fbgemm_gpu
    fi

    for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
    do
      pip_install_whl "${file}"
    done

    rm -rf fbgemm
  else
    pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/torchrec.git@${torchrec_commit}" dist/torchrec
    # Skip fbgemm for CUDA 13 as it's not compatible yet
    if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" != *cuda13* ]]; then
      pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/FBGEMM.git@${fbgemm_commit}#subdirectory=fbgemm_gpu" dist/fbgemm_gpu
    fi
  fi
}

function clone_pytorch_xla() {
  if [[ ! -d ./xla ]]; then
    git clone --recursive --quiet https://github.com/pytorch/xla.git
    pushd xla
    # pin the xla hash so that we don't get broken by changes to xla
    git checkout "$(cat ../.github/ci_commit_pins/xla.txt)"
    git submodule sync
    git submodule update --init --recursive
    popd
  fi
}

function install_torchao() {
  local commit
  commit=$(get_pinned_commit torchao)
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/ao.git@${commit}" dist/ao
}

function install_flash_attn_cute() {
  echo "Installing FlashAttention CuTe from GitHub..."
  # Grab latest main til we have a pinned commit
  local flash_attn_commit
  flash_attn_commit=$(git ls-remote https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention.git HEAD | cut -f1)

  # Clone the repo to a temporary directory
  rm -rf flash-attention-build
  git clone --depth 1 --recursive https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention.git flash-attention-build

  pushd flash-attention-build
  git checkout "${flash_attn_commit}"

  # Install only the 'cute' sub-directory
  pip_install -e flash_attn/cute/
  popd

  # remove the local repo
  rm -rf flash-attention-build
  echo "FlashAttention CuTe installation complete."
}

function print_sccache_stats() {
  echo 'PyTorch Build Statistics'
  sccache --show-stats

  if [[ -n "${OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID}" ]]; then
    sccache --show-stats --stats-format json | jq .stats \
      > "sccache-stats-${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}-${OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID}.json"
  else
    echo "env var OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID not set, will not write sccache stats to json"
  fi
}

# required to modify DEBUG or RETURN traps because functions don't
# inherit them unless the trace attribute is set
declare -f -t trap_add

function assert_git_not_dirty() {

    #       an unmodified state.
    if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" != *rocm* ]] && [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" != *xla* ]] ; then

        if [[ $git_status ]]; then

            echo "git status --porcelain:"

            exit 1
        fi
    fi

function pip_install_whl() {
  # This is used to install PyTorch and other build artifacts wheel locally
  # without using any network connection

  # Convert the input arguments into an array
  local args=("$@")

  # Check if the first argument contains multiple paths separated by spaces
  if [[ "${args[0]}" == *" "* ]]; then
    # Split the string by spaces into an array
    IFS=' ' read -r -a paths <<< "${args[0]}"
    # Loop through each path and install individually
    for path in "${paths[@]}"; do
      echo "Installing $path"
      python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps "$path"
    done
  else
    # Loop through each argument and install individually
    for path in "${args[@]}"; do
      echo "Installing $path"
      python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps "$path"
    done
  fi
}

function pip_build_and_install() {
  local build_target=$1
  local wheel_dir=$2

  local found_whl=0
  for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
  do

      found_whl=1
      break
    fi
  done

  # Build the wheel if it doesn't exist
  if [ "${found_whl}" == "0" ]; then
    python3 -m pip wheel \
      --no-build-isolation \
      --no-deps \
      -w "${wheel_dir}" \
      "${build_target}"
  fi

  for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
  do
    pip_install_whl "${file}"
  done
}

function pip_install() {
  # retry 3 times
  pip_install_pkg="python3 -m pip install --progress-bar off"
  ${pip_install_pkg} "$@" || \
    ${pip_install_pkg} "$@" || \
    ${pip_install_pkg} "$@"
}

function pip_uninstall() {
  # uninstall 2 times
  pip3 uninstall -y "$@" || pip3 uninstall -y "$@"
}

function get_exit_code() {
  set +e
  "$@"
  retcode=$?
  set -e
  return $retcode
}

function get_bazel() {
  # Download and use the cross-platform, dependency-free Python
  # version of Bazelisk to fetch the platform specific version of
  # Bazel to use from .bazelversion.
  retry curl --location --output tools/bazel \
    https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bazelbuild/bazelisk/v1.23.0/bazelisk.py
  shasum --algorithm=1 --check \
    <(echo '01df9cf7f08dd80d83979ed0d0666a99349ae93c  tools/bazel')
  chmod u+x tools/bazel
}

function install_monkeytype {
  # Install MonkeyType
  pip_install MonkeyType
}


function get_pinned_commit() {
  cat .github/ci_commit_pins/"${1}".txt
}

function detect_cuda_arch() {
  if [[ "${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}" == *cuda* ]]; then
    if command -v nvidia-smi; then
      TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=compute_cap --format=csv | tail -n 1)
    elif [[ "${TEST_CONFIG}" == *nogpu* ]]; then
      # There won't be nvidia-smi in nogpu tests, so just set TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST to the default
      # minimum supported value here
      TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=8.0
    fi
    export TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST
  fi
}

function install_torchaudio() {
  local commit
  commit=$(get_pinned_commit audio)
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/audio.git@${commit}" dist/audio
}

function install_torchtext() {
  local data_commit
  local text_commit
  data_commit=$(get_pinned_commit data)
  text_commit=$(get_pinned_commit text)
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/data.git@${data_commit}" dist/data
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/text.git@${text_commit}" dist/text
}

function install_torchvision() {
  local orig_preload
  local commit
  commit=$(get_pinned_commit vision)
  orig_preload=${LD_PRELOAD}
  if [ -n "${LD_PRELOAD}" ]; then
    # Silence dlerror to work-around glibc ASAN bug, see https://sourceware.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=27653#c9
    echo 'char* dlerror(void) { return "";}'|gcc -fpic -shared -o "${HOME}/dlerror.so" -x c -
    LD_PRELOAD=${orig_preload}:${HOME}/dlerror.so
  fi

  if [[ "${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}" == *cuda* ]]; then
    # Not sure if both are needed, but why not
    export FORCE_CUDA=1
    export WITH_CUDA=1
  fi
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/vision.git@${commit}" dist/vision

  if [ -n "${LD_PRELOAD}" ]; then
    LD_PRELOAD=${orig_preload}
  fi
}

function install_torchrec_and_fbgemm() {
  local torchrec_commit
  torchrec_commit=$(get_pinned_commit torchrec)
  local fbgemm_commit
  fbgemm_commit=$(get_pinned_commit fbgemm)
  if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" == *rocm* ]] ; then
    fbgemm_commit=$(get_pinned_commit fbgemm_rocm)
  fi
  pip_uninstall torchrec-nightly
  pip_uninstall fbgemm-gpu-nightly
  pip_install setuptools-git-versioning scikit-build pyre-extensions

  if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" == *rocm* ]] ; then
    # install torchrec first because it installs fbgemm nightly on top of rocm fbgemm
    pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/torchrec.git@${torchrec_commit}" dist/torchrec
    pip_uninstall fbgemm-gpu-nightly

    # Set ROCM_HOME isn't available, use ROCM_PATH if set or /opt/rocm
    ROCM_HOME="${ROCM_HOME:-${ROCM_PATH:-/opt/rocm}}"

    # Find rocm_version.h header file for ROCm version extract
    rocm_version_h="${ROCM_HOME}/include/rocm-core/rocm_version.h"
    if [ ! -f "$rocm_version_h" ]; then
        rocm_version_h="${ROCM_HOME}/include/rocm_version.h"
    fi

    # Error out if rocm_version.h not found
    if [ ! -f "$rocm_version_h" ]; then
        echo "Error: rocm_version.h not found in expected locations." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract major, minor and patch ROCm version numbers
    MAJOR_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_MAJOR' "$rocm_version_h" | awk '{print $3}')
    MINOR_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_MINOR' "$rocm_version_h" | awk '{print $3}')
    PATCH_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_PATCH' "$rocm_version_h" | awk '{print $3}')
    ROCM_INT=$((MAJOR_VERSION * 10000 + MINOR_VERSION * 100 + PATCH_VERSION))
    echo "ROCm version: $ROCM_INT"
    export BUILD_ROCM_VERSION="$MAJOR_VERSION.$MINOR_VERSION"

    pip_install tabulate  # needed for newer fbgemm
    pip_install patchelf  # needed for rocm fbgemm

    local wheel_dir=dist/fbgemm_gpu
    local found_whl=0
    for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
    do
      if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
        found_whl=1
        break
      fi
    done

    # Build the wheel if it doesn't exist
    if [ "${found_whl}" == "0" ]; then
      git clone --recursive https://github.com/pytorch/fbgemm
      pushd fbgemm/fbgemm_gpu
      git checkout "${fbgemm_commit}" --recurse-submodules
      # until the fbgemm_commit includes the tbb patch
      patch <<'EOF'
--- a/FbgemmGpu.cmake
+++ b/FbgemmGpu.cmake
@@ -184,5 +184,6 @@ gpu_cpp_library(
     fbgemm_gpu_tbe_cache
     fbgemm_gpu_tbe_optimizers
     fbgemm_gpu_tbe_utils
+    tbb
   DESTINATION
     fbgemm_gpu)
EOF
      python setup.py bdist_wheel --build-variant=rocm
      popd

      # Save the wheel before cleaning up
      mkdir -p dist/fbgemm_gpu
      cp fbgemm/fbgemm_gpu/dist/*.whl dist/fbgemm_gpu
    fi

    for file in "${wheel_dir}"/*.whl
    do
      pip_install_whl "${file}"
    done

    rm -rf fbgemm
  else
    pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/torchrec.git@${torchrec_commit}" dist/torchrec
    # Skip fbgemm for CUDA 13 as it's not compatible yet
    if [[ "$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT" != *cuda13* ]]; then
      pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/FBGEMM.git@${fbgemm_commit}#subdirectory=fbgemm_gpu" dist/fbgemm_gpu
    fi
  fi
}

function clone_pytorch_xla() {
  if [[ ! -d ./xla ]]; then
    git clone --recursive --quiet https://github.com/pytorch/xla.git
    pushd xla
    # pin the xla hash so that we don't get broken by changes to xla
    git checkout "$(cat ../.github/ci_commit_pins/xla.txt)"
    git submodule sync
    git submodule update --init --recursive
    popd
  fi
}

function install_torchao() {
  local commit
  commit=$(get_pinned_commit torchao)
  pip_build_and_install "git+https://github.com/pytorch/ao.git@${commit}" dist/ao
}

function install_flash_attn_cute() {
  echo "Installing FlashAttention CuTe from GitHub..."
  # Grab latest main til we have a pinned commit
  local flash_attn_commit
  flash_attn_commit=$(git ls-remote https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention.git HEAD | cut -f1)

  # Clone the repo to a temporary directory
  rm -rf flash-attention-build
  git clone --depth 1 --recursive https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention.git flash-attention-build

  pushd flash-attention-build
  git checkout "${flash_attn_commit}"

  # Install only the 'cute' sub-directory
  pip_install -e flash_attn/cute/
  popd

  # remove the local repo
  rm -rf flash-attention-build
  echo "FlashAttention CuTe installation complete."
}

function print_sccache_stats() {
  echo 'PyTorch Build Statistics'
  sccache --show-stats

  if [[ -n "${OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID}" ]]; then
    sccache --show-stats --stats-format json | jq .stats \
      > "sccache-stats-${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}-${OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID}.json"
  else
    echo "env var OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID not set, will not write sccache stats to json"
  fi
}
