{"content":"1: # The point of this workflow is to test that a FA3 wheel that was built based off the
2: # stable ABI as of torch nightly 20250830 can still run on the newer torch.
3: #
4: # This workflow is very similar to the _linux-test.yml workflow, with the following
5: # differences:
6: #   1. It is simpler (there is no test matrix)
7: #   2. It pulls flash-attention as a secondary repository in order to access the tests.
8: #      Note that it does not BUILD anything from flash-attention, as we have a prebuilt
9: #      wheel. We pull flash-attention only to run a few tests.
10: #   3. It runs only FA3 tests. No PyTorch tests are run.
11: name: linux-test-stable-fa3
12: 
13: on:
14:   workflow_call:
15:     inputs:
16:       build-environment:
17:         required: true
18:         type: string
19:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
20:       docker-image:
21:         required: true
22:         type: string
23:         description: Docker image to run in.
24:       timeout-minutes:
25:         required: false
26:         type: number
27:         default: 30
28:         description: |
29:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
30:       s3-bucket:
31:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
32:         required: false
33:         type: string
34:         default: "gha-artifacts"
35:     secrets:
36:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
37:         required: false
38:         description: |
39:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
40:       VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:
41:         required: false
42:         description: |
43:           HF Auth token to test vllm
44:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:
45:         required: false
46:         description: |
47:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint
48: 
49: env:
50:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
51: 
52: jobs:
53:   test:
54:     # Don't run on forked repos
55:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
56:     runs-on: linux.aws.h100
57:     timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes || 30 }}
58:     permissions:
59:       id-token: write
60:       contents: read
61:     steps:
62:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
63:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
64:         with:
65:           no-sudo: true
66: 
67:       - name: Checkout flash-attention as a secondary repository
68:         uses: actions/checkout@v4
69:         with:
70:           repository: Dao-AILab/flash-attention
71:           path: flash-attention
72: 
73:       - name: Setup Linux
74:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
75: 
76:       - name: Calculate docker image
77:         id: calculate-docker-image
78:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
79:         with:
80:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
81: 
82:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
83:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
84:         env:
85:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
86:         shell: bash
87:         run: |
88:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
89:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
90: 
91:       - name: Pull docker image
92:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
93:         with:
94:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
95: 
96:       - name: Check if in a container runner
97:         shell: bash
98:         id: check_container_runner
99:         run: echo "IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
100: 
101:       - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run
102:         id: setup-gpu-flag
103:         run: echo "GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
104: 
105:       - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container
106:         id: setup-sscache-port-flag
107:         run: echo "SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
108:         if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}
109: 
110:       - name: Get workflow job id
111:         id: get-job-id
112:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
113:         if: always()
114:         with:
115:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
116: 
117:       - name: Download build artifacts
118:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
119:         with:
120:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
121:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
122: 
123:       - name: Parse ref
124:         id: parse-ref
125:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
126: 
127:       - name: Set Test step time
128:         id: test-timeout
129:         shell: bash
130:         env:
131:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}
132:         run: |
133:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
134: 
135:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker
136:         shell: bash
137:         run: |
138:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
139:           env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
140: 
141:       - name: Test
142:         id: test
143:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
144:         env:
145:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
146:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
147:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
148:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
149:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
150:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
151:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
152:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
153:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
154:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
155:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
156:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
157:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}
158:           SHM_SIZE: '2g'
159:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
160:           VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN }}
161:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
162:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
163:           ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX: ${{ github.job }}-${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
164:         run: |
165:           set -x
166: 
167:           TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test_fa3_abi_stable.sh
168: 
169:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things
170:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf "%.3f \n", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)
171:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap
172:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058
173:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$(("${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}" + 3))
174: 
175: 
176:           SHM_OPTS="--shm-size=${SHM_SIZE}"
177:           JENKINS_USER="--user jenkins"
178:           DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=
179: 
180:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
181:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase
182:           # Used for GPU_FLAG, SHM_OPTS, JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD since that doesn't play nice
183:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090
184:           container_name=$(docker run \
185:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \
186:             ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \
187:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
188:             -e PR_NUMBER \
189:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
190:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \
191:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \
192:             -e GITHUB_JOB \
193:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \
194:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \
195:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
196:             -e JOB_ID \
197:             -e JOB_NAME \
198:             -e BASE_SHA \
199:             -e BRANCH \
200:             -e SHA1 \
201:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
202:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \
203:             -e VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN \
204:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \
205:             -e ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX \
206:             --memory="${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g" \
207:             --memory-swap="${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g" \
208:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
209:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
210:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
211:             --ipc=host \
212:             ${SHM_OPTS} \
213:             --tty \
214:             --detach \
215:             --name="${container_name}" \
216:             ${JENKINS_USER} \
217:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
218:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
219:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}" \
220:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}
221:           )
222: 
223:           echo "DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
224: 
225:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "python3 -m pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}"
226: 
227:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)
228:         if: always()
229:         run: |
230:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156
231:           find . -iname "core.[1-9]*" -exec docker exec "${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}" sh -c "gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'" \;
232: 
233:       - name: Store Core dumps on S3
234:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
235:         if: failure()
236:         with:
237:           name: coredumps-fa3-stable-abi-smoke-tests
238:           retention-days: 14
239:           if-no-files-found: ignore
240:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*
241: 
242:       - name: Upload utilization stats
243:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' }}
244:         continue-on-error: true
245:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
246:         with:
247:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
248:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
249:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
250:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
251:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
252: 
253:       - name: Teardown Linux
254:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
255:         if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'
256: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_linux-test-stable-fa3.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: linux-test
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       test-matrix:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.
14:       docker-image:
15:         required: true
16:         type: string
17:         description: Docker image to run in.
18:       sync-tag:
19:         required: false
20:         type: string
21:         default: ""
22:         description: |
23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
25:       timeout-minutes:
26:         required: false
27:         type: number
28:         default: 240
29:         description: |
30:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
31:       use-gha:
32:         required: false
33:         type: string
34:         default: ""
35:         description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.
36:       dashboard-tag:
37:         required: false
38:         type: string
39:         default: ""
40:       s3-bucket:
41:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
42:         required: false
43:         type: string
44:         default: "gha-artifacts"
45:       aws-role-to-assume:
46:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts
47:         required: false
48:         type: string
49:         default: ""
50:       disable-monitor:
51:         description: |
52:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.
53:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,
54:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.
55:         required: false
56:         type: boolean
57:         default: false
58:       monitor-log-interval:
59:         description: |
60:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
61:         required: false
62:         type: number
63:         default: 5
64:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
65:         description: |
66:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
67:         required: false
68:         type: number
69:         default: 1
70:     secrets:
71:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
72:         required: false
73:         description: |
74:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
75:       VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:
76:         required: false
77:         description: |
78:           HF Auth token to test vllm
79:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:
80:         required: false
81:         description: |
82:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint
83: 
84: env:
85:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
86: 
87: jobs:
88:   test:
89:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix
90:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'
91:     strategy:
92:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}
93:       fail-fast: false
94:     environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}
95:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
96:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
97:     permissions:
98:       id-token: write
99:       contents: read
100:     steps:
101:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
102:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
103:         if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
104:         with:
105:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
106:           instructions: |
107:             All testing is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:
108:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash
109: 
110:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
111:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
112:         with:
113:           no-sudo: true
114: 
115:       - name: Setup Python
116:         if: contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')
117:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
118:         with:
119:           python-version: '3.12'
120:           cache: pip
121: 
122:       - name: Setup Linux
123:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
124:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')
125: 
126:       - name: configure aws credentials
127:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
128:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
129:         with:
130:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}
131:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test
132:           aws-region: us-east-1
133: 
134:       - name: Login to Amazon ECR
135:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}
136:         id: login-ecr
137:         continue-on-error: true
138:         uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1
139: 
140:       - name: Calculate docker image
141:         id: calculate-docker-image
142:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
143:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
144:         with:
145:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
146: 
147:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
148:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
149:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
150:         env:
151:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
152:         shell: bash
153:         run: |
154:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
155:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
156: 
157:       - name: Pull docker image
158:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
159:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
160:         with:
161:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
162: 
163:       - name: Check if in a container runner
164:         shell: bash
165:         id: check_container_runner
166:         run: echo "IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
167: 
168:       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG
169:         id: install-nvidia-driver
170:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main
171:         with:
172:           driver-version: ${{ matrix.config == 'legacy_nvidia_driver' && '525.105.17' || '580.82.07' }}
173:         if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}
174: 
175:       - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run
176:         id: setup-gpu-flag
177:         run: echo "GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
178:         if: ${{ steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && (steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' || contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')) }}
179: 
180:       - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container
181:         id: setup-sscache-port-flag
182:         run: echo "SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
183:         if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}
184: 
185:       - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency
186:         run: |
187:           sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1
188:           sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410
189:           nvidia-smi
190:         if: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}
191: 
192:       - name: Get workflow job id
193:         id: get-job-id
194:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
195:         if: always()
196:         with:
197:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
198: 
199:       - name: Start monitoring script
200:         id: monitor-script
201:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
202:         shell: bash
203:         continue-on-error: true
204:         env:
205:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
206:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
207:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
208:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
209:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
210:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
211:         run: |
212:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84
213:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &
214:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
215: 
216:       - name: Download build artifacts
217:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
218:         with:
219:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
220:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
221:           use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}
222: 
223:       - name: Download TD artifacts
224:         continue-on-error: true
225:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts
226: 
227:       - name: Download Windows torch wheel for cross-compilation
228:         if: matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact != ''
229:         uses: seemethere/download-artifact-s3@1da556a7aa0a088e3153970611f6c432d58e80e6 # v4.2.0
230:         with:
231:           name: ${{ matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact }}
232:           path: win-torch-wheel
233: 
234:       - name: Extract Windows wheel and setup CUDA libraries
235:         if: matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact != ''
236:         shell: bash
237:         run: |
238:           set -x
239: 
240:           # Find the wheel file
241:           WHEEL_FILE=$(find win-torch-wheel -name "*.whl" -type f | head -n 1)
242:           if [ -z "$WHEEL_FILE" ]; then
243:             echo "Error: No wheel file found in win-torch-wheel directory"
244:             exit 1
245:           fi
246:           echo "Found wheel file: $WHEEL_FILE"
247: 
248:           # Unzip the wheel file
249:           unzip -q "$WHEEL_FILE" -d win-torch-wheel-extracted
250:           echo "Extracted wheel contents"
251: 
252:           # Setup CUDA libraries (cuda.lib and cudart.lib) directory
253:           mkdir -p win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64
254:           if [ -f "win-torch-wheel/cuda.lib" ]; then
255:             mv win-torch-wheel/cuda.lib win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/
256:             echo "Moved cuda.lib to win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/"
257:           fi
258:           if [ -f "win-torch-wheel/cudart.lib" ]; then
259:             mv win-torch-wheel/cudart.lib win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/
260:             echo "Moved cudart.lib to win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/"
261:           fi
262: 
263:           # Verify CUDA libraries are present
264:           echo "CUDA libraries:"
265:           ls -la win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/ || echo "No CUDA libraries found"
266: 
267:       - name: Parse ref
268:         id: parse-ref
269:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
270: 
271:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues
272:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently
273:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do
274:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.
275:         id: keep-going
276:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
277:         with:
278:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
279:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
280:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
281: 
282:       - name: Set Test step time
283:         id: test-timeout
284:         shell: bash
285:         env:
286:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
287:         run: |
288:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
289: 
290:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker
291:         shell: bash
292:         run: |
293:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
294:           env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
295: 
296:       - name: Test
297:         id: test
298:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
299:         env:
300:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
301:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
302:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
303:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
304:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
305:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
306:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
307:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
308:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
309:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
310:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
311:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
312:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}
313:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}
314:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}
315:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}
316:           EXTRA_FLAGS: ${{ matrix.extra_flags || '' }}
317:           OP_BENCHMARK_TESTS: ${{ matrix.op_benchmark_tests }}
318:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}
319:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}
320:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}
321:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}
322:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}
323:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}
324:           TD_DISTRIBUTED: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-td-distributed }}
325:           # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs
326:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && 'ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2' || '' }}
327:           SCCACHE_REGION: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && 'us-east-1' || '' }}
328:           SHM_SIZE: ${{ steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && '2g' || '1g' }}
329:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
330:           DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
331:           XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}
332:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla
333:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}
334:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}
335:           DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}
336:           VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN }}
337:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
338:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
339:           ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
340:         run: |
341:           set -x
342: 
343:           if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then
344:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh
345:           elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then
346:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/onnx/test.sh
347:           else
348:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh
349:           fi
350: 
351:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things
352:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf "%.3f \n", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)
353:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap
354:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058
355:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$(("${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}" + 3))
356: 
357:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"s390x"* ]]; then
358:             SHM_OPTS=
359:             JENKINS_USER=
360:             # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours
361:             # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container
362:             # when job is cancelled
363:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD="sleep 12h"
364:             USED_IMAGE="${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}"
365:           else
366:             SHM_OPTS="--shm-size=${SHM_SIZE}"
367:             JENKINS_USER="--user jenkins"
368:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=
369:             USED_IMAGE="${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
370:           fi
371: 
372:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
373:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase
374:           # Used for GPU_FLAG, SHM_OPTS, JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD since that doesn't play nice
375:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090
376:           container_name=$(docker run \
377:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \
378:             ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \
379:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
380:             -e PR_NUMBER \
381:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
382:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \
383:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \
384:             -e GITHUB_JOB \
385:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \
386:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \
387:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
388:             -e JOB_ID \
389:             -e JOB_NAME \
390:             -e BASE_SHA \
391:             -e BRANCH \
392:             -e SHA1 \
393:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \
394:             -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \
395:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \
396:             -e TEST_CONFIG \
397:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \
398:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \
399:             -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \
400:             -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \
401:             -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \
402:             -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \
403:             -e NO_TD \
404:             -e TD_DISTRIBUTED \
405:             -e PR_LABELS \
406:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
407:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \
408:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \
409:             -e XLA_CUDA \
410:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \
411:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \
412:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \
413:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \
414:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \
415:             -e VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN \
416:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \
417:             -e DASHBOARD_TAG \
418:             -e ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX \
419:             --memory="${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g" \
420:             --memory-swap="${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g" \
421:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
422:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
423:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
424:             --ipc=host \
425:             ${SHM_OPTS} \
426:             --tty \
427:             --detach \
428:             --name="${container_name}" \
429:             ${JENKINS_USER} \
430:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
431:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
432:             "${USED_IMAGE}" \
433:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}
434:           )
435:           echo "DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
436: 
437:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"s390x"* ]]; then
438:             docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "python3 -m pip install -r .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt"
439:           fi
440: 
441:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "python3 -m pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}"
442: 
443:       - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed
444:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload
445:         continue-on-error: true
446:         if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
447:         with:
448:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache
449:           shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}
450:           sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
451:           test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}
452:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}
453: 
454:       - name: Authenticate with AWS
455:         if: ${{ always() && contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}
456:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
457:         with:
458:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results
459:           # The max duration enforced by the server side
460:           role-duration-seconds: 18000
461:           aws-region: us-east-1
462: 
463:       - name: Upload the benchmark results
464:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@main
465:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
466:         with:
467:           benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports
468:           dry-run: false
469:           schema-version: v3
470:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
471: 
472:       - name: Print remaining test logs
473:         shell: bash
474:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion
475:         run: |
476:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true
477: 
478:       - name: Stop monitoring script
479:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
480:         shell: bash
481:         continue-on-error: true
482:         env:
483:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
484:         run: |
485:           kill "$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID"
486: 
487:       - name: Upload test artifacts
488:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts
489:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'
490:         with:
491:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
492:           use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}
493:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
494: 
495:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)
496:         if: always()
497:         run: |
498:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156
499:           find . -iname "core.[1-9]*" -exec docker exec "${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}" sh -c "gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'" \;
500: 
501:       - name: Store Core dumps on S3
502:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
503:         if: failure()
504:         with:
505:           name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}
506:           retention-days: 14
507:           if-no-files-found: ignore
508:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*
509: 
510:       - name: Upload utilization stats
511:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
512:         continue-on-error: true
513:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
514:         with:
515:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
516:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
517:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
518:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
519:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
520: 
521:       - name: Teardown Linux
522:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
523:         if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'
524: 
525:       # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with
526:       # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does
527:       # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:
528:       #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second
529:       #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU
530:       #     unknown error
531:       #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch
532:       #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon
533:       #
534:       # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of
535:       # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs
536:       - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step
537:         if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')
538:         shell: bash
539:         run: |
540:           set +e
541:           set -x
542: 
543:           nvidia-smi
544:           # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in
545:           # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version
546:           # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information
547:           # would be missing (ERR!), for example:
548:           #
549:           # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
550:           # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |
551:           # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+
552:           # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |
553:           # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |
554:           # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |
555:           # |===============================+======================+======================|
556:           # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |
557:           # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |
558:           # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |
559:           # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+
560:           #
561:           # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
562:           # | Processes:                                                                  |
563:           # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |
564:           # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |
565:           # |=============================================================================|
566:           # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
567:           #
568:           # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when
569:           # Docker tries to run with --gpus all
570:           #
571:           # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like
572:           # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly
573:           nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0
574:           NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?
575: 
576:           # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action
577:           if [ "$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS" -ne 0 ] && [ "$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS" -ne 14 ]; then
578:             echo "NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner..."
579:             .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh
580:           fi
581: 
582:           # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the
583:           # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails
584:           # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000
585:           GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)
586:           NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?
587: 
588:           # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action
589:           if [ "$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS" -ne 0 ] && [ "$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS" -ne 14 ]; then
590:             echo "NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner..."
591:             .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh
592:           fi
593: 
594:           # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2
595:           if [ "$GPU_COUNT" -le 8 ] && [ "$GPU_COUNT" -ne 1 ] && [ "$GPU_COUNT" -ne 2 ] && [ "$GPU_COUNT" -ne 4 ] && [ "$GPU_COUNT" -ne 8 ]; then
596:             echo "NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down..."
597:             .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh
598:           fi
599: 
600:       - name: Cleanup docker
601:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
602:         shell: bash
603:         run: |
604:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop
605:           docker stop -a || true
606:           docker kill -a || true
607: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: mac-build
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       runner-type:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Name of the GitHub-managed runner type to use for the build.
14:       build-generates-artifacts:
15:         required: true
16:         type: boolean
17:         description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.
18:       xcode-version:
19:         required: false
20:         type: string
21:         default: ""
22:         description: What xcode version to build with.
23:       sync-tag:
24:         required: false
25:         type: string
26:         default: ""
27:         description: |
28:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
29:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
30:       python-version:
31:         required: false
32:         type: string
33:         default: "3.12"
34:         description: |
35:           The python version to be used. Will be 3.9 by default
36:       test-matrix:
37:         required: false
38:         type: string
39:         description: |
40:           An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This
41:           is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter
42:           logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds
43:       sccache-use-gha:
44:         required: false
45:         type: boolean
46:         default: false
47:         description: If true, use the Github cache as the storage option for sccache instead of S3.
48: 
49:     outputs:
50:       test-matrix:
51:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}
52:         description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.
53:       build-outcome:
54:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-outcome }}
55:         description: The outcome of the build step. This is used to influence test filtering logic later on.
56:       build-environment:
57:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}
58:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
59: 
60: jobs:
61:   build:
62:     # Don't run on forked repos.
63:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
64:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner-type }}
65:     env:
66:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
67:       SCCACHE_USE_GHA: ${{ inputs.sccache-use-gha }}  # this is placed here instead of the sccache step to appease actionlint
68:     outputs:
69:       build-outcome: ${{ steps.build.outcome }}
70:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}
71:       build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
72:     steps:
73:       - name: Clean up disk space before running MacOS workflow
74:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@main
75: 
76:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
77:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
78:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
79: 
80:       - name: Set xcode version
81:         env:
82:           XCODE_VERSION: ${{ inputs.xcode-version }}
83:         run: |
84:           if [ -n "${XCODE_VERSION}" ]; then
85:             echo "DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode_${XCODE_VERSION}.app/Contents/Developer" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
86:           fi
87: 
88:       - name: Setup Python
89:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-python@main
90:         with:
91:           python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}
92:           pip-requirements-file: .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt
93: 
94:       - name: Install sccache (only for non-forked PRs, and pushes to trunk)
95:         uses: nick-fields/retry@7152eba30c6575329ac0576536151aca5a72780e # v3.0.0
96:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository }}
97:         with:
98:           timeout_minutes: 5
99:           max_attempts: 3
100:           retry_wait_seconds: 90
101:           command: |
102:             set -ex
103: 
104:             DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE=0
105:             SCCACHE_VERSION="0.4.1"
106:             LOCAL_PATH="/usr/local/bin"
107: 
108:             if [ ! -f "${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache" ]; then
109:               DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE=1
110:             else
111:               LOCAL_VERSION=$("${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache" --version | cut -d" " -f2)
112: 
113:               if [ "${LOCAL_VERSION}" != "${SCCACHE_VERSION}" ]; then
114:                 DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE=1
115:               fi
116:             fi
117: 
118:             if [ "${DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE}" == "1" ]; then
119:               sudo curl --retry 3 --retry-all-errors "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ossci-macos/sccache/sccache-v0.4.1-${RUNNER_ARCH}" --output "${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache"
120:               sudo chmod +x "${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache"
121:             fi
122: 
123:             if [[ "${SCCACHE_USE_GHA}" == "true" ]]; then
124:               echo "ACTIONS_CACHE_URL=${ACTIONS_CACHE_URL}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
125:               echo "ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN=${ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
126:               echo "SCCACHE_GHA_ENABLED=on" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
127:             else
128:               # The runner has access to the S3 bucket via IAM profile without the need
129:               # for any credential
130:               echo "SCCACHE_BUCKET=ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
131:               echo "SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX=${GITHUB_WORKFLOW}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
132:             fi
133: 
134:             # This is needed so that later build script could find sccache (which sccache)
135:             echo "${LOCAL_PATH}" >> $GITHUB_PATH
136: 
137:       - name: Get workflow job id
138:         id: get-job-id
139:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
140:         if: always()
141:         with:
142:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
143: 
144:       # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there
145:       - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)
146:         id: filter
147:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
148:         with:
149:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
150:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
151:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
152: 
153:       - name: Build
154:         if: steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == ''
155:         id: build
156:         env:
157:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
158:         run: |
159:           # TODO: Remove me later, and properly activate venv
160:           PATH="$VENV_PATH/bin:$PATH"
161:           export PATH
162: 
163:           # NB: Same trick as Linux, there is no need to initialize sccache with the risk of getting
164:           # it hangs or timeout at initialization. The cache will be started automatically
165:           export SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1
166:           .ci/pytorch/macos-build.sh
167: 
168:       - name: Archive artifacts into zip
169:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'
170:         run: |
171:           zip -1 -r artifacts.zip dist/ build/.ninja_log build/compile_commands.json .additional_ci_files
172: 
173:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts on GHA
174:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
175:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'
176:         with:
177:           name: ${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}
178:           retention-days: 14
179:           if-no-files-found: error
180:           path: artifacts.zip
181: 
182:       - name: Upload sccache stats to GHA
183:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
184:         # Only if sccache is installed, see above
185:         if: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' || github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository) && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' }}
186:         with:
187:           name: sccache-stats-${{ inputs.build-environment }}-runattempt${{ github.run_attempt }}-${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
188:           retention-days: 14
189:           if-no-files-found: warn
190:           path: sccache-stats-*.json
191: 
192:       - name: Clean up disk space
193:         if: always()
194:         continue-on-error: true
195:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@main
196: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_mac-build.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: mac-test
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       test-matrix:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.
14:       sync-tag:
15:         required: false
16:         type: string
17:         default: ""
18:         description: |
19:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
20:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
21:       python-version:
22:         required: false
23:         type: string
24:         default: "3.12"
25:         description: |
26:           The python version to be used. Will be 3.9 by default
27:       timeout-minutes:
28:         required: false
29:         type: number
30:         default: 270
31:         description: |
32:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
33:       disable-monitor:
34:         description: |
35:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.
36:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,
37:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.
38:         required: false
39:         type: boolean
40:         default: true
41:       monitor-log-interval:
42:         description: |
43:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
44:         required: false
45:         type: number
46:         default: 5
47:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
48:         description: |
49:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
50:         required: false
51:         type: number
52:         default: 1
53:     secrets:
54:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
55:         required: false
56:         description: |
57:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
58: 
59: jobs:
60:   test:
61:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix
62:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'
63:     defaults:
64:       run:
65:         shell: bash -e -l {0}
66:     strategy:
67:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}
68:       fail-fast: false
69:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
70:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
71:     env:
72:       GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
73:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
74:       TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}
75:       SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}
76:       NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}
77:       PR_BODY: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
78:     steps:
79:       - name: Print runner OS/HW info
80:         run: |
81:           sysctl machdep.cpu.brand_string kern.osproductversion
82: 
83:       - name: Clean up leftover processes on MacOS pet runner
84:         continue-on-error: true
85:         run: |
86:           for PROCESS in "python" "conda" "ninja" "clang"; do
87:             echo "Cleaning up all remaining ${PROCESS} process"
88:             pkill "${PROCESS}" || true
89:           done
90: 
91:       - name: Clean up brew miniconda, if installed
92:         continue-on-error: true
93:         run: |
94:           if brew list miniconda; then
95:             brew uninstall miniconda
96:             echo "REINSTALL_BREW_MINICONDA=1" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
97:           fi
98: 
99:       - name: Clean up leftover local python3 site-packages on MacOS pet runner
100:         continue-on-error: true
101:         run: |
102:           for dir in  ~/.local/lib/python3.*/site-packages; do
103:             echo "Cleaning up ${dir}"
104:             rm -rf "${dir}"
105:           done
106: 
107:       - name: Clean up disk space before running MacOS workflow
108:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@main
109: 
110:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
111:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
112:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
113: 
114:       - name: Get workflow job id
115:         id: get-job-id
116:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
117:         if: always()
118:         with:
119:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
120: 
121:       - name: Setup Python
122:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-python@main
123:         with:
124:           python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}
125:           pip-requirements-file: .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt
126: 
127:       - name: Start monitoring script
128:         id: monitor-script
129:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
130:         continue-on-error: true
131:         env:
132:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
133:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
134:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
135:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
136:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
137:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
138:         run: |
139:           "$VENV_PATH/bin/python3" -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7
140:           "$VENV_PATH/bin/python3" -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &
141:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
142: 
143:       - name: Download build artifacts
144:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
145:         with:
146:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
147:           use-gha: true
148: 
149:       - name: Download TD artifacts
150:         continue-on-error: true
151:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts
152:         with:
153:           use-gha: true
154: 
155:       - name: Parse ref
156:         id: parse-ref
157:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
158: 
159:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues
160:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently
161:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do
162:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.
163:         id: keep-going
164:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
165:         with:
166:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
167:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
168:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
169: 
170:       - name: Set Test step time
171:         id: test-timeout
172:         shell: bash
173:         env:
174:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
175:         run: |
176:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
177: 
178:       - name: Test
179:         id: test
180:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
181:         env:
182:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}
183:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}
184:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}
185:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}
186:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}
187:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}
188:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}
189:           PIP_REQUIREMENTS_FILE: .github/requirements/pip-requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt
190:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
191:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
192:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
193:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
194:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
195:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
196:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
197:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
198:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}
199:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
200:         run: |
201:           # shellcheck disable=SC1090
202:           set -ex
203: 
204:           # TODO: Remove me later, and properly activate venv
205:           PATH="$VENV_PATH/bin:$PATH"
206:           export PATH
207: 
208:           # Print out some information about the test environment
209:           for tool in python3 python; do
210:             which $tool
211:             $tool --version
212:           done
213: 
214:           python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps dist/*.whl
215: 
216:           set +e
217:           pushd "${RUNNER_TEMP}"
218:           # Install pip dependencies if they are not found. This is to mitigate a peculiar
219:           # flaky missing dependencies on MacOS
220:           python3 -c "import torch"
221:           RC=$?
222:           popd
223: 
224:           if [ "${RC}" -ne 0 ]; then
225:             python3 -mpip install --ignore-installed -r "${PIP_REQUIREMENTS_FILE}"
226:           fi
227:           set -e
228: 
229:           .ci/pytorch/macos-test.sh
230: 
231:       - name: Print remaining test logs
232:         shell: bash
233:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion
234:         run: |
235:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true
236: 
237:       - name: Run OP benchmark
238:         shell: bash
239:         if: ${{ contains(steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name, 'mps') }}
240:         run: |
241:           python3 test/bench_mps_ops.py
242: 
243: 
244:       - name: Stop monitoring script
245:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
246:         continue-on-error: true
247:         env:
248:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
249:         run: |
250:           kill "$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID"
251: 
252:       - name: Upload test artifacts
253:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts
254:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'
255:         with:
256:           use-gha: true
257:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
258: 
259:       - name: Upload the benchmark results
260:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@main
261:         with:
262:           benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports
263:           dry-run: false
264:           schema-version: v3
265:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
266: 
267:       - name: Upload utilization stats
268:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}
269:         continue-on-error: true
270:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
271:         with:
272:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
273:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
274:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
275:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
276:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
277:           local_path: usage_log.txt
278: 
279:       - name: Reinstall brew miniconda, if was installed
280:         if: always()
281:         continue-on-error: true
282:         run: |
283:           if [[ -n "$REINSTALL_BREW_MINICONDA" ]]; then
284:               brew install --cask miniconda
285:           fi
286: 
287:       - name: Clean up disk space
288:         if: always()
289:         continue-on-error: true
290:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@main
291: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_mac-test.yml"}

{"content":"1: # TODO: this looks sort of similar to _linux-test, but there are like a dozen
2: # places where you would have to insert an if statement. Probably it's better to
3: # just use a different workflow altogether
4: 
5: name: test
6: 
7: on:
8:   workflow_call:
9:     inputs:
10:       build-environment:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
14:       test-matrix:
15:         required: true
16:         type: string
17:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.
18:       docker-image:
19:         required: true
20:         type: string
21:         description: Docker image to run in.
22:       sync-tag:
23:         required: false
24:         type: string
25:         default: ""
26:         description: |
27:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
28:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
29:       timeout-minutes:
30:         required: false
31:         type: number
32:         default: 300
33:         description: |
34:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
35:       tests-to-include:
36:         required: false
37:         type: string
38:         default: ""
39:         description: |
40:           List of tests to include (empty string implies default list)
41:       dashboard-tag:
42:         required: false
43:         type: string
44:         default: ""
45:       disable-monitor:
46:         description: |
47:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.
48:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,
49:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.
50:         required: false
51:         type: boolean
52:         default: true
53:       monitor-log-interval:
54:         description: |
55:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
56:         required: false
57:         type: number
58:         default: 5
59:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
60:         description: |
61:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
62:         required: false
63:         type: number
64:         default: 1
65:     secrets:
66:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
67:         required: false
68:         description: |
69:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
70: env:
71:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
72: 
73: permissions:
74:   id-token: write
75:   contents: read
76: 
77: jobs:
78:   test:
79:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix
80:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'
81:     strategy:
82:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}
83:       fail-fast: false
84:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
85:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
86:     steps:
87:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
88:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
89:         with:
90:           no-sudo: true
91: 
92:       - name: Setup ROCm
93:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-rocm
94: 
95:       - name: Runner check GPU count (distributed jobs)
96:         if: ${{ contains(matrix.config, 'distributed') }}
97:         shell: bash
98:         run: |
99:           ngpu=$(rocminfo | grep -c -E 'Name:.*\sgfx')
100:           if [[ $ngpu -lt 2 ]]; then #We are temporarily reducing this down to 2 from 4 so that we can run tests on nodes with less gpus.
101:             echo "Error: only $ngpu GPU(s) detected, at least 2 GPUs are needed for distributed jobs"
102:             exit 1
103:           fi
104: 
105:       - name: Calculate docker image
106:         id: calculate-docker-image
107:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
108:         with:
109:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
110: 
111:       - name: Pull docker image
112:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
113:         with:
114:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
115: 
116:       - name: Get workflow job id
117:         id: get-job-id
118:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
119:         if: always()
120:         with:
121:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
122: 
123:       - name: Start monitoring script
124:         id: monitor-script
125:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
126:         shell: bash
127:         continue-on-error: true
128:         env:
129:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
130:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
131:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
132:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
133:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
134:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
135:         run: |
136:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7
137:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &
138:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
139: 
140:       - name: Download build artifacts
141:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
142:         with:
143:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
144: 
145:       - name: Download TD artifacts
146:         continue-on-error: true
147:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts
148: 
149:       - name: Parse ref
150:         id: parse-ref
151:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
152: 
153:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues
154:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently
155:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do
156:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.
157:         id: keep-going
158:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
159:         with:
160:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
161:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
162:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
163: 
164:       - name: Set Test step time
165:         id: test-timeout
166:         shell: bash
167:         env:
168:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
169:         run: |
170:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
171: 
172:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker
173:         shell: bash
174:         run: |
175:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
176:           env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
177: 
178:       - name: Test
179:         id: test
180:         env:
181:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
182:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
183:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
184:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
185:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}

187:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}

190:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
191:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}

193:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}
194:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}

207:           DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}
208:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
209:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
210:         run: |
211:           set -x
212: 
213:           if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then
214:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh

235:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
236:             -e JOB_ID \
237:             -e JOB_NAME \
238:             -e BASE_SHA \
239:             -e BRANCH \

242:             -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \
243:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \
244:             -e TEST_CONFIG \

246:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \

248:             -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \

254:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \

258:             --env-file="${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
259:             --ulimit stack=10485760:83886080 \
260:             --ulimit core=0 \

272:           )
273:           # save container name for later step
274:           echo "CONTAINER_NAME=${container_name}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
275:           # jenkins user does not have write permission to mounted workspace; work-around by copying within container to jenkins home
276:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "cd .. && cp -R workspace pytorch && cd pytorch && pip install dist/*.whl && ${TEST_COMMAND}"
277: 
278:       - name: Save test results

282:           docker exec -t "${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}" sh -c "cd ../pytorch && sudo cp -R test/test-reports ../workspace/test"

284:       - name: Change permissions (only needed for kubernetes runners for now)
285:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && (contains(matrix.runner, 'gfx942') || contains(matrix.runner, 'mi355')) }}
286:         run: |

304:       - name: Upload test artifacts

307:         with:

310: 
311:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)

314:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156

319:         if: failure()

322:           retention-days: 14

326:       - name: Authenticate with AWS
327:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
328:         with:
329:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results
330:           # The max duration enforced by the server side

334:       - name: Upload the benchmark results
335:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@main

338:           dry-run: false
339:           schema-version: v3
340:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
