342:       - name: Upload utilization stats
343:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}
344:         continue-on-error: true
345:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
346:         with:
347:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
348:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
349:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
350:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
351:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
352: 
353:       - name: Teardown ROCm
354:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-rocm
355: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: Check whether the workflow owner can use ARC runners
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       check_experiments:
7:         required: false
8:         type: string
9:         description: |
10:           List of experiments for this workflow. If not defined, all default experiments are included.
11:       opt_out_experiments:
12:         required: false
13:         type: string
14:         description: Comma-separated list of experiments this workflow will opt-out of.
15:       triggering_actor:
16:         required: true
17:         type: string
18:         description: The triggering_actor for the workflow. Use github.triggering_actor
19:       issue_owner:
20:         required: true
21:         type: string
22:         description: The owner of the issue. Use github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login
23:       curr_branch:
24:         required: true
25:         type: string
26:         description: Current branch or tag.
27:       curr_ref_type:
28:         required: false
29:         type: string
30:         default: branch
31:         description: The value of "github.ref_type", "branch" or "tag"
32:       issue_number:
33:         required: false
34:         type: string
35:         default: "5132"
36:         description: |
37:           Fetch's GitHub Issue from pytorch/test-infra
38:           Example: https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132
39: 
40:     outputs:
41:       label-type:
42:         description: Type of runners to use
43:         value: ${{ jobs.runner-determinator.outputs.label-type }}
44: 
45: jobs:
46:   runner-determinator:
47:     # Don't run on forked repos
48:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
49:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
50:     outputs:
51:       label-type: ${{ steps.set-condition.outputs.label-type }}
52:     env:
53:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
54:       ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.issue_number }}
55:       TRIGGERING_ACTOR: ${{ inputs.triggering_actor }}
56:       ISSUE_OWNER: ${{ inputs.issue_owner }}
57:       CHECK_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.check_experiments }}
58:       OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.opt_out_experiments }}
59:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
60:     steps:
61:       # - name: Checkout PyTorch
62:       #   uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
63:       #   with:
64:       #     fetch-depth: 1
65:       #     submodules: true
66: 
67:       # TODO: Remove the hardcoded step below
68:       # Hardcoding below is temporary for testing ALI runners
69:       # This file below should match the script found in .github/scripts/runner_determinator.py
70:       - name: Hardcode runner-determinator script
71:         id: hardcode-script
72:         run: |
73:           cat <<EOF > runner_determinator.py
74:           # flake8: noqa: G004
75: 
76:           # Note: Copies of this script in runner_determinator.py and _runner-determinator.yml
77:           #       must be kept in sync. You can do it easily by running the following command:
78:           #           python .github/scripts/update_runner_determinator.py
79: 
80:           """
81:           This runner determinator is used to determine which set of runners to run a
82:           GitHub job on. It uses the first comment of a GitHub issue (by default
83:           https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132) to define the configuration
84:           of which runners should be used to run which job.
85: 
86:           The configuration has two parts, the settings and a list of opted-in users,
87:           separated by a line containing "---".  If the line is not present, the
88:           settings are considered to be empty with only the second part, the user
89:           list, defined.
90: 
91:           The first part is a YAML block that defines the rollout settings. This can be
92:           used to define any settings that are needed to determine which runners to use.
93:           It's fields are defined by the RolloutSettings class below.
94: 
95:           The second part is a list of users who are explicitly opted in to the LF fleet.
96:           The user list is also a comma separated list of additional features or
97:           experiments which the user could be opted in to.
98: 
99:           The user list has the following rules:
100: 
101:           - Users are GitHub usernames, which must start with the @ prefix
102:           - Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable
103:           - A "#" prefix opts the user out of all experiments
104: 
105:           Example config:
106:               # A list of experiments that can be opted into.
107:               # This defines the behavior they'll induce when opted into.
108:               # Expected syntax is:
109:               #   [experiment_name]: # Name of the experiment. Also used for the label prefix.
110:               #      rollout_perc: [int] # % of workflows to run with this experiment when users are not opted in.
111: 
112:               experiments:
113:                 lf:
114:                   rollout_percent: 25
115:                   all_branches: false
116:                   default: true
117:               ---
118: 
119:               # Opt-ins:
120:               # Users can opt into the LF fleet by adding their GitHub username to this list
121:               # and specifying experiments to enable in a comma-separated list.
122:               # To always opt out of an experiment, prefix it with a "-".
123:               # Experiments should be from the above list.
124: 
125:               @User1,-lf,split_build
126:               @User2,lf
127:               @User3,split_build
128:           """
129: 
130:           import json
131:           import logging
132:           import os
133:           import random
134:           import re
135:           import sys
136:           from argparse import ArgumentParser
137:           from collections.abc import Iterable
138:           from functools import cache
139:           from logging import LogRecord
140:           from typing import Any, NamedTuple
141:           from urllib.request import Request, urlopen
142: 
143:           import yaml
144:           from github import Auth, Github
145:           from github.Issue import Issue
146: 
147: 
148:           DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX = ""  # use meta runners
149:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF = "lf."  # use runners from the linux foundation
150:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF_CANARY = "lf.c."  # use canary runners from the linux foundation
151: 
152:           GITHUB_OUTPUT = os.getenv("GITHUB_OUTPUT", "")
153:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_AMI = "runner-ami"
154:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE = "label-type"
155:           OPT_OUT_LABEL = "no-runner-experiments"
156: 
157:           SETTING_EXPERIMENTS = "experiments"
158: 
159:           LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT = "lf"
160:           CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX = ".c"
161: 
162: 
163:           class Experiment(NamedTuple):
164:               rollout_perc: float = (
165:                   0  # Percentage of workflows to experiment on when user is not opted-in.
166:               )
167:               all_branches: bool = (
168:                   False  # If True, the experiment is also enabled on the exception branches
169:               )
170:               default: bool = (
171:                   True  # If True, the experiment is enabled by default for all queries
172:               )
173: 
174:               # Add more fields as needed
175: 
176: 
177:           class Settings(NamedTuple):
178:               """
179:               Settings for the experiments that can be opted into.
180:               """
181: 
182:               experiments: dict[str, Experiment] = {}
183: 
184: 
185:           class ColorFormatter(logging.Formatter):
186:               """Color codes the log messages based on the log level"""
187: 
188:               COLORS = {
189:                   "WARNING": "\033[33m",  # Yellow
190:                   "ERROR": "\033[31m",  # Red
191:                   "CRITICAL": "\033[31m",  # Red
192:                   "INFO": "\033[0m",  # Reset
193:                   "DEBUG": "\033[0m",  # Reset
194:               }
195: 
196:               def format(self, record: LogRecord) -> str:
197:                   log_color = self.COLORS.get(record.levelname, "\033[0m")  # Default to reset
198:                   record.msg = f"{log_color}{record.msg}\033[0m"
199:                   return super().format(record)
200: 
201: 
202:           handler = logging.StreamHandler()
203:           handler.setFormatter(ColorFormatter(fmt="%(levelname)-8s: %(message)s"))
204: 
205:           log = logging.getLogger(os.path.basename(__file__))
206:           log.addHandler(handler)
207:           log.setLevel(logging.INFO)
208: 
209: 
210:           def set_github_output(key: str, value: str) -> None:
211:               """
212:               Defines outputs of the github action that invokes this script
213:               """
214:               if not GITHUB_OUTPUT:
215:                   # See https://github.blog/changelog/2022-10-11-github-actions-deprecating-save-state-and-set-output-commands/ for deprecation notice
216:                   log.warning(
217:                       "No env var found for GITHUB_OUTPUT, you must be running this code locally. Falling back to the deprecated print method."
218:                   )
219:                   print(f"::set-output name={key}::{value}")
220:                   return
221: 
222:               with open(GITHUB_OUTPUT, "a") as f:
223:                   log.info(f"Setting output: {key}='{value}'")
224:                   f.write(f"{key}={value}\n")
225: 
226: 
227:           def _str_comma_separated_to_set(value: str) -> frozenset[str]:
228:               return frozenset(
229:                   filter(lambda itm: itm != "", map(str.strip, value.strip(" \n\t").split(",")))
230:               )
231: 
232: 
233:           def parse_args() -> Any:
234:               parser = ArgumentParser("Get dynamic rollout settings")
235:               parser.add_argument("--github-token", type=str, required=True, help="GitHub token")
236:               parser.add_argument(
237:                   "--github-issue-repo",
238:                   type=str,
239:                   required=False,
240:                   default="pytorch/test-infra",
241:                   help="GitHub repo to get the issue",
242:               )
243:               parser.add_argument(
244:                   "--github-repo",
245:                   type=str,
246:                   required=True,
247:                   help="GitHub repo where CI is running",
248:               )
249:               parser.add_argument(
250:                   "--github-issue", type=int, required=True, help="GitHub issue number"
251:               )
252:               parser.add_argument(
253:                   "--github-actor", type=str, required=True, help="GitHub triggering_actor"
254:               )
255:               parser.add_argument(
256:                   "--github-issue-owner", type=str, required=True, help="GitHub issue owner"
257:               )
258:               parser.add_argument(
259:                   "--github-branch", type=str, required=True, help="Current GitHub branch or tag"
260:               )
261:               parser.add_argument(
262:                   "--github-ref-type",
263:                   type=str,
264:                   required=True,
265:                   help="Current GitHub ref type, branch or tag",
266:               )
267:               parser.add_argument(
268:                   "--eligible-experiments",
269:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,
270:                   required=False,
271:                   default="",
272:                   help="comma separated list of experiments to check, if omitted all experiments marked with default=True are checked",
273:               )
274:               parser.add_argument(
275:                   "--opt-out-experiments",
276:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,
277:                   required=False,
278:                   default="",
279:                   help=(
280:                       "comma separated list of experiments to opt-out of. If unset, no opt-outs will occur. "
281:                       "If the same experiment is listed both here and in '--eligible-experiments' opt-out will take priority."
282:                   ),
283:               )
284:               parser.add_argument(
285:                   "--pr-number",
286:                   type=str,
287:                   required=False,
288:                   default="",
289:                   help="the optional PR number where this is run",
290:               )
291: 
292:               return parser.parse_args()
293: 
294: 
295:           def get_gh_client(github_token: str) -> Github:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]
296:               auth = Auth.Token(github_token)
297:               return Github(auth=auth)
298: 
299: 
300:           def get_issue(gh: Github, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> Issue:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]
301:               repo = gh.get_repo(repo)
302:               return repo.get_issue(number=issue_num)
303: 
304: 
305:           def get_potential_pr_author(
306:               github_token: str, repo: str, username: str, ref_type: str, ref_name: str
307:           ) -> str:
308:               # If the trigger was a new tag added by a bot, this is a ciflow case
309:               # Fetch the actual username from the original PR. The PR number is
310:               # embedded in the tag name: ciflow/<name>/<pr-number>
311: 
312:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)
313: 
314:               if username == "pytorch-bot[bot]" and ref_type == "tag":
315:                   split_tag = ref_name.split("/")
316:                   if (
317:                       len(split_tag) == 3
318:                       and split_tag[0] == "ciflow"
319:                       and split_tag[2].isnumeric()
320:                   ):
321:                       pr_number = split_tag[2]
322:                       try:
323:                           repository = gh.get_repo(repo)
324:                           pull = repository.get_pull(number=int(pr_number))
325:                       except Exception as e:
326:                           raise Exception(  # noqa: TRY002
327:                               f"issue with pull request {pr_number} from repo {repository}"
328:                           ) from e
329:                       return pull.user.login  # type: ignore[no-any-return]
330:               # In all other cases, return the original input username
331:               return username
332: 
333: 
334:           def is_exception_branch(branch: str) -> bool:
335:               """
336:               Branches that get opted out of experiments by default, until they're explicitly enabled.
337:               """
338:               return branch.split("/")[0] in {"main", "nightly", "release", "landchecks"}
339: 
340: 
341:           def load_yaml(yaml_text: str) -> Any:
342:               try:
343:                   data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_text)
344:                   return data
345:               except yaml.YAMLError:
346:                   log.exception("Error loading YAML")
347:                   raise
348: 
349: 
350:           def extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
351:               """
352:               Extracts the text with settings, if any, and the opted in users from the rollout state.
353: 
354:               If the issue body contains "---" then the text above that is the settings
355:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.
356: 
357:               If it doesn't contain "---" then the settings are empty and the rest is the users.
358:               """
359:               rollout_state_parts = rollout_state.split("---")
360:               if len(rollout_state_parts) >= 2:
361:                   return rollout_state_parts[0], rollout_state_parts[1]
362:               else:
363:                   return "", rollout_state
364: 
365: 
366:           class UserOptins(dict[str, list[str]]):
367:               """
368:               Dictionary of users with a list of features they have opted into
369:               """
370: 
371: 
372:           def parse_user_opt_in_from_text(user_optin_text: str) -> UserOptins:
373:               """
374:               Parse the user opt-in text into a key value pair of username and the list of features they have opted into
375: 
376:               Users are GitHub usernames with the @ prefix. Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable.
377:                   - Example line: "@User1,lf,split_build"
378:                   - A "#" prefix indicates the user is opted out of all experiments
379: 
380: 
381:               """
382:               optins = UserOptins()
383:               for user in user_optin_text.split("\n"):
384:                   user = user.strip("\r\n\t -")
385:                   if not user or not user.startswith("@"):
386:                       # Not a valid user. Skip
387:                       continue
388: 
389:                   if user:
390:                       usr_name = user.split(",")[0].strip("@")
391:                       optins[usr_name] = [exp.strip(" ") for exp in user.split(",")[1:]]
392: 
393:               return optins
394: 
395: 
396:           def is_valid_experiment_name(experiment_name: str) -> bool:
397:               """
398:               Check if the experiment name is valid.
399:               A valid name:
400:                   - Contains only alphanumeric characters and the special characters "_" & "-"
401:                   - The special characters "_" & "-" shouldn't be the first or last characters
402:                   - Cannot contain spaces
403:               """
404: 
405:               valid_char_regex = r"^[a-zA-Z0-9]([\w-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?$"
406:               valid = bool(re.match(valid_char_regex, experiment_name))
407: 
408:               if valid:
409:                   return True
410: 
411:               log.error(
412:                   f"Invalid experiment name: {experiment_name}. Experiment names should only contain alphanumeric characters, '_', and '-'. They cannot contain spaces, and the special characters '_' and '-' cannot be the first or last characters."
413:               )
414:               return False
415: 
416: 
417:           def parse_settings_from_text(settings_text: str) -> Settings:
418:               """
419:               Parse the experiments from the issue body into a list of ExperimentSettings
420:               """
421:               try:
422:                   if settings_text:
423:                       # Escape the backtick as well so that we can have the settings in a code block on the GH issue
424:                       # for easy reading
425:                       # Note: Using ascii for the backtick so that the cat step in _runner-determinator.yml doesn't choke on
426:                       #       the backtick character in shell commands.
427:                       backtick = chr(96)  # backtick character
428:                       settings_text = settings_text.strip(f"\r\n\t{backtick} ")
429:                       settings = load_yaml(settings_text)
430: 
431:                       # For now we just load experiments. We can expand this if/when we add more settings
432:                       experiments = {}
433: 
434:                       for exp_name, exp_settings in settings.get(SETTING_EXPERIMENTS).items():
435:                           if not is_valid_experiment_name(exp_name):
436:                               # Exclude invalid experiments from the list. We log an error, but don't raise an exception so that other experiments can still be processed.
437:                               continue
438: 
439:                           valid_settings = {}
440:                           for setting in exp_settings:
441:                               if setting not in Experiment._fields:
442:                                   log.warning(
443:                                       f"Unexpected setting in experiment: {setting} = {exp_settings[setting]}"
444:                                   )
445:                               else:
446:                                   valid_settings[setting] = exp_settings[setting]
447: 
448:                           experiments[exp_name] = Experiment(**valid_settings)
449:                       return Settings(experiments)
450: 
451:               except Exception:
452:                   log.exception("Failed to parse settings")
453: 
454:               return Settings()
455: 
456: 
457:           def parse_settings(rollout_state: str) -> Settings:
458:               """
459:               Parse settings, if any, from the rollout state.
460: 
461:               If the issue body contains "---" then the text above that is the settings
462:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.
463: 
464:               If it doesn't contain "---" then the settings are empty and the default values are used.
465:               """
466:               settings_text, _ = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)
467:               return parse_settings_from_text(settings_text)
468: 
469: 
470:           def parse_users(rollout_state: str) -> UserOptins:
471:               """
472:               Parse users from the rollout state.
473: 
474:               """
475:               _, users_text = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)
476:               return parse_user_opt_in_from_text(users_text)
477: 
478: 
479:           def is_user_opted_in(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:
480:               """
481:               Check if a user is opted into an experiment
482:               """
483:               return experiment_name in user_optins.get(user, [])
484: 
485: 
486:           def is_user_opted_out(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:
487:               """
488:               Check if a user explicitly opted out of an experiment
489:               """
490:               # if the experiment is prefixed with a "-", then it's an opt-out
491:               experiment_optout = "-" + experiment_name
492:               if experiment_optout not in user_optins.get(user, []):
493:                   return False
494: 
495:               if is_user_opted_in(user, user_optins, experiment_name):
496:                   log.warning(
497:                       f"User {user} is opted into experiment {experiment_name}, but also opted out of it. Defaulting to opting out"
498:                   )
499: 
500:               return True
501: 
502: 
503:           def get_runner_prefix(
504:               rollout_state: str,
505:               workflow_requestors: Iterable[str],
506:               branch: str,
507:               eligible_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),
508:               opt_out_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),
509:               is_canary: bool = False,
510:           ) -> str:
511:               settings = parse_settings(rollout_state)
512:               user_optins = parse_users(rollout_state)
513: 
514:               fleet_prefix = ""
515:               prefixes = []
516:               for experiment_name, experiment_settings in settings.experiments.items():
517:                   if not experiment_settings.all_branches and is_exception_branch(branch):
518:                       log.info(
519:                           f"Branch {branch} is an exception branch. Not enabling experiment {experiment_name}."
520:                       )
521:                       continue
522: 
523:                   if opt_out_experiments:
524:                       if experiment_name in opt_out_experiments:
525:                           opt_out_exp_list = ", ".join(opt_out_experiments)
526:                           log.info(
527:                               f"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as this workflow has opted-out (opted out experiments are: {opt_out_exp_list})"
528:                           )
529:                           continue
530: 
531:                   if eligible_experiments:
532:                       if experiment_name not in eligible_experiments:
533:                           exp_list = ", ".join(eligible_experiments)
534:                           log.info(
535:                               f"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not in the eligible_experiments list: {exp_list}"
536:                           )
537:                           continue
538:                   elif not experiment_settings.default:
539:                       log.info(
540:                           f"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not a default experiment"
541:                       )
542:                       continue
543: 
544:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted out to this experiment?
545:                   opted_out_users = [
546:                       requestor
547:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors
548:                       if is_user_opted_out(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)
549:                   ]
550: 
551:                   if opted_out_users:
552:                       log.info(
553:                           f"{', '.join(opted_out_users)} have opted out of experiment {experiment_name}."
554:                       )
555:                       continue
556: 
557:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted in to this experiment?
558:                   opted_in_users = [
559:                       requestor
560:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors
561:                       if is_user_opted_in(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)
562:                   ]
563: 
564:                   enabled = False
565:                   if opted_in_users:
566:                       log.info(
567:                           f"{', '.join(opted_in_users)} have opted into experiment {experiment_name}."
568:                       )
569:                       enabled = True
570: 
571:                   elif experiment_settings.rollout_perc:
572:                       # If no user is opted in, then we randomly enable the experiment based on the rollout percentage
573:                       if random.uniform(0, 100) <= experiment_settings.rollout_perc:
574:                           log.info(
575:                               f"Based on rollout percentage of {experiment_settings.rollout_perc}%, enabling experiment {experiment_name}."
576:                           )
577:                           enabled = True
578: 
579:                   if enabled:
580:                       label = experiment_name
581:                       if experiment_name == LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT:
582:                           # We give some special treatment to the "lf" experiment since determines the fleet we use
583:                           #  - If it's enabled, then we always list it's prefix first
584:                           #  - If we're in the canary branch, then we append ".c" to the lf prefix
585:                           if is_canary:
586:                               label += CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX
587:                           fleet_prefix = label
588:                       else:
589:                           prefixes.append(label)
590: 
591:               if len(prefixes) > 1:
592:                   log.error(
593:                       f"Only a fleet and one other experiment can be enabled for a job at any time. Enabling {prefixes[0]} and ignoring the rest, which are {', '.join(prefixes[1:])}"
594:                   )
595:                   prefixes = prefixes[:1]
596: 
597:               # Fleet always comes first
598:               if fleet_prefix:
599:                   prefixes.insert(0, fleet_prefix)
600: 
601:               return ".".join(prefixes) + "." if prefixes else ""
602: 
603: 
604:           def get_rollout_state_from_issue(github_token: str, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> str:
605:               """
606:               Gets the first comment of the issue, which contains the desired rollout state.
607: 
608:               The default issue we use - https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132
609:               """
610:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)
611:               issue = get_issue(gh, repo, issue_num)
612:               return str(issue.get_comments()[0].body.strip("\n\t "))
613: 
614: 
615:           def download_json(url: str, headers: dict[str, str], num_retries: int = 3) -> Any:
616:               for _ in range(num_retries):
617:                   try:
618:                       req = Request(url=url, headers=headers)
619:                       content = urlopen(req, timeout=5).read().decode("utf-8")
620:                       return json.loads(content)
621:                   except Exception as e:
622:                       log.warning(f"Could not download {url}: {e}")
623: 
624:               log.warning(f"All {num_retries} retries exhausted, downloading {url} failed")
625:               return {}
626: 
627: 
628:           @cache
629:           def get_pr_info(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
630:               """
631:               Dynamically get PR information
632:               """
633:               github_api = f"https://api.github.com/repos/{github_repo}"
634:               headers = {
635:                   "Accept": "application/vnd.github.v3+json",
636:                   "Authorization": f"token {github_token}",
637:               }
638:               json_response: dict[str, Any] = download_json(
639:                   url=f"{github_api}/issues/{pr_number}",
640:                   headers=headers,
641:               )
642: 
643:               if not json_response:
644:                   log.warning(f"Failed to get the labels for #{pr_number}")
645:                   return {}
646: 
647:               return json_response
648: 
649: 
650:           def get_labels(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> set[str]:
651:               """
652:               Dynamically get the latest list of labels from the pull request
653:               """
654:               pr_info = get_pr_info(github_repo, github_token, pr_number)
655:               return {
656:                   label.get("name") for label in pr_info.get("labels", []) if label.get("name")
657:               }
658: 
659: 
660:           def main() -> None:
661:               args = parse_args()
662: 
663:               runner_label_prefix = DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX
664: 
665:               # Check if the PR is opt-out
666:               if args.pr_number:
667:                   labels = get_labels(args.github_repo, args.github_token, int(args.pr_number))
668:                   if OPT_OUT_LABEL in labels:
669:                       log.info(
670:                           f"Opt-out runner determinator because #{args.pr_number} has {OPT_OUT_LABEL} label"
671:                       )
672:                       set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)
673:                       sys.exit()
674: 
675:               try:
676:                   rollout_state = get_rollout_state_from_issue(
677:                       args.github_token, args.github_issue_repo, args.github_issue
678:                   )
679: 
680:                   username = get_potential_pr_author(
681:                       args.github_token,
682:                       args.github_repo,
683:                       args.github_actor,
684:                       args.github_ref_type,
685:                       args.github_branch,
686:                   )
687: 
688:                   is_canary = args.github_repo == "pytorch/pytorch-canary"
689: 
690:                   runner_label_prefix = get_runner_prefix(
691:                       rollout_state,
692:                       (args.github_issue_owner, username),
693:                       args.github_branch,
694:                       args.eligible_experiments,
695:                       args.opt_out_experiments,
696:                       is_canary,
697:                   )
698: 
699:               except Exception as e:
700:                   log.error(
701:                       f"Failed to get issue. Defaulting to Meta runners and no experiments. Exception: {e}"
702:                   )
703: 
704:               set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)
705: 
706: 
707:           if __name__ == "__main__":
708:               main()
709: 
710:           EOF
711: 
712:           cat runner_determinator.py
713: 
714:       - name: Install dependencies
715:         run: python3 -m pip install urllib3==1.26.18 PyGithub==2.3.0
716: 
717:       - name: Get the workflow type for the current user
718:         id: set-condition
719:         run: |
720:           curr_branch="${{ inputs.curr_branch }}"
721:           curr_ref_type="${{ inputs.curr_ref_type }}"
722:           echo "Current branch is '$curr_branch'"
723: 
724:           python3 runner_determinator.py \
725:             --github-token "$GITHUB_TOKEN" \
726:             --github-issue "$ISSUE_NUMBER" \
727:             --github-branch "$curr_branch" \
728:             --github-actor "$TRIGGERING_ACTOR" \
729:             --github-issue-owner "$ISSUE_OWNER" \
730:             --github-ref-type "$curr_ref_type" \
731:             --github-repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" \
732:             --eligible-experiments "$CHECK_EXPERIMENTS" \
733:             --opt-out-experiments "$OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS" \
734:             --pr-number "${PR_NUMBER}"
735: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml"}
