{"content":"1: name: bazel
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       docker-image-name:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Name of the base docker image to build with.
14:       cuda-version:
15:         required: true
16:         type: string
17:         description: What CUDA version to build with (i.e. "11.7"), "cpu" for none.
18:       sync-tag:
19:         required: false
20:         type: string
21:         default: ""
22:         description: |
23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
25:       test-matrix:
26:         required: true
27:         type: string
28:         description: |
29:           A JSON description of what configs to run later on.
30:       runner:
31:         required: false
32:         type: string
33:         default: "linux.large"
34:         description: Runner type
35: 
36: env:
37:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
38: 
39: jobs:
40:   filter:
41:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
42:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner }}
43:     outputs:
44:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}
45:       is-test-matrix-empty: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty }}
46:       keep-going: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.keep-going }}
47:       reenabled-issues: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.reenabled-issues }}
48:     steps:
49:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
50:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
51:         with:
52:           fetch-depth: 1
53:           submodules: false
54: 
55:       - name: Select all requested test configurations
56:         id: filter
57:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
58:         with:
59:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
60:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
61: 
62:   build-and-test:
63:     needs: filter
64:     # Don't run on forked repos.
65:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && needs.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False'
66:     strategy:
67:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(needs.filter.outputs.test-matrix) }}
68:       fail-fast: false
69:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
70:     steps:
71:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
72:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
73:         with:
74:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
75: 
76:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
77:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
78:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
79: 
80:       - name: Setup Linux
81:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
82: 
83:       - name: Calculate docker image
84:         id: calculate-docker-image
85:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
86:         with:
87:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}
88: 
89:       - name: Pull docker image
90:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
91:         with:
92:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
93: 
94:       - name: Check if in a container runner
95:         shell: bash
96:         id: check_container_runner
97:         run: echo "IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
98: 
99:       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG
100:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main
101: 
102:       - name: Output disk space left
103:         run: |
104:           sudo df -H
105: 
106:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker
107:         run: |
108:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
109:           env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
110: 
111:       - name: Parse ref
112:         id: parse-ref
113:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
114: 
115:       - name: Get workflow job id
116:         id: get-job-id
117:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
118:         if: always()
119:         with:
120:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
121: 
122:       - name: Build
123:         env:
124:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
125:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
126:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
127:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
128:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
129:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
130:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
131:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
132:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
133:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
134:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ needs.filter.outputs.reenabled-issues }}
135:           # TODO duplicated
136:           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
137:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
138:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2
139:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1
140:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: 5.2
141:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
142:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
143:           CUDA_VERSION: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}
144:         run: |
145:           export SHARD_NUMBER=0
146:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
147:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase
148:           # Make sure we copy test results from bazel-testlogs symlink to
149:           # a regular directory ./test/test-reports
150:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086
151:           container_name=$(docker run \
152:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \
153:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \
154:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
155:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
156:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \
157:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \
158:             -e GITHUB_JOB \
159:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \
160:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
161:             -e JOB_ID \
162:             -e GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH="$GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH" \
163:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \
164:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \
165:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
166:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \
167:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \
168:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \
169:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \
170:             -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \
171:             -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \
172:             -e CUDA_VERSION \
173:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
174:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
175:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
176:             --shm-size="1g" \
177:             --tty \
178:             --detach \
179:             --user jenkins \
180:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
181:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
182:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
183:           )
184:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'
185:           echo "container_id=${container_name}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
186: 
187:       - name: Test
188:         id: test
189:         # Time out the test phase after 3.5 hours
190:         timeout-minutes: 120
191:         run: |
192:           docker exec -t "${container_id}" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/test.sh && cp -Lr ./bazel-testlogs ./test/test-reports'
193: 
194:       - name: Print remaining test logs
195:         shell: bash
196:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion
197:         run: |
198:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true
199: 
200:       - name: Chown workspace
201:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace
202:         if: always()
203: 
204:       - name: Upload test artifacts
205:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts
206:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'
207:         with:
208:           file-suffix: bazel-${{ github.job }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
209: 
210:       - name: Teardown Linux
211:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
212:         if: always()
213: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: linux-binary-build
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build_name:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: The build's name
10:       build_environment:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: The build environment
14:       runner_prefix:
15:         required: false
16:         default: ""
17:         type: string
18:         description: prefix for runner label
19:       runs_on:
20:         required: false
21:         default: linux.12xlarge.memory.ephemeral
22:         type: string
23:         description: Hardware to run this "build" job on, linux.12xlarge or linux.arm64.2xlarge.
24:       timeout-minutes:
25:         required: false
26:         default: 240
27:         type: number
28:         description: timeout for the job
29:       ALPINE_IMAGE:
30:         required: false
31:         type: string
32:         default: "308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine"
33:         description: Alpine image to use
34:       PYTORCH_ROOT:
35:         required: true
36:         type: string
37:         description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository
38:       PACKAGE_TYPE:
39:         required: true
40:         type: string
41:         description: Package type
42:       DESIRED_CUDA:
43:         required: true
44:         type: string
45:         description: Desired Cuda version
46:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION:
47:         required: false
48:         type: string
49:         description: GPU Arch version
50:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE:
51:         required: true
52:         type: string
53:         description: GPU Arch type
54:       DOCKER_IMAGE:
55:         required: true
56:         type: string
57:         description: Docker image to use
58:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:
59:         required: true
60:         type: string
61:         description: Docker image tag to use
62:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG:
63:         required: false
64:         type: string
65:         description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)
66:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT:
67:         required: false

87: jobs:
88:   build:
89:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runs_on }}
90:     timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}
91:     env:
92:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_ROOT }}
93:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}
94:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in
95:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION
96:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}
97:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}
98:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}
99:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
100:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1
101:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}
102:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}
103:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}
104:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}
105:       ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
106:       AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
107:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env
108:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build_environment }}
109:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
110:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
111:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts
112:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
113:     steps:
114:       - name: Make the env permanent during this workflow (but not the secrets)
115:         shell: bash
116:         run: |
117:           {
118:             echo "PYTORCH_ROOT=${{ env.PYTORCH_ROOT }}"
119:             echo "PACKAGE_TYPE=${{ env.PACKAGE_TYPE }}"
120:             echo "DESIRED_CUDA=${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}"
121:             echo "GPU_ARCH_VERSION=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}"
122:             echo "GPU_ARCH_TYPE=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}"
123:             echo "DOCKER_IMAGE=${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}"
124:             echo "SKIP_ALL_TESTS=${{ env.SKIP_ALL_TESTS }}"
125:             echo "LIBTORCH_CONFIG=${{ env.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}"
126:             echo "LIBTORCH_VARIANT=${{ env.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}"
127:             echo "DESIRED_PYTHON=${{ env.DESIRED_PYTHON }}"
128:             echo "PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS=${{ env.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}"
129:             echo "ALPINE_IMAGE=${{ env.ALPINE_IMAGE }}"
130:             echo "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${{ env.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION }}"
131:             echo "BINARY_ENV_FILE=${{ env.BINARY_ENV_FILE }}"
132:             echo "BUILD_ENVIRONMENT=${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}"
133:             echo "BUILD_NAME=${{ env.BUILD_NAME }}"
134:             echo "PR_NUMBER=${{ env.PR_NUMBER }}"
135:             echo "PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}"
136:             echo "SHA1=${{ env.SHA1 }}"
137:           } >> "${GITHUB_ENV} }}"
138: 
139:       - name: List the env
140:         shell: bash
141:         run: env
142: 
143:       - name: "[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)"
144:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
145:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
146:         continue-on-error: true
147:         with:
148:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
149: 
150:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
151:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
152:         with:
153:           no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
154: 
155:       - name: Setup Linux
156:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
157:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
158: 
159:       - name: Chown workspace
160:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
161:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace
162:         with:
163:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
164: 
165:       - name: Clean workspace
166:         shell: bash
167:         run: |
168:           set -eux
169: 
170:           rm -rf "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
171:           mkdir "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
172: 
173:           if [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' ]] || [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' ]] ; then
174:             rm -rf "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts"
175:             mkdir "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts"
176:           fi
177: 
178:       - name: Checkout PyTorch to pytorch dir
179:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
180:         with:
181:           ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
182:           submodules: recursive
183:           path: pytorch
184:           show-progress: false
185: 
186:       - name: Clean PyTorch checkout
187:         run: |
188:           # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts
189:           git clean -fxd
190:         working-directory: pytorch
191: 
192:       - name: Check if the job is disabled
193:         id: filter
194:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs
195:         with:
196:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
197:           # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the
198:           # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled
199:           test-matrix: |
200:             { include: [
201:               { config: "default" },
202:             ]}
203: 
204:       - name: configure aws credentials
205:         id: aws_creds
206:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}
207:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
208:         with:
209:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only
210:           aws-region: us-east-1
211:           role-duration-seconds: 18000
212: 
213:       - name: Calculate docker image
214:         id: calculate-docker-image
215:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
216:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
217:         with:
218:           # If doing this in main or release branch, use docker.io. Otherwise
219:           # use ECR
220:           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}
221:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
222:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}
223:           # The build.sh script in this folder is not actually the correct one,
224:           # this is just needed for sha calculation
225:           docker-build-dir: .ci/docker
226:           working-directory: pytorch
227: 
228:       - name: Pull Docker image
229:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
230:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
231:         with:
232:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
233: 
234:       - name: Build PyTorch binary
235:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
236:         env:
237:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image || format('{0}:{1}', inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE, inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX) }}
238:         run: |
239:           set -x
240:           mkdir -p artifacts/
241:           container_name=$(docker run \
242:             -e BINARY_ENV_FILE \
243:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
244:             -e DESIRED_CUDA \
245:             -e DESIRED_PYTHON \
246:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
247:             -e GPU_ARCH_TYPE \
248:             -e GPU_ARCH_VERSION \
249:             -e LIBTORCH_VARIANT \
250:             -e PACKAGE_TYPE \
251:             -e PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR \
252:             -e PYTORCH_ROOT \
253:             -e SKIP_ALL_TESTS \
254:             -e PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS \
255:             --tty \
256:             --detach \
257:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/pytorch:/pytorch" \
258:             -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/artifacts" \
259:             -w / \
260:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
261:           )
262:           docker exec -t -w "${PYTORCH_ROOT}" "${container_name}" bash -c "bash .circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh"
263:           # Unified build script for all architectures (x86_64, aarch64, s390x)
264:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" bash -c "source ${BINARY_ENV_FILE} && bash /pytorch/.ci/${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}/build.sh"
265: 
266:       - name: Chown artifacts
267:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
268:         shell: bash
269:         run: |
270:           # Ensure the working directory gets chowned back to the current user
271:           docker run --rm -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/v" -w /v "${ALPINE_IMAGE}" chown -R "$(id -u):$(id -g)" .
272: 
273:       - uses: actions/upload-artifact@50769540e7f4bd5e21e526ee35c689e35e0d6874 # v4.4.0
274:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
275:         with:
276:           name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}
277:           if-no-files-found: error
278:           path:
279:             ${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/*
280: 
281:       - name: Teardown Linux
282:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
283:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
284: 
285:       - name: Chown workspace
286:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
287:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/chown-workspace
288:         with:
289:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
290: 
291:       - name: Cleanup docker
292:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
293:         shell: bash
294:         run: |
295:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop
296:           # ignore expansion of "docker ps -q" since it could be empty
297:           # shellcheck disable=SC2046
298:           docker stop $(docker ps -q) || true

51:         required: false
52:         type: string
53:         description: Desired python version
54:     secrets:
55:       github-token:
56:         required: true
57:         description: Github Token
58: 
59: jobs:
60:   upload:
61:     runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
62:     container:
63:       image: continuumio/miniconda3:4.12.0
64:     env:
65:       PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch
66:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}
67:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in
68:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION
69:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}
70:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}
71:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}
72:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
73:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1
74:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}
75:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}
76:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}
77:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env
78:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
79:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
80:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts
81:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
82:     steps:
83:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
84:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
85:         with:
86:           no-sudo: true
87: 
88:       - name: Configure AWS credentials(PyTorch account) for nightly
89:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.event.ref == 'refs/heads/nightly' }}
90:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
91:         with:
92:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::749337293305:role/gha_workflow_nightly_build_wheels
93:           aws-region: us-east-1
94: 
95:       - name: Configure AWS credentials(PyTorch account) for RC builds
96:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' &&  (startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/')) }}
97:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
98:         with:
99:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::749337293305:role/gha_workflow_test_build_wheels
100:           aws-region: us-east-1
101: 
102:       - name: Download Build Artifacts
103:         id: download-artifacts
104:         # NB: When the previous build job is skipped, there won't be any artifacts and
105:         # this step will fail. Binary build jobs can only be skipped on CI, not nightly
106:         continue-on-error: true
107:         uses: actions/download-artifact@65a9edc5881444af0b9093a5e628f2fe47ea3b2e # v4.1.7
108:         with:
109:           name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}
110:           path: "${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/"
111: 
112:       - name: Set DRY_RUN (only for tagged pushes)
113:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.event.ref == 'refs/heads/nightly' || (startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/'))) }}
114:         run: |
115:           echo "DRY_RUN=disabled" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
116: 
117:       - name: Set UPLOAD_CHANNEL (only for tagged pushes)
118:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}
119:         shell: bash -e -l {0}
120:         run: |
121:           # reference ends with an RC suffix
122:           if [[ "${GITHUB_REF_NAME}" = *-rc[0-9]* ]]; then
123:             echo "UPLOAD_CHANNEL=test" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
124:           fi
125: 
126:       - name: Upload binaries
127:         if: steps.download-artifacts.outcome && steps.download-artifacts.outcome == 'success'
128:         shell: bash
129:         env:
130:           PKG_DIR: "${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts"
131:           UPLOAD_SUBFOLDER: "${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}"
132:           BUILD_NAME: ${{ inputs.build_name }}
133:         run: |
134:             set -ex
135:             bash .circleci/scripts/binary_upload.sh

{"content":"1: name: build docs
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       docker-image:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Docker image to run in.
14:       push:
15:         required: false
16:         type: boolean
17:         default: false
18:         description: If set, push the docs to the docs website.
19:       run-doxygen:
20:         required: false
21:         type: boolean
22:         default: false
23:         description: If set, will enable C++ API doc generation using doxygen / breathe / exhale.
24:       sync-tag:
25:         required: false
26:         type: string
27:         default: ""
28:         description: |
29:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
30:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
31:       s3-bucket:
32:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
33:         required: false
34:         type: string
35:         default: "gha-artifacts"
36:       aws-role-to-assume:
37:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts
38:         required: false
39:         type: string
40:         default: ""
41:       upload-aws-role-to-assume:
42:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts
43:         required: false
44:         type: string
45:         default: ""
46:       runner_prefix:
47:         description: prefix for runner label
48:         type: string
49:         default: ""
50:     secrets:
51:       GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN:
52:         required: false
53:         description: Permissions for pushing to the docs site.
54: 
55: jobs:
56:   build-docs:
57:     # Don't run on forked repos.
58:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
59:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
60:     environment: ${{ (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'pytorchbot-env' || '' }}
61:     strategy:
62:       fail-fast: false
63:       matrix:
64:         include:
65:           - docs_type: cpp
66:             # We recently seeing lots of exit code 137 running this in Docker indicating
67:             # an OOM issue when running the job, so this upgrades the runner from 4xlarge
68:             # to the next available tier of 12xlarge. So much memory just to generate cpp
69:             # doc
70:             runner: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix }}linux.12xlarge.memory
71:             # TODO: Nightly cpp docs take longer and longer to finish (more than 3h now)
72:             # Let's try to figure out how this can be improved
73:             timeout-minutes: 360
74:           - docs_type: python
75:             runner: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix }}linux.c7i.2xlarge
76:             # It takes less than 30m to finish python docs unless there are issues
77:             timeout-minutes: 30
78:     # Set a fixed name for this job instead of using the current matrix-generated name, i.e. build-docs (cpp, linux.12xlarge, 180)
79:     # The current name requires updating the database last docs push query from test-infra every time the matrix is updated
80:     name: build-docs-${{ matrix.docs_type }}-${{ inputs.push }}
81:     steps:
82:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
83:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
84:         with:
85:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
86:           instructions: |
87:             All builds are done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:
88:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash
89:             To start Python docs build type:
90:               cd docs && make html && make coverage
91: 
92:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
93:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
94:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
95: 
96:       - name: Setup Linux
97:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
98: 
99:       - name: configure aws credentials
100:         if : ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' }}
101:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
102:         with:
103:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}
104:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test
105:           aws-region: us-east-1
106: 
107:       - name: Calculate docker image
108:         id: calculate-docker-image
109:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
110:         with:
111:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
112: 
113:       - name: Pull docker image
114:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
115:         with:
116:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
117: 
118:       - name: Download build artifacts
119:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
120:         with:
121:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
122:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
123: 
124:       - name: Generate netrc (only for docs-push)
125:         if: inputs.push
126:         env:
127:           GITHUB_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN }}
128:         run: |
129:           # sometimes .netrc exists as a directory even though this is the temp folder
130:           rm -rf "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc"
131:           # set credentials for https pushing
132:           echo "machine github.com" > "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc"
133:           echo "login pytorchbot" >> "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc"
134:           echo "password ${GITHUB_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN}" >> "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc"
135: 
136:       - name: Build ${{ matrix.docs_type }} docs
137:         timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.timeout-minutes }}
138:         id: build-docs
139:         env:
140:           # After https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/88373, pull workflow can now be run periodically,
141:           # so using a schedule event to determine if the docs should be pushed or not doesn't hold true
142:           # anymore
143:           WITH_PUSH: ${{ inputs.push }}
144:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
145:           DOCS_TYPE: ${{ matrix.docs_type }}
146:           RUN_DOXYGEN: ${{ inputs.run-doxygen }}
147:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
148:         run: |
149:           set -ex
150:           # Convert refs/tags/v1.12.0rc3 into 1.12
151:           if [[ "${GITHUB_REF}" =~ ^refs/tags/v([0-9]+\.[0-9]+)\.* ]]; then
152:             target="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
153:           else
154:             target="main"
155:           fi
156:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
157:           container_name=$(docker run \
158:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
159:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
160:             -e SHA1="$GITHUB_SHA" \
161:             -e DOCS_VERSION="${target}" \
162:             -e DOCS_TYPE \
163:             -e RUN_DOXYGEN \
164:             -e WITH_PUSH \
165:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
166:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
167:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
168:             --tty \
169:             --detach \
170:             --user jenkins \
171:             -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc":/var/lib/jenkins/.netrc \
172:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
173:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
174:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
175:           )
176:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" bash -c "sudo chown -R jenkins . && pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ./.ci/pytorch/${DOCS_TYPE}_doc_push_script.sh"
177: 
178:       - name: Chown workspace
179:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace
180:         if: always()
181: 
182:       - name: configure aws credentials
183:         if : ${{ inputs.upload-aws-role-to-assume != '' }}
184:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
185:         with:
186:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.upload-aws-role-to-assume }}
187:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test
188:           aws-region: us-east-1
189: 
190:       - name: Upload Python Docs Preview
191:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
192:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && matrix.docs_type == 'python' && steps.build-docs.outcome == 'success' }}
193:         with:
194:           retention-days: 14
195:           s3-bucket: doc-previews
196:           if-no-files-found: error
197:           path: pytorch_docs/main/
198:           s3-prefix: pytorch/pytorch/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
199: 
200:       - name: Upload C++ Docs Preview
201:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
202:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && matrix.docs_type == 'cpp' && steps.build-docs.outcome == 'success' }}
203:         with:
204:           retention-days: 14
205:           if-no-files-found: error
206:           s3-bucket: doc-previews
207:           path: cppdocs/
208:           s3-prefix: pytorch/pytorch/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/cppdocs
209: 
210:       - name: Teardown Linux
211:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
212:         if: always()
213: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_docs.yml"}

{"content":"1: on:
2:   workflow_call:
3:     inputs:
4:       runner:
5:         type: string
6:         required: true
7:       ref:
8:         type: string
9:         required: true
10: 
11: jobs:
12:   lint-urls:
13:     if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' || !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-url-lint') }}
14:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
15:     with:
16:       job-name: lint-urls
17:       timeout: 120
18:       runner: ${{ inputs.runner }}linux.2xlarge
19:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
20:       fetch-depth: 0
21:       submodules: false
22:       ref: ${{ inputs.ref }}
23:       script: |
24:         ./scripts/lint_urls.sh $(
25:           if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
26:             echo "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}" "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"
27:           else
28:             echo "${{ github.event.before }}" "${{ github.sha }}"
29:           fi
30:         ) || {
31:           echo
32:           echo "URL lint failed."
33:           echo "If this is a transient outage, you can bypass it by adding the \`skip-url-lint\` label to your PR."
34:           echo "Or add \`@lint-ignore\` somewhere on the same line as the URL you want to skip checking."
35:           exit 1
36:         }
37: 
38:   lint-xrefs:
39:     if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' || !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-xref-lint') }}
40:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
41:     with:
42:       job-name: lint-xrefs
43:       timeout: 60
44:       runner: ${{ inputs.runner }}linux.2xlarge
45:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
46:       fetch-depth: 0
47:       submodules: false
48:       ref: ${{ inputs.ref }}
49:       script: |
50:         ./scripts/lint_xrefs.sh $(
51:           if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
52:             echo "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}" "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"
53:           else
54:             echo "${{ github.event.before }}" "${{ github.sha }}"
55:           fi
56:         ) || {
57:           echo
58:           echo "Xref lint failed."
59:           echo "If this is a transient outage, you can bypass it by adding the \`skip-xref-lint\` label to your PR."
60:           echo "Or add \`@lint-ignore\` somewhere on the same line as the reference you want to skip checking."
61:           exit 1
62:         }

{"content":"1: name: linux-build
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       docker-image-name:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Name of the base docker image to build with.
14:       build-generates-artifacts:
15:         required: false
16:         type: boolean
17:         default: true
18:         description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.
19:       sync-tag:
20:         required: false
21:         type: string
22:         default: ""
23:         description: |
24:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
25:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
26:       cuda-arch-list:
27:         required: false
28:         type: string
29:         default: "5.2"
30:         description: |
31:           List of CUDA architectures CI build should target.
32:       runner_prefix:
33:         required: false
34:         default: ""
35:         type: string
36:         description: Prefix for runner label
37:       runner:
38:         required: false
39:         type: string
40:         default: "linux.c7i.2xlarge"
41:         description: |
42:           Label of the runner this job should run on.
43:       test-matrix:
44:         required: false
45:         type: string
46:         description: |
47:           An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This
48:           is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter
49:           logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds
50:       selected-test-configs:
51:         description: |
52:           A comma-separated list of test configurations from the test matrix to keep,
53:           The empty list means we are going to keep every configurations by defaults
54:         required: false
55:         type: string
56:         default: ""
57:       s3-bucket:
58:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
59:         required: false
60:         type: string
61:         default: "gha-artifacts"
62:       aws-role-to-assume:
63:         description: Role to assume for downloading artifacts
64:         required: false
65:         type: string
66:         default: ""
67:       disable-monitor:
68:         description: |
69:           Disable utilization monitoring for build job
70:         required: false
71:         type: boolean
72:         default: false
73:       monitor-log-interval:
74:         description: |
75:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
76:         required: false
77:         type: number
78:         default: 5
79:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
80:         description: |
81:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
82:         required: false
83:         type: number
84:         default: 1
85:       allow-reuse-old-whl:
86:         description: |
87:           If set, the build try to pull an old wheel from s3 that was built on a
88:           commit with no cpp changes from this commit
89:         required: false
90:         type: boolean
91:         default: true
92:       build-additional-packages:
93:         description: |
94:           If set, the build job will also builds these packages and saves their
95:           wheels as artifacts
96:         required: false
97:         type: string
98:         default: ""
99:       build-external-packages:
100:         description: |
101:           If set, the build external packages and saves their wheels as artifacts
102:           use command separated list of packages to build ex: 'vllm,transformers'.
103:         required: false
104:         type: string
105:         default: ""
106: 
107:     secrets:
108:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
109:         required: false
110:         description: |
111:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
112:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:
113:         required: false
114:         description: |
115:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint
116: 
117:     outputs:
118:       docker-image:
119:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.docker-image }}
120:         description: The docker image containing the built PyTorch.
121:       test-matrix:
122:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}
123:         description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.
124:       build-environment:
125:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}
126:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
127: 
128: jobs:
129:   build:
130:     environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}
131:     # Don't run on forked repos
132:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
133:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runner }}
134:     timeout-minutes: 480
135:     outputs:
136:       docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
137:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}
138:       build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
139:     steps:
140:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
141:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
142:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
143:         with:
144:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
145:           instructions: |
146:             Build is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:
147:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash
148: 
149:       # [pytorch repo ref]
150:       # Use a pytorch/pytorch reference instead of a reference to the local
151:       # checkout because when we run this action we don't *have* a local
152:       # checkout. In other cases you should prefer a local checkout.
153:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
154:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
155:         with:
156:           no-sudo: true
157: 
158:       - name: Setup Linux
159:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
160:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
161: 
162:       - name: configure aws credentials
163:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
164:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
165:         with:
166:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}
167:           role-session-name: gha-linux-build
168:           aws-region: us-east-1
169: 
170:       - name: Get workflow job id
171:         id: get-job-id
172:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
173:         if: always()
174:         with:
175:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
176: 
177:       - name: Check if can use old whl build
178:         id: use-old-whl
179:         uses: ./.github/actions/reuse-old-whl
180:         if: ${{ inputs.allow-reuse-old-whl }}
181:         with:
182:           build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
183:           run-id: ${{ github.run_id }}
184:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
185:           job-id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
186:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
187: 
188:       - name: Calculate docker image
189:         id: calculate-docker-image
190:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
191:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
192:         with:
193:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}
194: 
195:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
196:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
197:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
198:         env:
199:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
200:         shell: bash
201:         run: |
202:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
203:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
204: 
205:       - name: Pull docker image
206:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
207:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
208:         with:
209:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
210: 
211:       - name: Parse ref
212:         id: parse-ref
213:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
214: 
215:       # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there
216:       - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)
217:         id: filter
218:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
219:         with:
220:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
221:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
222:           selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.selected-test-configs }}
223:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
224: 
225:       - name: Start monitoring script
226:         id: monitor-script
227:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
228:         shell: bash
229:         continue-on-error: true
230:         env:
231:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
232:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
233:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
234:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
235:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
236:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
237:         run: |
238:           mkdir -p ../../usage_logs
239:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7
240:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor \
241:           --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" \
242:           --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" \
243:           > "../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_${JOB_ID}.txt" 2>&1 &
244:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
245: 
246:       - name: Download pytest cache
247:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download
248:         continue-on-error: true
249:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
250:         with:
251:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache
252:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}
253:           s3_bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
254: 
255:       - name: Build
256:         if: (steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == '') && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
257:         id: build
258:         env:
259:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
260:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
261:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
262:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
263:           # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs
264:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2
265:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1
266:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla
267:           PR_LABELS: ${{ toJson(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name) }}
268:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}
269:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
270:           DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}
271:           XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}
272:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
273:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
274:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
275:           BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES: ${{ inputs.build-additional-packages }}
276:           RUNNER: ${{ inputs.runner }}
277:         run: |
278:           START_TIME=$(date +%s)
279:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"s390x"* ]]; then
280:             JENKINS_USER=
281:             USED_IMAGE="${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}"
282:             # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours
283:             # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container
284:             # when job is cancelled
285:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD="sleep 12h"
286: 
287:             # since some steps are skipped on s390x, if they are necessary, run them here
288:             env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
289:             env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
290:           else
291:             JENKINS_USER="--user jenkins"
292:             USED_IMAGE="${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
293:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=
294:           fi
295: 
296:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things
297:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf "%.3f \n", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)
298:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap
299:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058
300:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$(("${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}" + 3))
301: 
302:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"riscv64"* ]]; then
303:             # EC2 specific setup for RISC-V emulation
304:             # Ensure binfmt_misc is available
305:             echo "Mounting binfmt_misc filesystem"
306:             sudo mount binfmt_misc -t binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc 2>/dev/null || true
307: 
308:             echo "QEMU registration: multiarch/qemu-user-static"
309:             docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes || true
310: 
311:             # Final verification
312:             echo "Checking binfmt_misc status:"
313:             ls -la /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/ 2>/dev/null || echo "Cannot access binfmt_misc directory"
314: 
315:             if [ -f /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/qemu-riscv64 ]; then
316:               echo "qemu-riscv64 registration successful"
317:             else
318:               echo "qemu-riscv64 registration failed - proceeding without emulation"
319:               echo "This may cause RISC-V builds to fail"
320:             fi
321: 
322:             RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS="--privileged"
323:           else
324:             RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=
325:           fi
326: 
327:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
328:           # Used for JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD, which can be empty
329:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086
330:           container_name=$(docker run \
331:             ${RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS} \
332:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
333:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
334:             -e PR_NUMBER \
335:             -e SHA1 \
336:             -e BRANCH \
337:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \
338:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \
339:             -e XLA_CUDA \
340:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \
341:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \
342:             -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \
343:             -e PR_LABELS \
344:             -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \
345:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \
346:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \
347:             -e BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES \
348:             -e RUNNER \
349:             --memory="${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g" \
350:             --memory-swap="${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g" \
351:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
352:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
353:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
354:             --tty \
355:             --detach \
356:             ${JENKINS_USER} \
357:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
358:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
359:             "${USED_IMAGE}" \
360:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}
361:           )
362: 
363:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"s390x"* ]]; then
364:             docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt"
365:           fi
366: 
367:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'
368: 
369:           END_TIME=$(date +%s)
370:           echo "build_time=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
371: 
372:       - name: Build external packages
373:         id: build-external-packages
374:         if: inputs.build-external-packages != '' &&  steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'
375:         uses: ./.github/actions/build-external-packages
376:         with:
377:           build-targets: ${{ inputs.build-external-packages }}
378:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
379:           cuda-arch-list: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}
380:           output-dir: external
381: 
382:       - name: Move external packages to dist
383:         if: steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir != '' && steps.build-external-packages.outcome != 'skipped'
384:         shell: bash
385:         run: |
386:           src="${{ steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir }}"
387:           if [ -d "$src" ]; then
388:             mkdir -p "dist/$(dirname "$src")"
389:             mv "$src" "dist/$(dirname "$src")/"
390:           fi
391: 
392:       - name: Stop monitoring script
393:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
394:         shell: bash
395:         continue-on-error: true
396:         env:
397:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
398:         run: |
399:           kill "$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID"
400: 
401:       - name: Archive artifacts into zip
402:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
403:         run: |
404:           zip -1 -r artifacts.zip dist/ build/custom_test_artifacts build/lib build/bin .additional_ci_files
405: 
406:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts on S3
407:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
408:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
409:         with:
410:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
411:           retention-days: 14
412:           if-no-files-found: error
413:           path: artifacts.zip
414:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
415: 
416:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts for s390x
417:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
418:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
419:         with:
420:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
421:           retention-days: 14
422:           if-no-files-found: error
423:           path: artifacts.zip
424: 
425:       - name: copy logs
426:         shell: bash
427:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}
428:         continue-on-error: true
429:         run: |
430:           rm -f ./usage_logs
431:           mkdir -p ./usage_logs
432:           cp ../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt ./usage_logs/
433: 
434:       - name: Upload raw usage log to s3
435:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}
436:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5
437:         with:
438:           s3-prefix: |
439:             ${{ github.repository }}/${{ github.run_id }}/${{ github.run_attempt }}/artifact
440:           retention-days: 14
441:           if-no-files-found: warn
442:           path: usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt
443: 
444:       - name: Upload sccache stats
445:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
446:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-sccache-stats
447:         with:
448:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
449:           build-time: ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}
450: 
451:       - name: Upload utilization stats
452:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
453:         continue-on-error: true
454:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
455:         with:
456:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
457:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
458:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
459:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
460:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
461:           artifact_prefix: usage_log_build_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
462: 
463:       - name: Teardown Linux
464:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
465:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
466: 
467:       - name: Cleanup docker
468:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
469:         shell: bash
470:         run: |
471:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop
472:           docker stop -a || true
473:           docker kill -a || true
474: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml"}

{"content":"1: # The point of this workflow is to test that a FA3 wheel that was built based off the
2: # stable ABI as of torch nightly 20250830 can still run on the newer torch.
3: #
4: # This workflow is very similar to the _linux-test.yml workflow, with the following
5: # differences:
6: #   1. It is simpler (there is no test matrix)
7: #   2. It pulls flash-attention as a secondary repository in order to access the tests.
8: #      Note that it does not BUILD anything from flash-attention, as we have a prebuilt
9: #      wheel. We pull flash-attention only to run a few tests.
10: #   3. It runs only FA3 tests. No PyTorch tests are run.
11: name: linux-test-stable-fa3
12: 
13: on:
14:   workflow_call:
15:     inputs:
16:       build-environment:
17:         required: true
18:         type: string
19:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
20:       docker-image:
21:         required: true
22:         type: string
23:         description: Docker image to run in.
24:       timeout-minutes:
25:         required: false
26:         type: number
27:         default: 30
28:         description: |
29:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
30:       s3-bucket:
31:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
32:         required: false
33:         type: string
34:         default: "gha-artifacts"
35:     secrets:
36:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
37:         required: false
38:         description: |
39:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
40:       VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:
41:         required: false
42:         description: |
43:           HF Auth token to test vllm
44:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:
45:         required: false
46:         description: |
47:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint
48: 
49: env:
50:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
51: 
52: jobs:
53:   test:
54:     # Don't run on forked repos
55:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
56:     runs-on: linux.aws.h100
57:     timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes || 30 }}
58:     permissions:
59:       id-token: write
60:       contents: read
61:     steps:
62:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
63:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
64:         with:
65:           no-sudo: true
66: 
67:       - name: Checkout flash-attention as a secondary repository
68:         uses: actions/checkout@v4
69:         with:
70:           repository: Dao-AILab/flash-attention
71:           path: flash-attention
72: 
73:       - name: Setup Linux
74:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
75: 
76:       - name: Calculate docker image
77:         id: calculate-docker-image
78:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
79:         with:
80:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
81: 
82:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
83:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
84:         env:
85:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
86:         shell: bash
87:         run: |
88:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
89:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
90: 
91:       - name: Pull docker image
92:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
93:         with:
94:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
95: 
96:       - name: Check if in a container runner
97:         shell: bash
98:         id: check_container_runner
99:         run: echo "IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
100: 
101:       - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run
102:         id: setup-gpu-flag
103:         run: echo "GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
104: 
105:       - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container
106:         id: setup-sscache-port-flag
107:         run: echo "SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
108:         if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}
109: 
110:       - name: Get workflow job id
111:         id: get-job-id
112:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
113:         if: always()
114:         with:
115:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
116: 
117:       - name: Download build artifacts
118:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
119:         with:
120:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
121:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
122: 
123:       - name: Parse ref
124:         id: parse-ref
125:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
126: 
127:       - name: Set Test step time
128:         id: test-timeout
129:         shell: bash
130:         env:
131:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}
132:         run: |
133:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
134: 
135:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker
136:         shell: bash
137:         run: |
138:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
139:           env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
140: 
141:       - name: Test
142:         id: test
143:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
144:         env:
145:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
146:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
147:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
148:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
149:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
150:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
151:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
152:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
153:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
154:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
155:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
156:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
157:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}
158:           SHM_SIZE: '2g'
159:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
160:           VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN }}
161:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
162:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
163:           ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX: ${{ github.job }}-${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
164:         run: |
165:           set -x
166: 
167:           TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test_fa3_abi_stable.sh
168: 
169:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things
170:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf "%.3f \n", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)
171:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap
172:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058
173:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$(("${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}" + 3))
174: 
175: 
176:           SHM_OPTS="--shm-size=${SHM_SIZE}"
177:           JENKINS_USER="--user jenkins"
178:           DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=
179: 
180:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
181:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase
182:           # Used for GPU_FLAG, SHM_OPTS, JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD since that doesn't play nice
183:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090
184:           container_name=$(docker run \
185:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \
186:             ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \
187:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
188:             -e PR_NUMBER \
189:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
190:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \
191:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \
192:             -e GITHUB_JOB \
193:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \
194:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \
195:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
196:             -e JOB_ID \
197:             -e JOB_NAME \
198:             -e BASE_SHA \
199:             -e BRANCH \
200:             -e SHA1 \
201:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
202:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \
203:             -e VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN \
204:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \
205:             -e ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX \
206:             --memory="${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g" \
207:             --memory-swap="${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g" \
208:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
209:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
210:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
211:             --ipc=host \
212:             ${SHM_OPTS} \
213:             --tty \
214:             --detach \
215:             --name="${container_name}" \
216:             ${JENKINS_USER} \
217:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
218:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
219:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}" \
220:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}
221:           )
222: 
223:           echo "DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
224: 
225:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "python3 -m pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}"
226: 
227:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)
228:         if: always()
229:         run: |
230:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156
231:           find . -iname "core.[1-9]*" -exec docker exec "${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}" sh -c "gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'" \;
232: 
233:       - name: Store Core dumps on S3
234:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
235:         if: failure()
236:         with:
237:           name: coredumps-fa3-stable-abi-smoke-tests
238:           retention-days: 14
239:           if-no-files-found: ignore
240:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*
241: 
242:       - name: Upload utilization stats
243:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' }}
244:         continue-on-error: true
245:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
246:         with:
247:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
248:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
249:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
250:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
251:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
252: 
253:       - name: Teardown Linux
254:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
255:         if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'
256: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_linux-test-stable-fa3.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: linux-test
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       test-matrix:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.
14:       docker-image:
15:         required: true
16:         type: string
17:         description: Docker image to run in.
18:       sync-tag:
19:         required: false
20:         type: string
21:         default: ""
22:         description: |
23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
25:       timeout-minutes:
26:         required: false
27:         type: number
28:         default: 240
29:         description: |
30:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
31:       use-gha:
32:         required: false
33:         type: string
34:         default: ""
35:         description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.
36:       dashboard-tag:
37:         required: false
38:         type: string
39:         default: ""
40:       s3-bucket:
41:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
42:         required: false
43:         type: string
44:         default: "gha-artifacts"
45:       aws-role-to-assume:
46:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts
47:         required: false
48:         type: string
49:         default: ""
50:       disable-monitor:
51:         description: |
52:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.
53:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,
54:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.
55:         required: false
56:         type: boolean
57:         default: false
58:       monitor-log-interval:
59:         description: |
60:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
61:         required: false
62:         type: number
63:         default: 5
64:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
65:         description: |
66:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
67:         required: false
68:         type: number
69:         default: 1
70:     secrets:
71:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
72:         required: false
73:         description: |
74:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
75:       VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:
76:         required: false
77:         description: |
78:           HF Auth token to test vllm
79:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:
80:         required: false
81:         description: |
82:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint
83: 
84: env:
85:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
86: 
87: jobs:
88:   test:
89:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix
90:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'
91:     strategy:
92:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}
93:       fail-fast: false
94:     environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}
95:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
96:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
97:     permissions:
98:       id-token: write
99:       contents: read
100:     steps:
101:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
102:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
103:         if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
104:         with:
105:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
106:           instructions: |
107:             All testing is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:
108:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash
109: 
110:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
111:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
112:         with:
113:           no-sudo: true
114: 
115:       - name: Setup Python
116:         if: contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')
117:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
118:         with:
119:           python-version: '3.12'
120:           cache: pip
121: 
122:       - name: Setup Linux
123:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
124:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')
125: 
126:       - name: configure aws credentials
127:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
128:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
129:         with:
130:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}
131:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test
132:           aws-region: us-east-1
133: 
134:       - name: Login to Amazon ECR
135:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}
136:         id: login-ecr
137:         continue-on-error: true
138:         uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1
139: 
140:       - name: Calculate docker image
141:         id: calculate-docker-image
142:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
143:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
144:         with:
145:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
146: 
147:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
148:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
149:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
150:         env:
151:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
152:         shell: bash
153:         run: |
154:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
155:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
156: 
157:       - name: Pull docker image
158:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
159:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
160:         with:
161:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
162: 
163:       - name: Check if in a container runner
164:         shell: bash
165:         id: check_container_runner
166:         run: echo "IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
167: 
168:       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG
169:         id: install-nvidia-driver
170:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main
171:         with:
172:           driver-version: ${{ matrix.config == 'legacy_nvidia_driver' && '525.105.17' || '580.82.07' }}
173:         if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}
174: 
175:       - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run
176:         id: setup-gpu-flag
177:         run: echo "GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
178:         if: ${{ steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && (steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' || contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')) }}
179: 
180:       - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container
181:         id: setup-sscache-port-flag
182:         run: echo "SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
183:         if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}
184: 
185:       - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency
186:         run: |
187:           sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1
188:           sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410
189:           nvidia-smi
