{"content":"1: # TODO: this looks sort of similar to _linux-test, but there are like a dozen
2: # places where you would have to insert an if statement. Probably it's better to
3: # just use a different workflow altogether
4: 
5: name: xpu-test
6: 
7: on:
8:   workflow_call:
9:     inputs:
10:       build-environment:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
14:       test-matrix:
15:         required: true
16:         type: string
17:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.
18:       docker-image:
19:         required: true
20:         type: string
21:         description: Docker image to run in.
22:       sync-tag:
23:         required: false
24:         type: string
25:         default: ""
26:         description: |
27:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
28:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
29:       timeout-minutes:
30:         required: false
31:         type: number
32:         default: 390
33:         description: |
34:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
35:       tests-to-include:
36:         required: false
37:         type: string
38:         default: ""
39:         description: |
40:           List of tests to include (empty string implies default list)
41:       dashboard-tag:
42:         required: false
43:         type: string
44:         default: ""
45:       disable-monitor:
46:         description: |
47:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.
48:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,
49:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.
50:         required: false
51:         type: boolean
52:         default: true
53:       monitor-log-interval:
54:         description: |
55:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
56:         required: false
57:         type: number
58:         default: 5
59:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
60:         description: |
61:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
62:         required: false
63:         type: number
64:         default: 1
65:     secrets:
66:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
67:         required: false
68:         description: |
69:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
70: permissions:
71:   id-token: write
72:   contents: read
73: 
74: env:
75:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
76: 
77: jobs:
78:   test:
79:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix
80:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'
81:     strategy:
82:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}
83:       fail-fast: false
84:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
85:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
86:     steps:
87:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
88:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
89:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
90: 
91:       - name: Setup XPU
92:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-xpu
93: 
94:       - name: configure aws credentials
95:         id: aws_creds
96:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
97:         with:
98:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only
99:           aws-region: us-east-1
100: 
101:       - name: Login to Amazon ECR
102:         id: login-ecr
103:         uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1
104: 
105:       - name: Calculate docker image
106:         id: calculate-docker-image
107:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
108:         with:
109:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
110: 
111:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
112:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
113:         env:
114:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
115:         shell: bash
116:         run: |
117:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
118:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
119: 
120:       - name: Pull docker image
121:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
122:         with:
123:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
124: 
125:       - name: Get workflow job id
126:         id: get-job-id
127:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
128:         if: always()
129:         with:
130:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
131: 
132:       - name: Start monitoring script
133:         id: monitor-script
134:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
135:         shell: bash
136:         continue-on-error: true
137:         env:
138:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
139:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
140:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
141:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
142:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
143:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
144:         run: |
145:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84
146:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &
147:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
148: 
149:       - name: Download build artifacts
150:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
151:         with:
152:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
153: 
154:       - name: Parse ref
155:         id: parse-ref
156:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
157: 
158:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues
159:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently
160:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do
161:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.
162:         id: keep-going
163:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
164:         with:
165:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
166:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
167:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
168: 
169:       - name: Set Test step time
170:         id: test-timeout
171:         shell: bash
172:         env:
173:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
174:         run: |
175:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
176: 
177:       - name: Test
178:         id: test
179:         env:
180:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
181:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
182:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
183:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
184:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
185:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
186:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
187:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
188:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
189:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
190:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
191:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
192:           PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES: 1
193:           PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL: 1
194:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}
195:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}
196:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}
197:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}
198:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}
199:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}
200:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}
201:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}
202:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}
203:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
204:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla
205:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}
206:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}
207:           TESTS_TO_INCLUDE: ${{ inputs.tests-to-include }}
208:           DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}
209:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
210:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
211:         run: |
212:           # Fetch aws credential from IMDs
213:           eval "$(python3 .github/scripts/get_aws_session_tokens.py)"
214:           set -x
215: 
216:           TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh
217: 
218:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
219:           # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice
220:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090
221:           container_name=$(docker run \
222:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \
223:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
224:             -e PR_NUMBER \
225:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
226:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \
227:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \
228:             -e GITHUB_JOB \
229:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \
230:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \
231:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
232:             -e JOB_ID \
233:             -e BRANCH \
234:             -e SHA1 \
235:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \
236:             -e AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID \
237:             -e AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY \
238:             -e AWS_SESSION_TOKEN \
239:             -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \
240:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \
241:             -e TEST_CONFIG \
242:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \
243:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \
244:             -e PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES \
245:             -e PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL \
246:             -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \
247:             -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \
248:             -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \
249:             -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \
250:             -e NO_TD \
251:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
252:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \
253:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \
254:             -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \
255:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \
256:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \
257:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \
258:             -e TESTS_TO_INCLUDE \
259:             -e ZE_AFFINITY_MASK \
260:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \
261:             -e DASHBOARD_TAG \
262:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
263:             --ulimit stack=10485760:83886080 \
264:             --ulimit core=0 \
265:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
266:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
267:             --shm-size="8g" \
268:             --tty \
269:             --detach \
270:             --name="${container_name}" \
271:             --user jenkins \
272:             --privileged \
273:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
274:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
275:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
276:           )
277:           # save container name for later step
278:           echo "CONTAINER_NAME=${container_name}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
279:           # jenkins user does not have write permission to mounted workspace; work-around by copying within container to jenkins home
280:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "cd .. && cp -R workspace pytorch && cd pytorch && pip install dist/*.whl && ${TEST_COMMAND}"
281: 
282:       - name: Save test results
283:         if: always()
284:         run: |
285:           # copy test results back to the mounted workspace, needed sudo, resulting permissions were correct
286:           docker exec -t "${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}" sh -c "cd ../pytorch && sudo cp -R test/test-reports ../workspace/test"
287: 
288:       - name: Change permissions
289:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion }}
290:         run: |
291:           docker exec -t "${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}" sh -c "sudo chown -R 1000:1000 test"
292: 
293:       - name: Print remaining test logs
294:         shell: bash
295:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion
296:         run: |
297:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true
298: 
299:       - name: Stop monitoring script
300:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
301:         shell: bash
302:         continue-on-error: true
303:         env:
304:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
305:         run: |
306:           kill "$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID"
307: 
308:       - name: Upload test artifacts
309:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts
310:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'
311:         with:
312:           use-gha: true
313:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
314: 
315:       - name: Upload utilization stats
316:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}
317:         continue-on-error: true
318:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
319:         with:
320:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
321:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
322:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
323:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
324:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
325: 
326:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)
327:         if: always()
328:         run: |
329:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156
330:           find . -iname "core.[1-9]*" -exec docker exec "${CONTAINER_NAME}" sh -c "gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'" \;
331: 
332:       - name: Stop container before exit
333:         if: always()
334:         run: |
335:           # Workaround for multiple runners on same IDC node
336:           docker stop "${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}"
337: 
338:       - name: Store Core dumps on GitHub
339:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
340:         if: failure()
341:         with:
342:           name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}
343:           retention-days: 14
344:           if-no-files-found: ignore
345:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*
346: 
347:       - name: Authenticate with AWS
348:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
349:         with:
350:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results
351:           # The max duration enforced by the server side
352:           role-duration-seconds: 18000
353:           aws-region: us-east-1
354: 
355:       - name: Upload the benchmark results
356:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@main
357:         with:
358:           benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports
359:           dry-run: false
360:           schema-version: v3
361:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
362: 
363:       - name: Teardown XPU
364:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-xpu
365: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: Lint
2: # Workflow that runs lint checks and also unittests for tools, and scripts.
3: 
4: on:
5:   pull_request:
6:     branches-ignore:
7:       - nightly
8:   push:
9:     branches:
10:       - main
11:       - release/*
12:       - landchecks/*
13:     tags:
14:       - ciflow/pull/*
15:       - ciflow/trunk/*
16:   workflow_dispatch:
17: 
18: permissions: read-all
19: # The names of steps that actually test the code should be suffixed with `(nonretryable)`.
20: # When any other step fails, it's job will be retried once by retryBot.
21: jobs:
22:   get-label-type:
23:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
24:     name: get-label-type
25:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@main
26:     with:
27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
30: 
31:   get-changed-files:
32:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
33:     name: Get changed files
34:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_get-changed-files.yml
35:     with:
36:       all_files: ${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'lint-all-files') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'Reverted') || github.event_name == 'push' }}
37: 
38:   lintrunner-clang:
39:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
40:     # Needed to prevent deduping on HUD
41:     name: lintrunner-clang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
42:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
43:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to clangtidy / clangformat
44:     if: |
45:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (
46:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||
47:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.h') ||
48:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cpp') ||
49:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cc') ||
50:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cxx') ||
51:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hpp') ||
52:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hxx') ||
53:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cu') ||
54:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cuh') ||
55:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.mm') ||
56:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.metal')
57:       )
58:     with:
59:       timeout: 120
60:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
61:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-linter
62:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
63:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
64:       fetch-depth: 0
65:       submodules: true
66:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
67:       script: |
68:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
69:         if [ "$CHANGED_FILES" = "*" ]; then
70:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT --all-files"
71:         else
72:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT $CHANGED_FILES"
73:         fi
74:         export CLANG=1
75:         .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
76: 
77:   # NOTE: mypy needs its own job because it depends on --all-files, without assessing all files it sometimes
78:   #       fails to find types when it should
79:   # NOTE: We should be able to disable this and consolidate with Pyrefly
80:   lintrunner-pyrefly:
81:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
82:     name: lintrunner-pyrefly-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
83:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
84:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to pyrefly
85:     if: |
86:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (
87:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||
88:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.py') ||
89:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.pyi')
90:       )
91:     with:
92:       timeout: 120
93:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
94:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
95:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
96:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
97:       fetch-depth: 0
98:       submodules: true
99:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
100:       script: |
101:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
102:         echo "Running pyrefly"
103:         ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take PYREFLY --all-files" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
104: 
105:   lintrunner-noclang:
106:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
107:     name: lintrunner-noclang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
108:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
109:     with:
110:       timeout: 120
111:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
112:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
113:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
114:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
115:       fetch-depth: 0
116:       submodules: true
117:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
118:       script: |
119:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
120:         echo "Running all other linters"
121:         if [ "$CHANGED_FILES" = '*' ]; then
122:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY --all-files" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
123:         else
124:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY ${CHANGED_FILES}" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
125:         fi
126: 
127:   quick-checks:
128:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
129:     needs: get-label-type
130:     with:
131:       timeout: 120
132:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
133:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
134:       fetch-depth: 0
135:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
136:       script: |
137:         # Ensure no non-breaking spaces
138:         # NB: We use 'printf' below rather than '\u000a' since bash pre-4.2
139:         # does not support the '\u000a' syntax (which is relevant for local linters)
140:         (! git --no-pager grep -In "$(printf '\xC2\xA0')" -- . || (echo "The above lines have non-breaking spaces (U+00A0); please convert them to spaces (U+0020)"; false))
141: 
142:         # Ensure cross-OS compatible file names
143:         (! git ls-files | grep -E '([<>:"|?*]|[ .]$)' || (echo "The above file names are not valid across all operating systems. Please ensure they don't contain the characters '<>:""|?*' and don't end with a white space or a '.' "; false))
144: 
145:         # Ensure no versionless Python shebangs
146:         (! git --no-pager grep -In '#!.*python$' -- . || (echo "The above lines have versionless Python shebangs; please specify either python2 or python3"; false))
147: 
148:         # Ensure ciflow tags mentioned in config
149:         python3 .github/scripts/collect_ciflow_labels.py --validate-tags
150: 
151:         # C++ docs check
152:         pushd docs/cpp/source
153:         ./check-doxygen.sh
154:         popd
155: 
156:         # CUDA kernel launch check
157:         set -eux
158:         python3 torch/testing/_internal/check_kernel_launches.py |& tee cuda_kernel_launch_checks.txt
159: 
160:   pr-sanity-checks:
161:     name: pr-sanity-checks
162:     needs: get-label-type
163:     runs-on: [self-hosted, "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.large"]
164:     # Only run this on pull requests. This check is simple enough to be done without a Docker image
165:     if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-pr-sanity-checks')
166:     steps:
167:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
168:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
169:         with:
170:           submodules: false
171:           fetch-depth: -1
172: 
173:       - name: PR size check (nonretryable)
174:         env:
175:           BASE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
176:           HEAD: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
177:         run: |
178:           bash .github/scripts/pr-sanity-check.sh
179: 
180:   workflow-checks:
181:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
182:     needs: get-label-type
183:     with:
184:       timeout: 120
185:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
186:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
187:       fetch-depth: -1
188:       submodules: true
189:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
190:       script: |
191:         # Regenerate workflows
192:         .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py
193: 
194:         RC=0
195:         # Assert that regenerating the workflows didn't change them
196:         if ! .github/scripts/report_git_status.sh .github/workflows; then
197:           echo
198:           echo 'As shown by the above diff, the committed .github/workflows'
199:           echo 'are not up to date according to .github/templates.'
200:           echo 'Please run this command, commit, and push again to your PR:'
201:           echo
202:           echo '    .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py'
203:           echo
204:           echo 'If running that command does nothing, you may need to rebase'
205:           echo 'onto a more recent commit from the PyTorch main branch.'
206:           RC=1
207:         fi
208: 
209:         # Check that jobs will be cancelled
210:         .github/scripts/ensure_actions_will_cancel.py
211: 
212:         exit $RC
213: 
214:   toc:
215:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
216:     needs: get-label-type
217:     with:
218:       timeout: 120
219:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
220:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
221:       fetch-depth: 0
222:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
223:       script: |
224:         # Regenerate ToCs and check that they didn't change
225:         set -eu
226: 
227:         export PATH=~/.npm-global/bin:"$PATH"
228:         for FILE in $(git grep -Il '<!-- toc -->' -- '**.md'); do
229:           markdown-toc --bullets='-' -i "$FILE"
230:         done
231: 
232:         if ! .github/scripts/report_git_status.sh .; then
233:           echo
234:           echo 'As shown by the above diff, the table of contents in one or'
235:           echo 'more Markdown files is not up to date with the file contents.'
236:           echo 'You can either apply that Git diff directly to correct the'
237:           echo 'table of contents, or if you have npm installed, you can'
238:           echo 'install the npm package markdown-toc and run the following'
239:           # shellcheck disable=SC2016
240:           echo 'command (replacing $FILE with the filename for which you want'
241:           echo 'to regenerate the table of contents):'
242:           echo
243:           # shellcheck disable=SC2016
244:           echo "    markdown-toc --bullets='-' -i \"\$FILE\""
245:           false
246:         fi
247: 
248:   test-tools:
249:     name: Test tools
250:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}
251:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
252:     needs: get-label-type
253:     with:
254:       timeout: 120
255:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
256:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
257:       fetch-depth: 0
258:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
259:       script: |
260:         # Test tools
261:         PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) pytest tools/stats
262:         PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) pytest tools/test -o "python_files=test*.py"
263:         PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) pytest .github/scripts -o "python_files=test*.py"
264: 
265:   test_run_test:
266:     name: Test `run_test.py` is usable without boto3
267:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}
268:     runs-on: linux.24_04.4x
269:     steps:
270:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
271:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
272:         with:
273:           submodules: false
274:           fetch-depth: 1
275:       - name: Setup Python 3.10
276:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
277:         with:
278:           python-version: '3.10'
279:           architecture: x64
280:           cache: pip
281:       - name: Install dependencies
282:         run: |
283:           python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
284:           pip install pytest-rerunfailures==11.1.* pytest-flakefinder==1.1.* pytest-xdist==3.3.* expecttest==0.3.* fbscribelogger==0.1.* numpy==1.24.*
285:           pip install torch --pre --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly/cpu/
286:       - name: Run run_test.py (nonretryable)
287:         run: |
288:           # Run test_vulkan, which is a fast noop on Linux
289:           python3 test/run_test.py --include test_vulkan --verbose
290: 
291:   test_collect_env:
292:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}
293:     name: Test collect_env
294:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
295:     strategy:
296:       matrix:
297:         include:
298:           - test_type: with_torch
299:             runner: linux.24_04.4x
300:           - test_type: without_torch
301:             runner: linux.24_04.4x
302:           - test_type: older_python_version
303:             runner: linux.24_04.4x
304:     steps:
305:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
306:       # deep clone (fetch-depth 0) required, to allow us to use git log
307:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
308:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
309:         with:
310:           submodules: false
311:           fetch-depth: 1
312:       - name: Get min python version
313:         id: get-min-python-version
314:         if: matrix.test_type == 'older_python_version'
315:         run: |
316:           set -eou pipefail
317:           # Generate PyTorch version to use
318:           echo "MIN_PYTHON_VERSION=$(python3 .github/scripts/get_ci_variable.py --min-python-version)" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
319:       - name: Setup Old Python version
320:         if: matrix.test_type == 'older_python_version'
321:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
322:         with:
323:           python-version: 3.8
324:           architecture: x64
325:           check-latest: false
326:           cache: pip
327:           cache-dependency-path: |
328:             **/requirements-build.txt
329:             **/requirements.txt
330:       - name: Setup Min Python version
331:         if: matrix.test_type != 'older_python_version'
332:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
333:         with:
334:           python-version: ${{ steps.get-min-python-version.outputs.MIN_PYTHON_VERSION }}
335:           architecture: x64
336:           check-latest: false
337:           cache: pip
338:           cache-dependency-path: |
339:             **/requirements-build.txt
340:             **/requirements.txt
341:       - name: Install torch
342:         if: matrix.test_type == 'with_torch'
343:         run: |
344:           pip install -r requirements.txt
345:           # Doesn't really matter what torch version, we just need ANY torch installed
346:           pip install 'torch==2.*'
347:       - name: Run collect_env.py (nonretryable)
348:         run: |
349:           # All we need to see is that it passes
350:           python3 torch/utils/collect_env.py
351: 
352:   link-check:
353:     name: Link checks
354:     needs: get-label-type
355:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_link_check.yml
356:     with:
357:       runner: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}
358:       ref:    ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
359:     secrets: inherit
360: 
361: concurrency:
362:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}
363:   cancel-in-progress: true
364: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/lint.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: nightly
2: 
3: on:
4:   schedule:
5:     - cron: 0 0 * * *
6:   push:
7:     tags:
8:       # NOTE: Doc build pipelines should only get triggered on:
9:       # Major or minor release candidates builds
10:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.0+-rc[0-9]+
11:       # Final RC for major, minor and patch releases
12:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+
13:       - ciflow/nightly/*
14:   workflow_dispatch:
15: 
16: 
17: concurrency:
18:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}--${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}
19:   cancel-in-progress: true
20: 
21: jobs:
22:   get-label-type:
23:     name: get-label-type
24:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@main
25:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}
26:     with:
27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
30:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
31: 
32:   link-check:
33:     name: Link checks
34:     needs: get-label-type
35:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_link_check.yml
36:     with:
37:       runner: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}
38:       ref:    ${{ github.sha }}
39:     secrets: inherit
40: 
41:   docs-build:
42:     name: docs build
43:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml
44:     needs: get-label-type
45:     with:
46:       runner_prefix: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}"
47:       build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11
48:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11
49:     secrets: inherit
50: 
51:   docs-push:
52:     name: docs push
53:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_docs.yml
54:     needs:
55:       - docs-build
56:       - get-label-type
57:     with:
58:       runner_prefix: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}"
59:       build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11
60:       docker-image: ${{ needs.docs-build.outputs.docker-image }}
61:       push: ${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/v') }}
62:       run-doxygen: true
63:     secrets:
64:       GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN }}
65: 
66:   update-commit-hashes:
67:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
68:     environment: update-commit-hash
69:     strategy:
70:       matrix:
71:         include:
72:           - repo-name: vision
73:             repo-owner: pytorch
74:             branch: main
75:             pin-folder: .github/ci_commit_pins
76:           - repo-name: audio
77:             repo-owner: pytorch
78:             branch: main
79:             pin-folder: .github/ci_commit_pins
80:           # executorch jobs are disabled since it needs some manual work for the hash update
81:           # - repo-name: executorch
82:           #   repo-owner: pytorch
83:           #   branch: main
84:           #   pin-folder: .ci/docker/ci_commit_pins
85:           - repo-name: triton
86:             repo-owner: triton-lang
87:             branch: main
88:             pin-folder: .ci/docker/ci_commit_pins
89:           - repo-name: vllm
90:             repo-owner: vllm-project
91:             branch: main
92:             pin-folder: .github/ci_commit_pins
93:     # Allow this to be triggered on either a schedule or on workflow_dispatch to allow for easier testing
94:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')
95:     steps:
96:       - name: "${{ matrix.repo-owner }}/${{ matrix.repo-name }} update-commit-hash"
97:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/update-commit-hash@main
98:         with:
99:           repo-owner: ${{ matrix.repo-owner }}
100:           repo-name: ${{ matrix.repo-name }}
101:           branch: ${{ matrix.branch }}
102:           pin-folder: ${{ matrix.pin-folder}}
103:           updatebot-token: ${{ secrets.UPDATEBOT_TOKEN }}
104:           pytorchbot-token: ${{ secrets.GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN }}
105: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/nightly.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: weekly
2: 
3: on:
4:   schedule:
5:     # Mondays at 7:37am UTC = 12:27am PST
6:     # Choose a random time near midnight PST because it may be delayed if there are high loads
7:     # See https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows#schedule
8:     - cron: 37 7 * * 1
9:   workflow_dispatch:
10: 
11: permissions: read-all
12: 
13: jobs:
14:   update-commit-hash:
15:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
16:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
17:     environment: update-commit-hash
18:     steps:
19:       - name: Checkout repo
20:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
21:         with:
22:           fetch-depth: 0
23:       - name: update-xla-commit-hash
24:         continue-on-error: true
25:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/update-commit-hash@main
26:         with:
27:           repo-name: xla
28:           branch: master
29:           pin-folder: .github/ci_commit_pins
30:           test-infra-ref: main
31:           updatebot-token: ${{ secrets.UPDATEBOT_TOKEN }}
32:           pytorchbot-token: ${{ secrets.GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN }}
33: 
34:   update-slow-tests:
35:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
36:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
37:     environment: update-commit-hash
38:     steps:
39:       - name: Checkout repo
40:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
41:         with:
42:           fetch-depth: 0
43:           token: ${{ secrets.UPDATEBOT_TOKEN }}
44:       - name: Setup Python
45:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
46:         with:
47:           python-version: '3.10'
48:       - name: Install requirements
49:         shell: bash
50:         run: |
51:           pip install requests==2.32.2 clickhouse-connect==0.8.14
52:       - name: Update slow test file
53:         shell: bash
54:         env:
55:           PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN }}
56:           UPDATEBOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.UPDATEBOT_TOKEN }}
57:           CLICKHOUSE_ENDPOINT: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_ENDPOINT }}
58:           CLICKHOUSE_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_READONLY_USERNAME }}
59:           CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_READONLY_PASSWORD }}
60:         run: |
61:           git config --global user.name "PyTorch UpdateBot"
62:           git config --global user.email "pytorchupdatebot@users.noreply.github.com"
63:           python tools/testing/update_slow_tests.py
64: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/weekly.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: trunk
2: 
3: on:
4:   push:
5:     branches:
6:       - main
7:       - release/*
8:       - landchecks/*
9:     tags:
10:       - ciflow/trunk/*
11:   workflow_dispatch:
12:   schedule:
13:     - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT
14: 
15: concurrency:
16:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}
17:   cancel-in-progress: true
18: 
19: permissions:
20:   id-token: write
21:   contents: read
22: 
23: jobs:
24:   llm-td:
25:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
26:     name: before-test
27:     uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml
28:     permissions:
29:       id-token: write
30:       contents: read
31: 
32:   target-determination:
33:     name: before-test
34:     uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml
35:     needs: llm-td
36:     permissions:
37:       id-token: write
38:       contents: read
39: 
40:   get-label-type:
41:     name: get-label-type
42:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@main
43:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}
44:     with:
45:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
46:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
47:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
48:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
49: 
50:   libtorch-linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-debug-build:
51:     name: libtorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-debug
52:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml
53:     needs: get-label-type
54:     with:
55:       build-environment: libtorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11
56:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11
57:       build-generates-artifacts: false
58:       runner_prefix: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}"
59:       runner: "linux.c7i.4xlarge"
60:       test-matrix: |
61:         { include: [
62:           { config: "default", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },
63:         ]}
64:     secrets: inherit
65: 
66:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build:
67:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11
68:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml
69:     needs: get-label-type
70:     with:
71:       runner_prefix: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}"
72:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11
73:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11
74:       cuda-arch-list: '7.5 8.9'
75:       test-matrix: |
76:         { include: [
77:           { config: "default", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
78:           { config: "default", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
79:           { config: "default", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
80:           { config: "default", shard: 4, num_shards: 5, runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
81:           { config: "default", shard: 5, num_shards: 5, runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },

{"content":"1: name: Update viable/strict
2: 
3: on:
4:   schedule:
5:     - cron: 17,47 * * * *
6:   workflow_dispatch:
7: 
8: concurrency:
9:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}
10:   cancel-in-progress: true
11: 
12: jobs:
13:   do_update_viablestrict:
14:     permissions:
15:       id-token: write
16:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}
17:     runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
18:     environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'schedule') && 'mergebot' || '' }}
19:     steps:
20:       - name: Update viable/strict
21:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/update-viablestrict@main
22:         id: update_viablestrict
23:         with:
24:           repository: pytorch/pytorch
25:           stable-branch: viable/strict
26:           requires: '[\"pull\", \"trunk\", \"lint\",  \"linux-aarch64\"]'
27:           secret-bot-token: ${{ secrets.MERGEBOT_TOKEN }}
28:           clickhouse-url: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_URL }}
29:           clickhouse-username: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_VIABLESTRICT_USERNAME }}
30:           clickhouse-password: ${{ secrets.CLICKHOUSE_VIABLESTRICT_PASSWORD }}
31: 
32:       - name: Authenticate to AWS with OIDC
33:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
34:         with:
35:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/upload_to_ossci_raw_job_status
36:           aws-region: us-east-1
37: 
38:       - name: Print sha
39:         env:
40:           LATEST_SHA: ${{ steps.update_viablestrict.outputs.latest_viable_sha }}
41:           PUSH_RESULT: ${{ steps.update_viablestrict.outputs.push_result }}
42:           TIME: ${{ steps.update_viablestrict.outputs.time }}
43:         run: |
44:           echo "${PUSH_RESULT}"
45:           if [ "$PUSH_RESULT" = "Everything up-to-date" ]; then
46:             echo "No update pushed"
47:           elif [ "${LATEST_SHA}" == "None" ]; then
48:             echo "No viable/strict candidate found"
49:           else
50:             echo "{\"sha\": \"${LATEST_SHA}\", \"repository\":\"pytorch/pytorch\", \"timestamp\": ${TIME}}" > "/tmp/${LATEST_SHA}.json"
51:             pip install awscli==1.29.40
52:             aws s3 cp "/tmp/${LATEST_SHA}.json" "s3://ossci-raw-job-status/stable_pushes/pytorch/pytorch/${LATEST_SHA}.json"
53:             # Push new viable/strict tag
54:             cd pytorch/pytorch
55:             git push origin "${LATEST_SHA}:refs/tags/viable/strict/${TIME}"
56:           fi
57: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/update-viablestrict.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: Update PyTorch Labels in S3
2: 
3: on:
4:   label:
5:   workflow_dispatch:
6: 
7: concurrency:
8:   group: 1
9:   cancel-in-progress: true
10: 
11: jobs:
12:   update-labels-in-S3:
13:     runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
14:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}
15:     permissions:
16:       id-token: write
17:       contents: read
18:     steps:
19:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
20:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
21:         with:
22:           fetch-depth: 1
23:           submodules: false
24:       - name: configure aws credentials
25:         id: aws_creds
26:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
27:         with:
28:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_update_pytorch_labels
29:           aws-region: us-east-1
30:       - name: Update PyTorch labels list in S3
31:         run: |
32:           python3 -m pip install boto3==1.35.42
33:           .github/scripts/export_pytorch_labels.py pytorch pytorch
34: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/update_pytorch_labels.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: Upload test stats while running
2: 
3: on:
4:   schedule:
5:     # Every hour
6:     - cron: '0 * * * *'
7: 
8: concurrency:
9:   group: upload-test-stats-while-running
10:   cancel-in-progress: true
11: 
12: jobs:
13:   upload_test_stats_while_running:
14:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
15:     name: Upload test stats while running
16:     runs-on: linux.2xlarge
17:     steps:
18:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
19:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
20:         with:
21:           fetch-depth: 1
22:           submodules: false
23: 
24:       - name: Setup Linux
25:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
26: 
27:       - name: Install requirements
28:         run: |
29:           python3 -m pip install requests==2.32.2 boto3==1.35.42
30: 
31:       - name: Upload test stats
32:         env:
33:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
34:         run: |
35:           python3 -m tools.stats.upload_test_stats_running_jobs

{"content":"1: name: Upload test stats
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_run:
5:     workflows:
6:       - pull
7:       - trunk
8:       - trunk-rocm-mi300
9:       - periodic
10:       - periodic-rocm-mi200
11:       - periodic-rocm-mi300
12:       - inductor
13:       - unstable
14:       - slow
15:       - slow-rocm-mi200
16:       - unstable-periodic
17:       - inductor-periodic
18:       - rocm-mi200
19:       - rocm-mi300
20:       - rocm-mi355
21:       - rocm-navi31
22:       - inductor-micro-benchmark
23:       - inductor-micro-benchmark-x86
24:       - inductor-cu124
25:       - inductor-rocm-mi200
26:       - inductor-rocm-mi300
27:       - mac-mps
28:       - linux-aarch64
29:     types:
30:       - completed
31: 
32: jobs:
33:   # the conclusion field in the github context is sometimes null
34:   # solution adapted from https://github.com/community/community/discussions/21090#discussioncomment-3226271
35:   get_workflow_conclusion:
36:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
37:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
38:     outputs:
39:       conclusion: ${{ fromJson(steps.get_conclusion.outputs.data).conclusion }}
40:     steps:
41:       - name: Get workflow run conclusion
42:         # TODO (huydhn): Pin this once https://github.com/octokit/request-action/issues/315 is resolved
43:         uses: octokit/request-action@05a2312de9f8207044c4c9e41fe19703986acc13 # v2.x
44:         id: get_conclusion
45:         with:
46:           route: GET /repos/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}/attempts/${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
47:         env:
48:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
49: 
50:   upload-test-stats:
51:     needs: get_workflow_conclusion
52:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
53:     runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
54:     environment: upload-stats
55:     permissions:
56:       id-token: write
57:     name: Upload test stats for ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}, attempt ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
58:     steps:
59:       - name: Print workflow information
60:         env:
61:           TRIGGERING_WORKFLOW: ${{ toJSON(github.event.workflow_run) }}
62:         run: echo "${TRIGGERING_WORKFLOW}"
63: 
64:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
65:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
66: 
67:       - name: Configure aws credentials
68:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
69:         continue-on-error: true
70:         with:
71:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-torch-test-stats
72:           aws-region: us-east-1
73: 
74:       - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
75:         with:
76:           python-version: '3.11'
77:           cache: pip
78: 
79:       - run: |
80:           pip3 install requests==2.32.2 boto3==1.35.42
81: 
82:       - name: Upload test artifacts
83:         id: upload-s3
84:         env:
85:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
86:           WORKFLOW_ARTIFACTS_URL: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.artifacts_url }}
87:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}
88:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
89:           REPO_FULLNAME: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.repository.full_name }}
90:         run: |
91:           echo "${WORKFLOW_ARTIFACTS_URL}"
92: 
93:           # Note that in the case of Linux and Windows, their artifacts have already been uploaded to S3, so there simply won't be
94:           # anything on GitHub to upload. The command should return right away
95:           python3 -m tools.stats.upload_artifacts --workflow-run-id "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ID}" --workflow-run-attempt "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT}" --repo "${REPO_FULLNAME}"
96: 
97:       - name: Upload test stats
98:         env:
99:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
100:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}
101:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
102:           WORKFLOW_URL: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.html_url }}
103:           HEAD_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_repository.full_name }}
104:           HEAD_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch }}
105:         run: |
106:           echo "${WORKFLOW_URL}"
107:           python3 -m tools.stats.upload_test_stats --workflow-run-id "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ID}" --workflow-run-attempt "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT}" --head-branch "${HEAD_BRANCH}" --head-repository "${HEAD_REPOSITORY}"
108:           python3 -m tools.stats.upload_sccache_stats --workflow-run-id "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ID}" --workflow-run-attempt "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT}"
109: 
110:       - name: Analyze disabled tests rerun
111:         env:
112:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
113:           WORKFLOW_ARTIFACTS_URL: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.artifacts_url }}
114:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}
115:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
116:           REPO_FULLNAME: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.repository.full_name }}
117:         run: |
118:           # Analyze the results from disable tests rerun and upload them to S3
119:           python3 -m tools.stats.check_disabled_tests --workflow-run-id "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ID}" --workflow-run-attempt "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT}" --repo "${REPO_FULLNAME}"
120: 
121:       - name: Upload gpt-fast benchmark results to s3
122:         if: steps.upload-s3.outcome && steps.upload-s3.outcome == 'success' && contains(github.event.workflow_run.name, 'inductor-micro-benchmark')
123:         env:
124:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}
125:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
126:           REPO_FULLNAME: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.repository.full_name }}
127:           HEAD_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch }}
128:         run: |
129:           python3 -m tools.stats.upload_dynamo_perf_stats --workflow-run-id "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ID}" --workflow-run-attempt "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT}" --repo "${REPO_FULLNAME}" --head-branch "${HEAD_BRANCH}" --dynamodb-table torchci-oss-ci-benchmark --match-filename "^gpt_fast_benchmark"
130: 
131:   check-api-rate:
132:     if: ${{ always() && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}
133:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
134:     continue-on-error: true
135:     steps:
136:       - name: Get our GITHUB_TOKEN API limit usage
137:         env:
138:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
139:         run: |
140:           curl -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" -H "Authorization: token $GITHUB_TOKEN" https://api.github.com/rate_limit
141: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/upload-test-stats.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: Upload torch dynamo performance stats
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_run:
5:     workflows: [inductor-A100-perf-nightly, inductor-perf-nightly-A10g, inductor-perf-nightly-aarch64, inductor-perf-nightly-x86, inductor-perf-nightly-macos, inductor-perf-nightly-rocm, inductor-perf-nightly-h100]
6:     types:
7:       - completed
8: 
9: jobs:
10:   get-conclusion:
11:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
12:     outputs:
13:       conclusion: ${{ fromJson(steps.get-conclusion.outputs.data).conclusion }}
14:     steps:
15:       - name: Get workflow run conclusion
16:         # TODO (huydhn): Pin this once https://github.com/octokit/request-action/issues/315 is resolved
17:         uses: octokit/request-action@05a2312de9f8207044c4c9e41fe19703986acc13 # v2.x
18:         id: get-conclusion
19:         with:
20:           route: GET /repos/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}/attempts/${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
21:         env:
22:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
23: 
24:   upload-perf-stats:
25:     needs: get-conclusion
26:     if: github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' || needs.get-conclusion.outputs.conclusion == 'success' ||
27:         github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'failure' || needs.get-conclusion.outputs.conclusion == 'failure'
28:     runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
29:     environment: upload-stats
30:     permissions:
31:       id-token: write
32:     name: Upload dynamo performance stats for ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}, attempt ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
33:     steps:
34:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
35:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
36:         with:
37:           submodules: false
38:           fetch-depth: 1
39: 
40:       - name: Configure aws credentials
41:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
42:         continue-on-error: true
43:         with:
44:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-torch-test-stats
45:           aws-region: us-east-1
46: 
47:       - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
48:         with:
49:           python-version: '3.11'
50:           cache: pip
51: 
52:       - run: |
53:           pip3 install requests==2.32.2 boto3==1.35.42
54: 
55:       - name: Upload torch dynamo performance stats to S3
56:         id: upload-s3
57:         env:
58:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
59:           WORKFLOW_ARTIFACTS_URL: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.artifacts_url }}
60:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}
61:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
62:           REPO_FULLNAME: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.repository.full_name }}
63:         run: |
64:           # Upload perf test reports from GHA to S3, which can now be downloaded
65:           # on HUD
66:           python3 -m tools.stats.upload_artifacts --workflow-run-id "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ID}" --workflow-run-attempt "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT}" --repo "${REPO_FULLNAME}"
67: 
68:       - name: Upload torch dynamo performance stats to s3
69:         if: steps.upload-s3.outcome && steps.upload-s3.outcome == 'success'
70:         env:
71:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.id }}
72:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.run_attempt }}
73:           REPO_FULLNAME: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.repository.full_name }}
74:           HEAD_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch }}
75:         run: |
76:           python3 -m tools.stats.upload_dynamo_perf_stats --workflow-run-id "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ID}" --workflow-run-attempt "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ATTEMPT}" --repo "${REPO_FULLNAME}" --head-branch "${HEAD_BRANCH}" --dynamodb-table torchci-dynamo-perf-stats --match-filename "^inductor_"
77: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/upload-torch-dynamo-perf-stats.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: Upload test stats intermediate
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_dispatch:
5:     inputs:
6:       workflow_id:
7:         description: workflow_id of the run

10: permissions:

15:     name: Intermediate upload test stats for ${{ inputs.workflow_id }}
16:     runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
17:     environment: upload-stats
18:     steps:
19:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
20:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
21:         with:
22:           fetch-depth: 1
23:           submodules: false
24: 
25:       - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
26:         with:
27:           python-version: '3.11'
28:           cache: pip
29: 
30:       - run: |
31:           pip3 install requests==2.32.2 boto3==1.35.42
32: 
33:       - name: Authenticate with AWS
34:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
35:         with:
36:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_upload_test_stats_intermediate_workflow

41:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
42:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{ inputs.workflow_id }}

45:             --workflow-run-id "${WORKFLOW_RUN_ID}"
46: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/upload_test_stats_intermediate.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: vllm-test
2: 
3: on:
4:   push:
5:     branches:
6:       - main
7:       - release/*
8:     tags:
9:       - ciflow/vllm/*
10:   workflow_dispatch:
11:   schedule:
12:     - cron: '0 */8 * * *'  # every 8 hours at minute 0 (UTC)
13: 
14: concurrency:
15:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}
16:   cancel-in-progress: true
17: 
18: permissions:
19:   id-token: write
20:   contents: read
21: 
22: jobs:
23:   get-label-type:
24:     name: get-label-type
25:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@main
26:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}
27:     with:
28:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
29:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
30:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
31:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
32:       opt_out_experiments: lf
33: 
34:   torch-build:
35:     name: ci-vllm-test
36:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml
37:     needs: get-label-type
38:     with:
39:       # When building vLLM, uv doesn't like that we rename wheel without changing the wheel metadata
40:       allow-reuse-old-whl: false
41:       build-additional-packages: "vision audio"
42:       build-external-packages: "vllm"
43:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.12-gcc11
44:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.12-gcc11-vllm
45:       cuda-arch-list: '8.0 8.9 9.0'
46:       runner: linux.24xlarge.memory
47:       test-matrix: |
48:         { include: [
49:           { config: "vllm_basic_correctness_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
50:           { config: "vllm_basic_models_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
51:           { config: "vllm_entrypoints_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1,runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
52:           { config: "vllm_regression_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
53:           { config: "vllm_multi_model_processor_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
54:           { config: "vllm_pytorch_compilation_unit_tests", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
55:           { config: "vllm_lora_28_failure_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
56:           { config: "vllm_multi_model_test_28_failure_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu"},
57:           { config: "vllm_language_model_test_extended_generation_28_failure_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu"},
58:           { config: "vllm_distributed_test_2_gpu_28_failure_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
59:           { config: "vllm_lora_test", shard: 0, num_shards: 4, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
60:           { config: "vllm_lora_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 4, runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
61:           { config: "vllm_lora_test", shard: 2, num_shards: 4,  runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
62:           { config: "vllm_lora_test", shard: 3, num_shards: 4,  runner: "linux.g6.4xlarge.experimental.nvidia.gpu" },
63:           { config: "vllm_lora_tp_test_distributed", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.12xlarge.nvidia.gpu"},
64:           { config: "vllm_distributed_test_28_failure_test", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "linux.g6.12xlarge.nvidia.gpu"}
65:         ]}
66:     secrets: inherit
67: 
68:   vllm-test-sm89:
69:       name: ci-vllm-test
70:       uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml
71:       needs: [
72:         torch-build,
73:       ]
74:       with:
75:         build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.12-gcc11
76:         docker-image: ${{ needs.torch-build.outputs.docker-image }}
77:         test-matrix: ${{ needs.torch-build.outputs.test-matrix }}
78:       secrets: inherit
79: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/vllm.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: xpu
2: 
3: on:
4:   push:
5:     tags:
6:       - ciflow/xpu/*
7:   workflow_dispatch:
8:   schedule:
9:     # Run 3 times on weekdays and less frequently on weekends.
10:     - cron: 45 0,8,16 * * 1-5
11:     - cron: 45 4 * * 0,6
12: 
13: concurrency:
14:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}
15:   cancel-in-progress: true
16: 
17: jobs:
18: 
19:   get-label-type:
20:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
21:     name: get-label-type
22:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@main
23:     with:
24:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
25:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
26:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
27:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
28: 
29:   linux-jammy-xpu-n-1-py3_10-build:
30:     name: linux-jammy-xpu-n-1-py3.10
31:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml
32:     needs: get-label-type
33:     with:
34:       sync-tag: linux-xpu-n-1-build
35:       runner_prefix: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}
36:       build-environment: linux-jammy-xpu-n-1-py3.10
37:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-xpu-n-1-py3
38:       runner: linux.c7i.12xlarge
39:       test-matrix: |
40:         { include: [
41:           { config: "default", shard: 1, num_shards: 6, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
42:           { config: "default", shard: 2, num_shards: 6, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
43:           { config: "default", shard: 3, num_shards: 6, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
44:           { config: "default", shard: 4, num_shards: 6, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
45:           { config: "default", shard: 5, num_shards: 6, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
46:           { config: "default", shard: 6, num_shards: 6, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
47:         ]}
48:     secrets: inherit
49: 
50:   linux-noble-xpu-n-py3_10-build:
51:     name: linux-noble-xpu-n-py3.10
52:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml
53:     needs: get-label-type
54:     with:
55:       sync-tag: linux-xpu-n-build
56:       runner_prefix: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}
57:       build-environment: linux-noble-xpu-n-py3.10
58:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-noble-xpu-n-py3
59:       runner: linux.c7i.12xlarge
60:       test-matrix: |
61:         { include: [
62:           { config: "default", shard: 1, num_shards: 12, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
63:           { config: "default", shard: 2, num_shards: 12, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
64:           { config: "default", shard: 3, num_shards: 12, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
65:           { config: "default", shard: 4, num_shards: 12, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
66:           { config: "default", shard: 5, num_shards: 12, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },
67:           { config: "default", shard: 6, num_shards: 12, runner: "linux.idc.xpu" },

{"content":"1: name: CI for distributed tests on B200
2: 
3: on:
4:   pull_request:
5:     paths:
6:       - .github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml
7:   workflow_dispatch:
8:   push:
9:     tags:
10:       - ciflow/b200-distributed/*
11:   schedule:
12:     - cron: 46 8 * * *  # about 1:46am PDT
13: 
14: concurrency:
15:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}
16:   cancel-in-progress: true
17: 
18: permissions:
19:   id-token: write
20:   contents: read
21: 
22: jobs:
23: 
24:   get-label-type:
25:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
26:     name: get-label-type
27:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@main
28:     with:
29:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
30:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
31:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
32:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
33: 
34:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200:
35:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200
36:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml
37:     needs: get-label-type
38:     with:
39:       runner_prefix: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}"
40:       runner: linux.r7i.4xlarge
41:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-distributed-b200
42:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11
43:       cuda-arch-list: '10.0'
44:       test-matrix: |
45:         { include: [
46:           { config: "distributed", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: "linux.dgx.b200.8" },
47:           { config: "distributed", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: "linux.dgx.b200.8" },
48:         ]}
49:     secrets: inherit
50: 
51:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-test-distributed-b200:
52:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-test-b200
53:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml
54:     needs:
55:       - linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200
56:     with:
57:       timeout-minutes: 1200
58:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200.outputs.build-environment }}
59:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200.outputs.docker-image }}
60:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200.outputs.test-matrix }}
61:       aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only
62:     secrets: inherit
63: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: Build almalinux docker images
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_dispatch:
5:   push:
6:     branches:
7:       - main
8:       - release/*
9:     tags:
10:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate or nightly builds
11:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1
12:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+
13:     paths:
14:       - .ci/docker/**
15:       - .github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml
16:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**
17:   pull_request:
18:     paths:
19:       - .ci/docker/**
20:       - .github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml
21:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**
22: 
23: env:
24:   DOCKER_REGISTRY: "docker.io"
25:   DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1
26:   WITH_PUSH: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) }}
27: 
28: concurrency:
29:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}
30:   cancel-in-progress: true
31: 
32: jobs:
33:   build-docker:
34:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
35:     environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'docker-build') || '' }}
36:     runs-on: linux.9xlarge.ephemeral
37:     strategy:
38:       matrix:
39:         tag: ["cuda12.6", "cuda12.8", "cuda12.9", "cuda13.0", "rocm7.0", "rocm7.1", "cpu"]
40:     steps:
41:       - name: Build docker image

{"content":"1: name: Build libtorch docker images
2: 
3: on:
4:   push:
5:     branches:
6:       - main
7:       - release/*
8:     tags:
9:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate or nightly builds
10:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1
11:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+
12:     paths:
13:       - .ci/docker/**
14:       - .github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml
15:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**
16:   pull_request:
17:     paths:
18:       - .ci/docker/**
19:       - .github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml
20:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**
21: 
22: env:
23:   DOCKER_REGISTRY: "docker.io"
24:   DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1
25:   WITH_PUSH: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) }}
26: 
27: concurrency:
28:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}
29:   cancel-in-progress: true
30: 
31: jobs:
32:   get-label-type:
33:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
34:     name: get-label-type
35:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@main
36:     with:
37:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
38:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
39:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
40:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
41: 
42:   build:
43:     environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'docker-build') || '' }}
44:     needs: get-label-type
45:     runs-on: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.9xlarge.ephemeral
46:     name: libtorch-cxx11-builder:${{ matrix.tag }}
47:     strategy:
48:       fail-fast: false
49:       matrix:
50:         include: [
51:           { tag: "cuda13.0" },
52:           { tag: "cuda12.9" },
53:           { tag: "cuda12.8" },
54:           { tag: "cuda12.6" },
55:           { tag: "rocm7.0"  },
56:           { tag: "rocm7.1"  },
57:           { tag: "cpu"      },
58:         ]
59:     steps:
60:       - name: Build docker image
61:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/binary-docker-build@main
62:         with:
63:           docker-image-name: libtorch-cxx11-builder
64:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ matrix.tag }}
65:           docker-build-dir: libtorch
66:           DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_TOKEN }}
67:           DOCKER_ID: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_ID }}
