{"content":"1: name: Build manywheel docker images for s390x
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_dispatch:
5:   push:
6:     tags:
7:       - ciflow/s390/*
8:     paths:
9:       - .github/workflows/build-manywheel-images-s390x.yml
10: 
11: 
12: env:
13:   DOCKER_REGISTRY: "docker.io"
14:   DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1
15:   WITH_PUSH: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) }}
16: 
17: concurrency:
18:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}
19:   cancel-in-progress: true
20: 
21: jobs:
22:   build-docker-cpu-s390x:
23:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
24:     environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'docker-build') || '' }}
25:     runs-on: linux.s390x
26:     steps:
27:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
28:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
29:         with:
30:           submodules: false
31:           no-sudo: true
32: 
33:       - name: Build Docker Image
34:         run: |
35:           .ci/docker/manywheel/build.sh manylinuxs390x-builder:cpu-s390x -t manylinuxs390x-builder:cpu-s390x
36: 
37:       - name: Tag and (if WITH_PUSH) push docker image to docker.io
38:         env:
39:           DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_TOKEN }}
40:           DOCKER_ID: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_ID }}
41:           CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME: manylinuxs390x-builder:cpu-s390x
42:         shell: bash
43:         run: |
44:           set -euox pipefail
45:           GITHUB_REF="${GITHUB_REF:-$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD || git describe --tags --exact-match)}"
46:           GIT_BRANCH_NAME="${GITHUB_REF##*/}"
47:           GIT_COMMIT_SHA="${GITHUB_SHA:-$(git rev-parse HEAD)}"
48:           CI_FOLDER_SHA="$(git rev-parse HEAD:.ci/docker)"
49: 
50:           DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX="docker.io/pytorch/${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME}"
51: 
52:           docker tag "${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME}" "${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_BRANCH_NAME}"
53:           docker tag "${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME}" "${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_COMMIT_SHA}"
54:           docker tag "${CREATED_FULL_DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME}" "${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${CI_FOLDER_SHA}"
55: 
56:           # Pretty sure Github will mask tokens and I'm not sure if it will even be
57:           # printed due to pipe, but just in case
58:           set +x
59:           if [[ "${WITH_PUSH:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
60:             echo "${DOCKER_TOKEN}" | docker login -u "${DOCKER_ID}" --password-stdin
61:             docker push "${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_BRANCH_NAME}"
62:             docker push "${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${GIT_COMMIT_SHA}"
63:             docker push "${DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME_PREFIX}-${CI_FOLDER_SHA}"
64:           fi
65: 
66:       - name: Cleanup docker
67:         if: cancelled()
68:         shell: bash
69:         run: |
70:           # If podman build command is interrupted,
71:           # it can leave a couple of processes still running.
72:           # Order them to stop for clean shutdown.
73:           # It looks like sometimes some processes remain
74:           # after first cleanup.
75:           # Wait a bit and do cleanup again. It looks like it helps.
76:           docker system prune --build -f || true
77:           sleep 60
78:           docker system prune --build -f || true
79: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/build-manywheel-images-s390x.yml"}

import json
with open('/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/320af8c1-2dd1-41cc-aa19-d3a83cbfd0b2/tool-results/mcp-sourcegraph-sg_compare_revisions-1773022223080.txt') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

# For files where added != deleted, show the full diff body
for file in data['files']:
    if file['added'] != file['deleted']:
        print(f"\n=== {file['path']} (+{file['added']} -{file['deleted']}) ===")
        for hunk in file['hunks']:
            print(hunk['body'])
EOF

/workflows/lint-bc.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/lint.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/linux-aarch64.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/mac-mps.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/nightly-s3-uploads.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/nightly.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/nitpicker.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/operator_benchmark.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/operator_microbenchmark.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/periodic-rocm-mi200.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/periodic-rocm-mi300.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/periodic.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/pull.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/quantization-periodic.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/revert.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/riscv64.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/rocm-mi200.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/rocm-mi300.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/rocm-mi355.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/rocm-navi31.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/runner-determinator-validator.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/runner_determinator_script_sync.yaml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/s390.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/s390x-periodic.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/scorecards.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/slow-rocm-mi200.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/slow.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/stale.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/target-determination-indexer.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/target_determination.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/test-b200.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/test-check-binary.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/test-h100.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/tools-unit-tests.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/torchbench.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/trunk-rocm-mi300.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/trunk-tagging.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/trunk.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/trymerge.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/tryrebase.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/unstable-periodic.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/unstable.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/update-viablestrict.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/update_pytorch_labels.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/upload-test-stats-while-running.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/upload-test-stats.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/upload-torch-dynamo-perf-stats.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/upload_test_stats_intermediate.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/vllm.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/weekly.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/win-arm64-build-test.yml"},{"isDirectory":false,"path":".github/workflows/xpu.yml"}]}

{"content":"1: {% import 'common.yml.j2' as common %}
2: {% import 'upload.yml.j2' as upload %}
3: 
4: {%- block name -%}
5: # Template is at:    .github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2
6: # Generation script: .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py
7: name: !{{ build_environment }}
8: {%- endblock %}
9: 
10: {%- macro set_runner_specific_vars() -%}
11:       # NOTE: These environment variables are put here so that they can be applied on every job equally
12:       #       They are also here because setting them at a workflow level doesn't give us access to the
13:       #       runner.temp variable, which we need.
14:       - name: Populate binary env
15:         shell: bash
16:         run: |
17:           # shellcheck disable=SC2129
18:           echo "BINARY_ENV_FILE=${RUNNER_TEMP}/env" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
19:           # shellcheck disable=SC2129
20:           echo "PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
21:           # shellcheck disable=SC2129
22:           echo "MAC_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
23: {%- endmacro %}
24: 
25: {%- macro setup_python(py_ver) -%}
26:       - name: Setup Python
27:         uses: actions/setup-python@v6
28:         with:
29:           # .4 version is min minor for 3.10, and also no-gil version of 3.13 needs at least 3.13.3
30:           python-version: "!{{ py_ver.strip('t') + ('.4' if '3.14' not in py_ver else '.0') }}"
31:           freethreaded: !{{ "true" if py_ver.endswith('t') else "false" }}
32: {%- endmacro %}
33: 
34: on:
35: # TODO: Migrate to new ciflow trigger, reference https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/70321
36:   push:
37:     # NOTE: Meta Employees can trigger new nightlies using: https://fburl.com/trigger_pytorch_nightly_build
38:     branches:
39:       - nightly
40:     tags:
41:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate builds
42:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1
43:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+
44: {%- for label in ciflow_config.labels | sort %}
45:     {%- if loop.first and branches != "nightly" %}
46:     tags:
47:     {%- endif %}
48:       - '!{{ label }}/*'
49: {%- endfor %}
50:   workflow_dispatch:
51: 
52: env:
53:   ALPINE_IMAGE: "308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine"
54:   AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
55:   BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: !{{ build_environment }}
56:   GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
57:   PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
58:   SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 0
59: !{{ common.concurrency(build_environment) }}
60: 
61: jobs:
62: {%- for config in build_configs %}
63:   !{{ config["build_name"] }}-build:
64:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}
65:     runs-on: !{{ macos_runner }}
66:     timeout-minutes: !{{ common.timeout_minutes }}
67:     !{{ upload.binary_env(config, true) }}
68:     {%- if config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements is defined and config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements|d('')|length > 0  %}
69:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: !{{ config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements }}
70:     {%- endif %}
71:     steps:
72:       !{{ set_runner_specific_vars() }}
73:       !{{ setup_python(config.get("python_version", "3.10")) }}
74:       !{{ common.checkout(deep_clone=False, directory="pytorch") }}
75:       - name: Populate binary env
76:         run: |
77:           "${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh"
78:       - name: Build PyTorch binary
79:         run: |
80:           set -eux -o pipefail
81:           # shellcheck disable=SC1090
82:           source "${BINARY_ENV_FILE:-/Users/distiller/project/env}"
83:           mkdir -p "$PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR"
84: 
85:           # Build
86:           USE_PYTORCH_METAL_EXPORT=1
87:           USE_COREML_DELEGATE=1
88:           TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME="${TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME//-/_}"
89:           export USE_PYTORCH_METAL_EXPORT
90:           export USE_COREML_DELEGATE
91:           export TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME
92:           "${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.ci/wheel/build_wheel.sh"
93: {%- if config["package_type"] == "wheel" %}
94:       - name: Test PyTorch wheel
95:         run: |
96:           set -eux -o pipefail
97:           # shellcheck disable=SC1090
98:           source "${BINARY_ENV_FILE:-/Users/distiller/project/env}"
99:           pip uninstall -y "$TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME" || true
100:           pip uninstall -y "$TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME" || true
101: 
102:           # Create new "clean" conda environment for testing
103: 
104:           SMOKE_TEST_PARAMS=""
105: 
106:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086
107:           python -mvenv test_venv
108:           source test_venv/bin/activate
109:           pip install "$PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR"/*.whl numpy -v
110: 
111:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086
112:           python "${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.ci/pytorch/smoke_test/smoke_test.py" --package torchonly ${SMOKE_TEST_PARAMS}
113: {%- endif %}
114:       - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4.4.0
115:         if: always()
116:         with:
117:           name: !{{ config["build_name"] }}
118:           retention-days: 14
119:           if-no-files-found: error
120:           path: "${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}"
121:   !{{ upload.upload_binaries(config, has_test=False, use_s3=False) }}
122: {%- endfor %}
123: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2"}

{"content":"1: name: Lint
2: # Workflow that runs lint checks and also unittests for tools, and scripts.
3: 
4: on:
5:   pull_request:
6:     branches-ignore:
7:       - nightly
8:   push:
9:     branches:
10:       - main
11:       - release/*
12:       - landchecks/*
13:     tags:
14:       - ciflow/pull/*
15:       - ciflow/trunk/*
16:   workflow_dispatch:
17: 
18: permissions: read-all
19: # The names of steps that actually test the code should be suffixed with `(nonretryable)`.
20: # When any other step fails, it's job will be retried once by retryBot.
21: jobs:
22:   get-label-type:
23:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
24:     name: get-label-type
25:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@main
26:     with:
27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
30: 
31:   get-changed-files:
32:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
33:     name: Get changed files
34:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_get-changed-files.yml
35:     with:
36:       all_files: ${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'lint-all-files') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'Reverted') || github.event_name == 'push' }}
37: 
38:   lintrunner-clang:
39:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
40:     # Needed to prevent deduping on HUD
41:     name: lintrunner-clang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
42:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
43:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to clangtidy / clangformat
44:     if: |
45:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (
46:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||
47:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.h') ||
48:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cpp') ||
49:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cc') ||
50:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cxx') ||
51:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hpp') ||
52:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hxx') ||
53:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cu') ||
54:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cuh') ||
55:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.mm') ||
56:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.metal')
57:       )
58:     with:
59:       timeout: 120
60:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
61:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-linter
62:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
63:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
64:       fetch-depth: 0
65:       submodules: true
66:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
67:       script: |
68:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
69:         if [ "$CHANGED_FILES" = "*" ]; then
70:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT --all-files"
71:         else
72:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT $CHANGED_FILES"
73:         fi
74:         export CLANG=1
75:         .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
76: 
77:   # NOTE: mypy needs its own job because it depends on --all-files, without assessing all files it sometimes
78:   #       fails to find types when it should
79:   # NOTE: We should be able to disable this and consolidate with Pyrefly
80:   lintrunner-pyrefly:
81:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
82:     name: lintrunner-pyrefly-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
83:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
84:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to pyrefly
85:     if: |
86:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (
87:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||
88:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.py') ||
89:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.pyi')
90:       )
91:     with:
92:       timeout: 120
93:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
94:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
95:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
96:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
97:       fetch-depth: 0
98:       submodules: true
99:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
100:       script: |
101:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
102:         echo "Running pyrefly"
103:         ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take PYREFLY --all-files" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
104: 
105:   lintrunner-noclang:
106:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
107:     name: lintrunner-noclang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
108:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
109:     with:
110:       timeout: 120
111:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
112:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
113:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
114:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
115:       fetch-depth: 0
116:       submodules: true
117:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
118:       script: |
119:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
120:         echo "Running all other linters"
121:         if [ "$CHANGED_FILES" = '*' ]; then
122:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY --all-files" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
123:         else
124:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY ${CHANGED_FILES}" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
125:         fi
126: 
127:   quick-checks:
128:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
129:     needs: get-label-type
130:     with:
131:       timeout: 120
132:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
133:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
134:       fetch-depth: 0
135:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
136:       script: |
137:         # Ensure no non-breaking spaces
138:         # NB: We use 'printf' below rather than '\u000a' since bash pre-4.2
139:         # does not support the '\u000a' syntax (which is relevant for local linters)
140:         (! git --no-pager grep -In "$(printf '\xC2\xA0')" -- . || (echo "The above lines have non-breaking spaces (U+00A0); please convert them to spaces (U+0020)"; false))
141: 
142:         # Ensure cross-OS compatible file names
143:         (! git ls-files | grep -E '([<>:"|?*]|[ .]$)' || (echo "The above file names are not valid across all operating systems. Please ensure they don't contain the characters '<>:""|?*' and don't end with a white space or a '.' "; false))
144: 
145:         # Ensure no versionless Python shebangs
146:         (! git --no-pager grep -In '#!.*python$' -- . || (echo "The above lines have versionless Python shebangs; please specify either python2 or python3"; false))
147: 
148:         # Ensure ciflow tags mentioned in config
149:         python3 .github/scripts/collect_ciflow_labels.py --validate-tags
150: 
151:         # C++ docs check
152:         pushd docs/cpp/source
153:         ./check-doxygen.sh
154:         popd
155: 
156:         # CUDA kernel launch check
157:         set -eux
158:         python3 torch/testing/_internal/check_kernel_launches.py |& tee cuda_kernel_launch_checks.txt
159: 
160:   pr-sanity-checks:
161:     name: pr-sanity-checks
162:     needs: get-label-type
163:     runs-on: [self-hosted, "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.large"]
164:     # Only run this on pull requests. This check is simple enough to be done without a Docker image
165:     if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-pr-sanity-checks')
166:     steps:
167:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
168:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
169:         with:
170:           submodules: false
171:           fetch-depth: -1
172: 
173:       - name: PR size check (nonretryable)
174:         env:
175:           BASE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
176:           HEAD: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
177:         run: |
178:           bash .github/scripts/pr-sanity-check.sh
179: 
180:   workflow-checks:
181:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
182:     needs: get-label-type
183:     with:
184:       timeout: 120
185:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
186:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
187:       fetch-depth: -1
188:       submodules: true
189:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
190:       script: |
191:         # Regenerate workflows
192:         .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py
193: 
194:         RC=0
195:         # Assert that regenerating the workflows didn't change them
196:         if ! .github/scripts/report_git_status.sh .github/workflows; then
197:           echo
198:           echo 'As shown by the above diff, the committed .github/workflows'
199:           echo 'are not up to date according to .github/templates.'
200:           echo 'Please run this command, commit, and push again to your PR:'
201:           echo
202:           echo '    .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py'
203:           echo
204:           echo 'If running that command does nothing, you may need to rebase'
205:           echo 'onto a more recent commit from the PyTorch main branch.'
206:           RC=1
207:         fi
208: 
209:         # Check that jobs will be cancelled
210:         .github/scripts/ensure_actions_will_cancel.py
211: 
212:         exit $RC

214:   toc:
215:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
216:     needs: get-label-type
217:     with:
218:       timeout: 120
219:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
220:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
221:       fetch-depth: 0
222:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
223:       script: |
224:         # Regenerate ToCs and check that they didn't change
225:         set -eu
226: 
227:         export PATH=~/.npm-global/bin:"$PATH"
228:         for FILE in $(git grep -Il '<!-- toc -->' -- '**.md'); do
229:           markdown-toc --bullets='-' -i "$FILE"
230:         done
231: 
232:         if ! .github/scripts/report_git_status.sh .; then
233:           echo
234:           echo 'As shown by the above diff, the table of contents in one or'
235:           echo 'more Markdown files is not up to date with the file contents.'
236:           echo 'You can either apply that Git diff directly to correct the'
237:           echo 'table of contents, or if you have npm installed, you can'
238:           echo 'install the npm package markdown-toc and run the following'
239:           # shellcheck disable=SC2016
240:           echo 'command (replacing $FILE with the filename for which you want'
241:           echo 'to regenerate the table of contents):'
242:           echo
243:           # shellcheck disable=SC2016
244:           echo "    markdown-toc --bullets='-' -i \"\$FILE\""
245:           false
246:         fi
247: 
248:   test-tools:
249:     name: Test tools
250:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}
251:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
252:     needs: get-label-type
253:     with:
254:       timeout: 120
255:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
256:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
257:       fetch-depth: 0
258:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
259:       script: |
260:         # Test tools
261:         PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) pytest tools/stats
262:         PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) pytest tools/test -o "python_files=test*.py"
263:         PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) pytest .github/scripts -o "python_files=test*.py"
264: 
265:   test_run_test:
266:     name: Test `run_test.py` is usable without boto3
267:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}
268:     runs-on: linux.24_04.4x
269:     steps:
270:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
271:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
272:         with:
273:           submodules: false
274:           fetch-depth: 1
275:       - name: Setup Python 3.10
276:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
277:         with:
278:           python-version: '3.10'
279:           architecture: x64
280:           cache: pip
281:       - name: Install dependencies
282:         run: |
283:           python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip
284:           pip install pytest-rerunfailures==11.1.* pytest-flakefinder==1.1.* pytest-xdist==3.3.* expecttest==0.3.* fbscribelogger==0.1.* numpy==1.24.*
285:           pip install torch --pre --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly/cpu/
286:       - name: Run run_test.py (nonretryable)
287:         run: |
288:           # Run test_vulkan, which is a fast noop on Linux
289:           python3 test/run_test.py --include test_vulkan --verbose
290: 
291:   test_collect_env:
292:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}
293:     name: Test collect_env
294:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
295:     strategy:
296:       matrix:
297:         include:
298:           - test_type: with_torch
299:             runner: linux.24_04.4x
300:           - test_type: without_torch
301:             runner: linux.24_04.4x
302:           - test_type: older_python_version
303:             runner: linux.24_04.4x
304:     steps:
305:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
306:       # deep clone (fetch-depth 0) required, to allow us to use git log
307:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
308:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
309:         with:
310:           submodules: false
311:           fetch-depth: 1
312:       - name: Get min python version
313:         id: get-min-python-version
314:         if: matrix.test_type == 'older_python_version'
315:         run: |
316:           set -eou pipefail
317:           # Generate PyTorch version to use
318:           echo "MIN_PYTHON_VERSION=$(python3 .github/scripts/get_ci_variable.py --min-python-version)" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
319:       - name: Setup Old Python version
320:         if: matrix.test_type == 'older_python_version'
321:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
322:         with:
323:           python-version: 3.8
324:           architecture: x64
325:           check-latest: false
326:           cache: pip
327:           cache-dependency-path: |
328:             **/requirements-build.txt
329:             **/requirements.txt
330:       - name: Setup Min Python version
331:         if: matrix.test_type != 'older_python_version'
332:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
333:         with:
334:           python-version: ${{ steps.get-min-python-version.outputs.MIN_PYTHON_VERSION }}
335:           architecture: x64
336:           check-latest: false
337:           cache: pip
338:           cache-dependency-path: |
339:             **/requirements-build.txt
340:             **/requirements.txt
341:       - name: Install torch
342:         if: matrix.test_type == 'with_torch'
343:         run: |
344:           pip install -r requirements.txt
345:           # Doesn't really matter what torch version, we just need ANY torch installed
346:           pip install 'torch==2.*'
347:       - name: Run collect_env.py (nonretryable)
348:         run: |
349:           # All we need to see is that it passes
350:           python3 torch/utils/collect_env.py

{"content":"1: name: Lint
2: # Workflow that runs lint checks and also unittests for tools, and scripts.
3: 
4: on:
5:   pull_request:
6:     branches-ignore:
7:       - nightly
8:   push:
9:     branches:
10:       - main
11:       - release/*
12:       - landchecks/*
13:     tags:
14:       - ciflow/pull/*
15:       - ciflow/trunk/*
16:   workflow_dispatch:
17: 
18: permissions: read-all
19: # The names of steps that actually test the code should be suffixed with `(nonretryable)`.
20: # When any other step fails, it's job will be retried once by retryBot.
21: jobs:
22:   get-label-type:
23:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
24:     name: get-label-type
25:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10
26:     with:
27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
30: 
31:   get-changed-files:
32:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
33:     name: Get changed files
34:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_get-changed-files.yml
35:     with:
36:       all_files: ${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'lint-all-files') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'Reverted') || github.event_name == 'push' }}
37: 
38:   lintrunner-clang:
39:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10
40:     # Needed to prevent deduping on HUD
41:     name: lintrunner-clang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
42:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
43:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to clangtidy / clangformat
44:     if: |
45:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (
46:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||
47:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.h') ||
48:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cpp') ||
49:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cc') ||
50:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cxx') ||
51:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hpp') ||
52:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hxx') ||
53:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cu') ||
54:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cuh') ||
55:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.mm') ||
56:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.metal')
57:       )
58:     with:
59:       timeout: 120
60:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
61:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-linter
62:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
63:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
64:       fetch-depth: 0
65:       submodules: true
66:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
67:       script: |
68:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
69:         if [ "$CHANGED_FILES" = "*" ]; then
70:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT --all-files"
71:         else
72:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT $CHANGED_FILES"
73:         fi
74:         export CLANG=1
75:         .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
76: 
77:   # NOTE: mypy needs its own job because it depends on --all-files, without assessing all files it sometimes
78:   #       fails to find types when it should
79:   # NOTE: We should be able to disable this and consolidate with Pyrefly
80:   lintrunner-pyrefly:
81:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10
82:     name: lintrunner-pyrefly-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
83:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
84:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to pyrefly
85:     if: |
86:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (
87:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||
88:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.py') ||
89:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.pyi')
90:       )
91:     with:
92:       timeout: 120
93:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
94:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
95:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
96:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
97:       fetch-depth: 0
98:       submodules: true
99:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
100:       script: |
101:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
102:         echo "Running pyrefly"
103:         ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take PYREFLY --all-files" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
104: 
105:   lintrunner-noclang:
106:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10
107:     name: lintrunner-noclang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
108:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
109:     with:
110:       timeout: 120
111:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
112:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
113:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
114:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
115:       fetch-depth: 0
116:       submodules: true
117:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
118:       script: |
119:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
120:         echo "Running all other linters"
121:         if [ "$CHANGED_FILES" = '*' ]; then
122:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY --all-files" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
123:         else
124:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY ${CHANGED_FILES}" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
125:         fi
126: 
127:   quick-checks:
128:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10
129:     needs: get-label-type
130:     with:
131:       timeout: 120
132:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
133:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
134:       fetch-depth: 0
135:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
136:       script: |
137:         # Ensure no non-breaking spaces
138:         # NB: We use 'printf' below rather than '\u000a' since bash pre-4.2
139:         # does not support the '\u000a' syntax (which is relevant for local linters)
140:         (! git --no-pager grep -In "$(printf '\xC2\xA0')" -- . || (echo "The above lines have non-breaking spaces (U+00A0); please convert them to spaces (U+0020)"; false))
141: 
142:         # Ensure cross-OS compatible file names
143:         (! git ls-files | grep -E '([<>:"|?*]|[ .]$)' || (echo "The above file names are not valid across all operating systems. Please ensure they don't contain the characters '<>:""|?*' and don't end with a white space or a '.' "; false))
144: 
145:         # Ensure no versionless Python shebangs
146:         (! git --no-pager grep -In '#!.*python$' -- . || (echo "The above lines have versionless Python shebangs; please specify either python2 or python3"; false))
147: 
148:         # Ensure ciflow tags mentioned in config
149:         python3 .github/scripts/collect_ciflow_labels.py --validate-tags
150: 
151:         # C++ docs check
152:         pushd docs/cpp/source
153:         ./check-doxygen.sh
154:         popd
155: 
156:         # CUDA kernel launch check
157:         set -eux
158:         python3 torch/testing/_internal/check_kernel_launches.py |& tee cuda_kernel_launch_checks.txt
159: 
160:   pr-sanity-checks:
161:     name: pr-sanity-checks
162:     needs: get-label-type
163:     runs-on: [self-hosted, "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.large"]
164:     # Only run this on pull requests. This check is simple enough to be done without a Docker image
165:     if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-pr-sanity-checks')
166:     steps:
167:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
168:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10
169:         with:
170:           submodules: false
171:           fetch-depth: -1
172: 
173:       - name: PR size check (nonretryable)
174:         env:
175:           BASE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
176:           HEAD: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
177:         run: |
178:           bash .github/scripts/pr-sanity-check.sh
179: 
180:   workflow-checks:
181:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10
182:     needs: get-label-type
183:     with:
184:       timeout: 120
185:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
186:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
187:       fetch-depth: -1
188:       submodules: true
189:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
190:       script: |
191:         # Regenerate workflows
192:         export RELEASE_VERSION_TAG=2.10
193:         .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py
194: 
195:         RC=0
196:         # Assert that regenerating the workflows didn't change them
197:         if ! .github/scripts/report_git_status.sh .github/workflows; then
198:           echo
199:           echo 'As shown by the above diff, the committed .github/workflows'
200:           echo 'are not up to date according to .github/templates.'
201:           echo 'Please run this command, commit, and push again to your PR:'
202:           echo
203:           echo '    .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py'
204:           echo
205:           echo 'If running that command does nothing, you may need to rebase'
206:           echo 'onto a more recent commit from the PyTorch main branch.'
207:           RC=1
208:         fi
209: 
210:         # Check that jobs will be cancelled
211:         .github/scripts/ensure_actions_will_cancel.py
212: 
213:         exit $RC

306:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]

319:           echo "MIN_PYTHON_VERSION=$(python3 .github/scripts/get_ci_variable.py --min-python-version)" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
320:       - name: Setup Old Python version
321:         if: matrix.test_type == 'older_python_version'
322:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
323:         with:
324:           python-version: 3.8
325:           architecture: x64
326:           check-latest: false
327:           cache: pip
328:           cache-dependency-path: |
329:             **/requirements-build.txt
330:             **/requirements.txt
331:       - name: Setup Min Python version
332:         if: matrix.test_type != 'older_python_version'
333:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0
334:         with:
335:           python-version: ${{ steps.get-min-python-version.outputs.MIN_PYTHON_VERSION }}
336:           architecture: x64
337:           check-latest: false
338:           cache: pip
339:           cache-dependency-path: |
340:             **/requirements-build.txt
341:             **/requirements.txt
342:       - name: Install torch
343:         if: matrix.test_type == 'with_torch'
344:         run: |
345:           pip install -r requirements.txt
346:           # Doesn't really matter what torch version, we just need ANY torch installed
347:           pip install 'torch==2.*'
348:       - name: Run collect_env.py (nonretryable)
349:         run: |
350:           # All we need to see is that it passes
351:           python3 torch/utils/collect_env.py
352: 
353:   link-check:
354:     name: Link checks
355:     needs: get-label-type
356:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_link_check.yml
357:     with:
358:       runner: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}
359:       ref:    ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
360:     secrets: inherit
361: 
362: concurrency:
363:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}
364:   cancel-in-progress: true

{"content":"1: name: bazel
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       docker-image-name:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Name of the base docker image to build with.
14:       cuda-version:
15:         required: true
16:         type: string
17:         description: What CUDA version to build with (i.e. "11.7"), "cpu" for none.
18:       sync-tag:
19:         required: false
20:         type: string
21:         default: ""
22:         description: |
23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
25:       test-matrix:
26:         required: true
27:         type: string
28:         description: |
29:           A JSON description of what configs to run later on.
30:       runner:
31:         required: false
32:         type: string
33:         default: "linux.large"
34:         description: Runner type
35: 
36: env:
37:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
38: 
39: jobs:
40:   filter:
41:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
42:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner }}
43:     outputs:
44:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}
45:       is-test-matrix-empty: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty }}
46:       keep-going: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.keep-going }}
47:       reenabled-issues: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.reenabled-issues }}
48:     steps:
49:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
50:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
51:         with:
52:           fetch-depth: 1
53:           submodules: false
54: 
55:       - name: Select all requested test configurations
56:         id: filter
57:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
58:         with:
59:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
60:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
61: 
62:   build-and-test:
63:     needs: filter
64:     # Don't run on forked repos.
65:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && needs.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False'
66:     strategy:
67:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(needs.filter.outputs.test-matrix) }}
68:       fail-fast: false
69:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
70:     steps:
71:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
72:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
73:         with:
74:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
75: 
76:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
77:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
78:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
79: 
80:       - name: Setup Linux
81:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
82: 
83:       - name: Calculate docker image
84:         id: calculate-docker-image
85:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
86:         with:
87:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}
88: 
89:       - name: Pull docker image
90:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
91:         with:
92:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
93: 
94:       - name: Check if in a container runner
95:         shell: bash
96:         id: check_container_runner
97:         run: echo "IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
98: 
99:       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG
100:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main
101: 
102:       - name: Output disk space left
103:         run: |
104:           sudo df -H
105: 
106:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker
107:         run: |
108:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
109:           env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
110: 
111:       - name: Parse ref
112:         id: parse-ref
113:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
114: 
115:       - name: Get workflow job id
116:         id: get-job-id
117:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
118:         if: always()
119:         with:
120:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
121: 
122:       - name: Build
123:         env:
124:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
125:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
126:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
127:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
128:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
129:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
130:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
131:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
132:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
133:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
134:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ needs.filter.outputs.reenabled-issues }}
135:           # TODO duplicated
136:           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
137:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
138:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2
139:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1
140:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: 5.2
141:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
142:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
143:           CUDA_VERSION: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}
144:         run: |
145:           export SHARD_NUMBER=0
146:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
147:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase
148:           # Make sure we copy test results from bazel-testlogs symlink to
149:           # a regular directory ./test/test-reports
150:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086
151:           container_name=$(docker run \
152:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \
153:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \
154:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
155:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
156:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \
157:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \
158:             -e GITHUB_JOB \
159:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \
160:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
161:             -e JOB_ID \
162:             -e GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH="$GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH" \
163:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \
164:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \
165:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
166:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \
167:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \
168:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \
169:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \
170:             -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \
171:             -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \
172:             -e CUDA_VERSION \
173:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
174:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
175:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
176:             --shm-size="1g" \
177:             --tty \
178:             --detach \
179:             --user jenkins \
180:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
181:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
182:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
183:           )
184:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'
185:           echo "container_id=${container_name}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
186: 
187:       - name: Test
188:         id: test
189:         # Time out the test phase after 3.5 hours
190:         timeout-minutes: 120
191:         run: |
192:           docker exec -t "${container_id}" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/test.sh && cp -Lr ./bazel-testlogs ./test/test-reports'
193: 
194:       - name: Print remaining test logs
195:         shell: bash
196:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion
197:         run: |
198:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true
199: 
200:       - name: Chown workspace
201:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace
202:         if: always()
203: 
204:       - name: Upload test artifacts
205:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts
206:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'
207:         with:
208:           file-suffix: bazel-${{ github.job }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
209: 
210:       - name: Teardown Linux
211:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
212:         if: always()
213: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: linux-binary-build
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build_name:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: The build's name
10:       build_environment:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: The build environment
14:       runner_prefix:
15:         required: false
16:         default: ""
17:         type: string
18:         description: prefix for runner label
19:       runs_on:
20:         required: false
21:         default: linux.12xlarge.memory.ephemeral
22:         type: string
23:         description: Hardware to run this "build" job on, linux.12xlarge or linux.arm64.2xlarge.
24:       timeout-minutes:
25:         required: false
26:         default: 240
27:         type: number
28:         description: timeout for the job
29:       ALPINE_IMAGE:
30:         required: false
31:         type: string
32:         default: "308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine"
33:         description: Alpine image to use
34:       PYTORCH_ROOT:
35:         required: true
36:         type: string
37:         description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository
38:       PACKAGE_TYPE:
39:         required: true
40:         type: string
41:         description: Package type
42:       DESIRED_CUDA:
43:         required: true
44:         type: string
45:         description: Desired Cuda version
46:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION:
47:         required: false
48:         type: string
49:         description: GPU Arch version
50:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE:
51:         required: true
52:         type: string
53:         description: GPU Arch type
54:       DOCKER_IMAGE:
55:         required: true
56:         type: string
57:         description: Docker image to use
58:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:
59:         required: true
60:         type: string
61:         description: Docker image tag to use
62:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG:
63:         required: false
64:         type: string
65:         description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)
66:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT:
67:         required: false
68:         type: string
69:         description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)
70:       DESIRED_PYTHON:
71:         required: false
72:         type: string
73:         description: Desired python version
74:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS:
75:         required: false
76:         type: string
77:         description: Extra install requirements
78:         default: ""
79:     secrets:
80:       github-token:
81:         required: true
82:         description: Github Token
83: 
84: permissions:
85:   id-token: write
86: 
87: jobs:
88:   build:
89:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runs_on }}
90:     timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}
91:     env:
92:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_ROOT }}
93:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}
94:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in
95:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION
96:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}
97:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}
98:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}
99:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
100:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1
101:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}
102:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}
103:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}
104:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}
105:       ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
106:       AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
107:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env
108:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build_environment }}
109:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
110:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
111:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts
112:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
113:     steps:
114:       - name: Make the env permanent during this workflow (but not the secrets)
115:         shell: bash
116:         run: |
117:           {
118:             echo "PYTORCH_ROOT=${{ env.PYTORCH_ROOT }}"
119:             echo "PACKAGE_TYPE=${{ env.PACKAGE_TYPE }}"
120:             echo "DESIRED_CUDA=${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}"
121:             echo "GPU_ARCH_VERSION=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}"
122:             echo "GPU_ARCH_TYPE=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}"
123:             echo "DOCKER_IMAGE=${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}"
124:             echo "SKIP_ALL_TESTS=${{ env.SKIP_ALL_TESTS }}"
125:             echo "LIBTORCH_CONFIG=${{ env.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}"
126:             echo "LIBTORCH_VARIANT=${{ env.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}"
127:             echo "DESIRED_PYTHON=${{ env.DESIRED_PYTHON }}"
128:             echo "PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS=${{ env.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}"
129:             echo "ALPINE_IMAGE=${{ env.ALPINE_IMAGE }}"
130:             echo "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${{ env.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION }}"
131:             echo "BINARY_ENV_FILE=${{ env.BINARY_ENV_FILE }}"
132:             echo "BUILD_ENVIRONMENT=${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}"
133:             echo "BUILD_NAME=${{ env.BUILD_NAME }}"
134:             echo "PR_NUMBER=${{ env.PR_NUMBER }}"
135:             echo "PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}"
136:             echo "SHA1=${{ env.SHA1 }}"
137:           } >> "${GITHUB_ENV} }}"
138: 
139:       - name: List the env
140:         shell: bash
141:         run: env
142: 
143:       - name: "[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)"
144:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
145:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
146:         continue-on-error: true
147:         with:
148:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
149: 
150:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
151:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
152:         with:
153:           no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
154: 
155:       - name: Setup Linux
156:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
157:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
158: 
159:       - name: Chown workspace
160:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
161:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace
162:         with:
163:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
164: 
165:       - name: Clean workspace
166:         shell: bash
167:         run: |
168:           set -eux
169: 
170:           rm -rf "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
171:           mkdir "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
172: 
173:           if [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' ]] || [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' ]] ; then
174:             rm -rf "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts"
175:             mkdir "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts"
176:           fi
177: 
178:       - name: Checkout PyTorch to pytorch dir
179:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
180:         with:
181:           ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
182:           submodules: recursive
183:           path: pytorch
184:           show-progress: false
185: 
186:       - name: Clean PyTorch checkout
187:         run: |
188:           # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts
189:           git clean -fxd
190:         working-directory: pytorch
191: 
192:       - name: Check if the job is disabled
193:         id: filter
194:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs
195:         with:
196:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
197:           # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the
198:           # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled
199:           test-matrix: |
200:             { include: [
201:               { config: "default" },
202:             ]}
203: 
204:       - name: configure aws credentials
205:         id: aws_creds
206:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}
207:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
208:         with:
209:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only
210:           aws-region: us-east-1
211:           role-duration-seconds: 18000
212: 
213:       - name: Calculate docker image
214:         id: calculate-docker-image
215:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
216:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
217:         with:
218:           # If doing this in main or release branch, use docker.io. Otherwise
219:           # use ECR
220:           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}
221:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
222:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}
223:           # The build.sh script in this folder is not actually the correct one,
224:           # this is just needed for sha calculation
225:           docker-build-dir: .ci/docker
226:           working-directory: pytorch
227: 
228:       - name: Pull Docker image
229:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
230:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
231:         with:
232:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
233: 
234:       - name: Build PyTorch binary
235:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
236:         env:
237:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image || format('{0}:{1}', inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE, inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX) }}
238:         run: |
239:           set -x
240:           mkdir -p artifacts/
241:           container_name=$(docker run \
242:             -e BINARY_ENV_FILE \
243:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
244:             -e DESIRED_CUDA \
245:             -e DESIRED_PYTHON \
246:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
247:             -e GPU_ARCH_TYPE \
248:             -e GPU_ARCH_VERSION \
249:             -e LIBTORCH_VARIANT \
250:             -e PACKAGE_TYPE \
251:             -e PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR \
252:             -e PYTORCH_ROOT \
253:             -e SKIP_ALL_TESTS \
254:             -e PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS \
255:             --tty \
256:             --detach \
257:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/pytorch:/pytorch" \
258:             -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/artifacts" \
259:             -w / \
260:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
261:           )
262:           docker exec -t -w "${PYTORCH_ROOT}" "${container_name}" bash -c "bash .circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh"
263:           # Unified build script for all architectures (x86_64, aarch64, s390x)
264:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" bash -c "source ${BINARY_ENV_FILE} && bash /pytorch/.ci/${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}/build.sh"
265: 
266:       - name: Chown artifacts
267:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
268:         shell: bash
269:         run: |
270:           # Ensure the working directory gets chowned back to the current user
271:           docker run --rm -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/v" -w /v "${ALPINE_IMAGE}" chown -R "$(id -u):$(id -g)" .
272: 
273:       - uses: actions/upload-artifact@50769540e7f4bd5e21e526ee35c689e35e0d6874 # v4.4.0
274:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
275:         with:
276:           name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}
277:           if-no-files-found: error
278:           path:
279:             ${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/*
280: 
281:       - name: Teardown Linux
282:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
283:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
284: 
285:       - name: Chown workspace
286:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
287:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/chown-workspace
288:         with:
289:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
290: 
291:       - name: Cleanup docker
292:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
293:         shell: bash
294:         run: |
295:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop
296:           # ignore expansion of "docker ps -q" since it could be empty
297:           # shellcheck disable=SC2046
298:           docker stop $(docker ps -q) || true
