{"content":"1: # TODO: this looks sort of similar to _linux-test, but there are like a dozen
2: # places where you would have to insert an if statement. Probably it's better to
3: # just use a different workflow altogether
4: 
5: name: test
6: 
7: on:
8:   workflow_call:
9:     inputs:
10:       build-environment:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
14:       test-matrix:
15:         required: true
16:         type: string
17:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.
18:       docker-image:
19:         required: true
20:         type: string
21:         description: Docker image to run in.
22:       sync-tag:
23:         required: false
24:         type: string
25:         default: ""
26:         description: |
27:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
28:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
29:       timeout-minutes:
30:         required: false
31:         type: number
32:         default: 300
33:         description: |
34:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
35:       tests-to-include:
36:         required: false
37:         type: string
38:         default: ""
39:         description: |
40:           List of tests to include (empty string implies default list)
41:       dashboard-tag:
42:         required: false
43:         type: string
44:         default: ""
45:       disable-monitor:
46:         description: |
47:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.
48:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,
49:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.
50:         required: false
51:         type: boolean
52:         default: true
53:       monitor-log-interval:
54:         description: |
55:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
56:         required: false
57:         type: number
58:         default: 5
59:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
60:         description: |
61:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
62:         required: false
63:         type: number
64:         default: 1
65:     secrets:
66:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
67:         required: false
68:         description: |
69:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
70: env:
71:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
72: 
73: permissions:
74:   id-token: write
75:   contents: read
76: 
77: jobs:
78:   test:
79:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix
80:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'
81:     strategy:
82:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}
83:       fail-fast: false
84:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
85:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
86:     steps:
87:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
88:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
89:         with:
90:           no-sudo: true
91: 
92:       - name: Setup ROCm
93:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-rocm
94: 
95:       - name: Runner check GPU count (distributed jobs)
96:         if: ${{ contains(matrix.config, 'distributed') }}
97:         shell: bash
98:         run: |
99:           ngpu=$(rocminfo | grep -c -E 'Name:.*\sgfx')
100:           if [[ $ngpu -lt 2 ]]; then #We are temporarily reducing this down to 2 from 4 so that we can run tests on nodes with less gpus.
101:             echo "Error: only $ngpu GPU(s) detected, at least 2 GPUs are needed for distributed jobs"
102:             exit 1
103:           fi
104: 
105:       - name: Calculate docker image
106:         id: calculate-docker-image
107:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
108:         with:
109:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
110: 
111:       - name: Pull docker image
112:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
113:         with:
114:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
115: 
116:       - name: Get workflow job id
117:         id: get-job-id
118:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
119:         if: always()
120:         with:
121:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
122: 
123:       - name: Start monitoring script
124:         id: monitor-script
125:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
126:         shell: bash
127:         continue-on-error: true
128:         env:
129:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
130:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
131:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
132:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
133:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
134:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
135:         run: |
136:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7
137:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &
138:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
139: 
140:       - name: Download build artifacts
141:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
142:         with:
143:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
144: 
145:       - name: Download TD artifacts
146:         continue-on-error: true
147:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts
148: 
149:       - name: Parse ref
150:         id: parse-ref
151:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
152: 
153:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues
154:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently
155:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do
156:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.
157:         id: keep-going
158:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
159:         with:
160:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
161:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
162:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
163: 
164:       - name: Set Test step time
165:         id: test-timeout
166:         shell: bash
167:         env:
168:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
169:         run: |
170:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
171: 
172:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker
173:         shell: bash
174:         run: |
175:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
176:           env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
177: 
178:       - name: Test
179:         id: test
180:         env:
181:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
182:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
183:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
184:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
185:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
186:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
187:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
188:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
189:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
190:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
191:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
192:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
193:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}
194:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}
195:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}
196:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}
197:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}
198:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}
199:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}
200:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}
201:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}
202:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}
203:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
204:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}
205:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}
206:           TESTS_TO_INCLUDE: ${{ inputs.tests-to-include }}
207:           DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}
208:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
209:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
210:         run: |
211:           set -x
212: 
213:           if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then
214:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh
215:           elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then
216:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/caffe2/test.sh
217:           else
218:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh
219:           fi
220: 
221:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
222:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase
223:           # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice
224:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090
225:           container_name=$(docker run \
226:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \
227:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
228:             -e PR_NUMBER \
229:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
230:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \
231:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \
232:             -e GITHUB_JOB \
233:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \
234:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \
235:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
236:             -e JOB_ID \
237:             -e JOB_NAME \
238:             -e BASE_SHA \
239:             -e BRANCH \
240:             -e SHA1 \
241:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \
242:             -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \
243:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \
244:             -e TEST_CONFIG \
245:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \
246:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \
247:             -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \
248:             -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \
249:             -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \
250:             -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \
251:             -e NO_TD \
252:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
253:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \
254:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \
255:             -e TESTS_TO_INCLUDE \
256:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \
257:             -e DASHBOARD_TAG \
258:             --env-file="${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
259:             --ulimit stack=10485760:83886080 \
260:             --ulimit core=0 \
261:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
262:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
263:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
264:             --shm-size="8g" \
265:             --tty \
266:             --detach \
267:             --name="${container_name}" \
268:             --user jenkins \
269:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
270:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
271:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
272:           )
273:           # save container name for later step
274:           echo "CONTAINER_NAME=${container_name}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
275:           # jenkins user does not have write permission to mounted workspace; work-around by copying within container to jenkins home
276:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "cd .. && cp -R workspace pytorch && cd pytorch && pip install dist/*.whl && ${TEST_COMMAND}"
277: 
278:       - name: Save test results
279:         if: always()
280:         run: |
281:           # copy test results back to the mounted workspace, needed sudo, resulting permissions were correct
282:           docker exec -t "${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}" sh -c "cd ../pytorch && sudo cp -R test/test-reports ../workspace/test"
283: 
284:       - name: Change permissions (only needed for kubernetes runners for now)
285:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && (contains(matrix.runner, 'gfx942') || contains(matrix.runner, 'mi355')) }}
286:         run: |
287:           docker exec -t "${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}" sh -c "sudo chown -R 1001:1001 test"
288: 
289:       - name: Print remaining test logs
290:         shell: bash
291:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion
292:         run: |
293:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true
294: 
295:       - name: Stop monitoring script
296:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
297:         shell: bash
298:         continue-on-error: true
299:         env:
300:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
301:         run: |
302:           kill "$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID"
303: 
304:       - name: Upload test artifacts
305:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts
306:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'
307:         with:
308:           use-gha: true
309:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
310: 
311:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)
312:         if: always()
313:         run: |
314:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156
315:           find . -iname "core.[1-9]*" -exec docker exec "${CONTAINER_NAME}" sh -c "gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'" \;
316: 
317:       - name: Store Core dumps on GitHub
318:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
319:         if: failure()
320:         with:
321:           name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}
322:           retention-days: 14
323:           if-no-files-found: ignore
324:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*
325: 
326:       - name: Authenticate with AWS
327:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
328:         with:
329:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results
330:           # The max duration enforced by the server side
331:           role-duration-seconds: 18000
332:           aws-region: us-east-1
333: 
334:       - name: Upload the benchmark results
335:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@main
336:         with:
337:           benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports
338:           dry-run: false
339:           schema-version: v3
340:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
341: 
342:       - name: Upload utilization stats
343:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}
344:         continue-on-error: true
345:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
346:         with:
347:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
348:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
349:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
350:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
351:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
352: 
353:       - name: Teardown ROCm
354:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-rocm
355: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: Check whether the workflow owner can use ARC runners
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       check_experiments:
7:         required: false
8:         type: string
9:         description: |
10:           List of experiments for this workflow. If not defined, all default experiments are included.
11:       opt_out_experiments:
12:         required: false
13:         type: string
14:         description: Comma-separated list of experiments this workflow will opt-out of.
15:       triggering_actor:
16:         required: true
17:         type: string
18:         description: The triggering_actor for the workflow. Use github.triggering_actor
19:       issue_owner:
20:         required: true
21:         type: string
22:         description: The owner of the issue. Use github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login
23:       curr_branch:
24:         required: true
25:         type: string
26:         description: Current branch or tag.
27:       curr_ref_type:
28:         required: false
29:         type: string
30:         default: branch
31:         description: The value of "github.ref_type", "branch" or "tag"
32:       issue_number:
33:         required: false
34:         type: string
35:         default: "5132"
36:         description: |
37:           Fetch's GitHub Issue from pytorch/test-infra
38:           Example: https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132
39: 
40:     outputs:
41:       label-type:
42:         description: Type of runners to use
43:         value: ${{ jobs.runner-determinator.outputs.label-type }}
44: 
45: jobs:
46:   runner-determinator:
47:     # Don't run on forked repos
48:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
49:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
50:     outputs:
51:       label-type: ${{ steps.set-condition.outputs.label-type }}
52:     env:
53:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
54:       ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.issue_number }}
55:       TRIGGERING_ACTOR: ${{ inputs.triggering_actor }}
56:       ISSUE_OWNER: ${{ inputs.issue_owner }}
57:       CHECK_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.check_experiments }}
58:       OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.opt_out_experiments }}
59:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
60:     steps:
61:       # - name: Checkout PyTorch
62:       #   uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
63:       #   with:
64:       #     fetch-depth: 1
65:       #     submodules: true
66: 
67:       # TODO: Remove the hardcoded step below
68:       # Hardcoding below is temporary for testing ALI runners
69:       # This file below should match the script found in .github/scripts/runner_determinator.py
70:       - name: Hardcode runner-determinator script
71:         id: hardcode-script
72:         run: |
73:           cat <<EOF > runner_determinator.py
74:           # flake8: noqa: G004
75: 
76:           # Note: Copies of this script in runner_determinator.py and _runner-determinator.yml
77:           #       must be kept in sync. You can do it easily by running the following command:
78:           #           python .github/scripts/update_runner_determinator.py
79: 
80:           """
81:           This runner determinator is used to determine which set of runners to run a
82:           GitHub job on. It uses the first comment of a GitHub issue (by default
83:           https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132) to define the configuration
84:           of which runners should be used to run which job.
85: 
86:           The configuration has two parts, the settings and a list of opted-in users,
87:           separated by a line containing "---".  If the line is not present, the
88:           settings are considered to be empty with only the second part, the user
89:           list, defined.
90: 
91:           The first part is a YAML block that defines the rollout settings. This can be
92:           used to define any settings that are needed to determine which runners to use.
93:           It's fields are defined by the RolloutSettings class below.
94: 
95:           The second part is a list of users who are explicitly opted in to the LF fleet.
96:           The user list is also a comma separated list of additional features or
97:           experiments which the user could be opted in to.
98: 
99:           The user list has the following rules:
100: 
101:           - Users are GitHub usernames, which must start with the @ prefix
102:           - Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable
103:           - A "#" prefix opts the user out of all experiments
104: 
105:           Example config:
106:               # A list of experiments that can be opted into.
107:               # This defines the behavior they'll induce when opted into.
108:               # Expected syntax is:
109:               #   [experiment_name]: # Name of the experiment. Also used for the label prefix.
110:               #      rollout_perc: [int] # % of workflows to run with this experiment when users are not opted in.
111: 
112:               experiments:
113:                 lf:
114:                   rollout_percent: 25
115:                   all_branches: false
116:                   default: true
117:               ---
118: 
119:               # Opt-ins:
120:               # Users can opt into the LF fleet by adding their GitHub username to this list
121:               # and specifying experiments to enable in a comma-separated list.
122:               # To always opt out of an experiment, prefix it with a "-".
123:               # Experiments should be from the above list.
124: 
125:               @User1,-lf,split_build
126:               @User2,lf
127:               @User3,split_build
128:           """
129: 
130:           import json
131:           import logging
132:           import os
133:           import random
134:           import re
135:           import sys
136:           from argparse import ArgumentParser
137:           from collections.abc import Iterable
138:           from functools import cache
139:           from logging import LogRecord
140:           from typing import Any, NamedTuple
141:           from urllib.request import Request, urlopen
142: 
143:           import yaml
144:           from github import Auth, Github
145:           from github.Issue import Issue
146: 
147: 
148:           DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX = ""  # use meta runners
149:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF = "lf."  # use runners from the linux foundation
150:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF_CANARY = "lf.c."  # use canary runners from the linux foundation
151: 
152:           GITHUB_OUTPUT = os.getenv("GITHUB_OUTPUT", "")
153:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_AMI = "runner-ami"
154:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE = "label-type"
155:           OPT_OUT_LABEL = "no-runner-experiments"
156: 
157:           SETTING_EXPERIMENTS = "experiments"
158: 
159:           LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT = "lf"
160:           CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX = ".c"
161: 
162: 
163:           class Experiment(NamedTuple):
164:               rollout_perc: float = (
165:                   0  # Percentage of workflows to experiment on when user is not opted-in.
166:               )
167:               all_branches: bool = (
168:                   False  # If True, the experiment is also enabled on the exception branches
169:               )
170:               default: bool = (
171:                   True  # If True, the experiment is enabled by default for all queries
172:               )
173: 
174:               # Add more fields as needed
175: 
176: 
177:           class Settings(NamedTuple):
178:               """
179:               Settings for the experiments that can be opted into.
180:               """
181: 
182:               experiments: dict[str, Experiment] = {}
183: 
184: 
185:           class ColorFormatter(logging.Formatter):
186:               """Color codes the log messages based on the log level"""
187: 
188:               COLORS = {
189:                   "WARNING": "\033[33m",  # Yellow
190:                   "ERROR": "\033[31m",  # Red
191:                   "CRITICAL": "\033[31m",  # Red
192:                   "INFO": "\033[0m",  # Reset
193:                   "DEBUG": "\033[0m",  # Reset
194:               }
195: 
196:               def format(self, record: LogRecord) -> str:
197:                   log_color = self.COLORS.get(record.levelname, "\033[0m")  # Default to reset
198:                   record.msg = f"{log_color}{record.msg}\033[0m"
199:                   return super().format(record)
200: 
201: 
202:           handler = logging.StreamHandler()
203:           handler.setFormatter(ColorFormatter(fmt="%(levelname)-8s: %(message)s"))
204: 
205:           log = logging.getLogger(os.path.basename(__file__))
206:           log.addHandler(handler)
207:           log.setLevel(logging.INFO)
208: 
209: 
210:           def set_github_output(key: str, value: str) -> None:
211:               """
212:               Defines outputs of the github action that invokes this script
213:               """
214:               if not GITHUB_OUTPUT:
215:                   # See https://github.blog/changelog/2022-10-11-github-actions-deprecating-save-state-and-set-output-commands/ for deprecation notice
216:                   log.warning(
217:                       "No env var found for GITHUB_OUTPUT, you must be running this code locally. Falling back to the deprecated print method."
218:                   )
219:                   print(f"::set-output name={key}::{value}")
220:                   return
221: 
222:               with open(GITHUB_OUTPUT, "a") as f:
223:                   log.info(f"Setting output: {key}='{value}'")
224:                   f.write(f"{key}={value}\n")
225: 
226: 
227:           def _str_comma_separated_to_set(value: str) -> frozenset[str]:
228:               return frozenset(
229:                   filter(lambda itm: itm != "", map(str.strip, value.strip(" \n\t").split(",")))
230:               )
231: 
232: 
233:           def parse_args() -> Any:
234:               parser = ArgumentParser("Get dynamic rollout settings")
235:               parser.add_argument("--github-token", type=str, required=True, help="GitHub token")
236:               parser.add_argument(
237:                   "--github-issue-repo",
238:                   type=str,
239:                   required=False,
240:                   default="pytorch/test-infra",
241:                   help="GitHub repo to get the issue",
242:               )
243:               parser.add_argument(
244:                   "--github-repo",
245:                   type=str,
246:                   required=True,
247:                   help="GitHub repo where CI is running",
248:               )
249:               parser.add_argument(
250:                   "--github-issue", type=int, required=True, help="GitHub issue number"
251:               )
252:               parser.add_argument(
253:                   "--github-actor", type=str, required=True, help="GitHub triggering_actor"
254:               )
255:               parser.add_argument(
256:                   "--github-issue-owner", type=str, required=True, help="GitHub issue owner"
257:               )
258:               parser.add_argument(
259:                   "--github-branch", type=str, required=True, help="Current GitHub branch or tag"
260:               )
261:               parser.add_argument(
262:                   "--github-ref-type",
263:                   type=str,
264:                   required=True,
265:                   help="Current GitHub ref type, branch or tag",
266:               )
267:               parser.add_argument(
268:                   "--eligible-experiments",
269:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,
270:                   required=False,
271:                   default="",
272:                   help="comma separated list of experiments to check, if omitted all experiments marked with default=True are checked",
273:               )
274:               parser.add_argument(
275:                   "--opt-out-experiments",
276:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,
277:                   required=False,
278:                   default="",
279:                   help=(
280:                       "comma separated list of experiments to opt-out of. If unset, no opt-outs will occur. "
281:                       "If the same experiment is listed both here and in '--eligible-experiments' opt-out will take priority."
282:                   ),
283:               )
284:               parser.add_argument(
285:                   "--pr-number",
286:                   type=str,
287:                   required=False,
288:                   default="",
289:                   help="the optional PR number where this is run",
290:               )
291: 
292:               return parser.parse_args()
293: 
294: 
295:           def get_gh_client(github_token: str) -> Github:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]
296:               auth = Auth.Token(github_token)
297:               return Github(auth=auth)
298: 
299: 
300:           def get_issue(gh: Github, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> Issue:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]
301:               repo = gh.get_repo(repo)
302:               return repo.get_issue(number=issue_num)
303: 
304: 
305:           def get_potential_pr_author(
306:               github_token: str, repo: str, username: str, ref_type: str, ref_name: str
307:           ) -> str:
308:               # If the trigger was a new tag added by a bot, this is a ciflow case
309:               # Fetch the actual username from the original PR. The PR number is
310:               # embedded in the tag name: ciflow/<name>/<pr-number>
311: 
312:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)
313: 
314:               if username == "pytorch-bot[bot]" and ref_type == "tag":
315:                   split_tag = ref_name.split("/")
316:                   if (
317:                       len(split_tag) == 3
318:                       and split_tag[0] == "ciflow"
319:                       and split_tag[2].isnumeric()
320:                   ):
321:                       pr_number = split_tag[2]
322:                       try:
323:                           repository = gh.get_repo(repo)
324:                           pull = repository.get_pull(number=int(pr_number))
325:                       except Exception as e:
326:                           raise Exception(  # noqa: TRY002
327:                               f"issue with pull request {pr_number} from repo {repository}"
328:                           ) from e
329:                       return pull.user.login  # type: ignore[no-any-return]
330:               # In all other cases, return the original input username
331:               return username
332: 
333: 
334:           def is_exception_branch(branch: str) -> bool:
335:               """
336:               Branches that get opted out of experiments by default, until they're explicitly enabled.
337:               """
338:               return branch.split("/")[0] in {"main", "nightly", "release", "landchecks"}
339: 
340: 
341:           def load_yaml(yaml_text: str) -> Any:
342:               try:
343:                   data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_text)
344:                   return data
345:               except yaml.YAMLError:
346:                   log.exception("Error loading YAML")
347:                   raise
348: 
349: 
350:           def extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
351:               """
352:               Extracts the text with settings, if any, and the opted in users from the rollout state.
353: 
354:               If the issue body contains "---" then the text above that is the settings
355:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.
356: 
357:               If it doesn't contain "---" then the settings are empty and the rest is the users.
358:               """
359:               rollout_state_parts = rollout_state.split("---")
360:               if len(rollout_state_parts) >= 2:
361:                   return rollout_state_parts[0], rollout_state_parts[1]
362:               else:
363:                   return "", rollout_state
364: 
365: 
366:           class UserOptins(dict[str, list[str]]):
367:               """
368:               Dictionary of users with a list of features they have opted into
369:               """
370: 
371: 
372:           def parse_user_opt_in_from_text(user_optin_text: str) -> UserOptins:
373:               """
374:               Parse the user opt-in text into a key value pair of username and the list of features they have opted into
375: 
376:               Users are GitHub usernames with the @ prefix. Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable.
377:                   - Example line: "@User1,lf,split_build"
378:                   - A "#" prefix indicates the user is opted out of all experiments
379: 
380: 
381:               """
382:               optins = UserOptins()
383:               for user in user_optin_text.split("\n"):
384:                   user = user.strip("\r\n\t -")
385:                   if not user or not user.startswith("@"):
386:                       # Not a valid user. Skip
387:                       continue
388: 
389:                   if user:
390:                       usr_name = user.split(",")[0].strip("@")
391:                       optins[usr_name] = [exp.strip(" ") for exp in user.split(",")[1:]]
392: 
393:               return optins
394: 
395: 
396:           def is_valid_experiment_name(experiment_name: str) -> bool:
397:               """
398:               Check if the experiment name is valid.
399:               A valid name:
400:                   - Contains only alphanumeric characters and the special characters "_" & "-"
401:                   - The special characters "_" & "-" shouldn't be the first or last characters
402:                   - Cannot contain spaces
403:               """
404: 
405:               valid_char_regex = r"^[a-zA-Z0-9]([\w-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?$"
406:               valid = bool(re.match(valid_char_regex, experiment_name))
407: 
408:               if valid:
409:                   return True
410: 
411:               log.error(
412:                   f"Invalid experiment name: {experiment_name}. Experiment names should only contain alphanumeric characters, '_', and '-'. They cannot contain spaces, and the special characters '_' and '-' cannot be the first or last characters."
413:               )
414:               return False
415: 
416: 
417:           def parse_settings_from_text(settings_text: str) -> Settings:
418:               """
419:               Parse the experiments from the issue body into a list of ExperimentSettings
420:               """
421:               try:
422:                   if settings_text:
423:                       # Escape the backtick as well so that we can have the settings in a code block on the GH issue
424:                       # for easy reading
425:                       # Note: Using ascii for the backtick so that the cat step in _runner-determinator.yml doesn't choke on
426:                       #       the backtick character in shell commands.
427:                       backtick = chr(96)  # backtick character
428:                       settings_text = settings_text.strip(f"\r\n\t{backtick} ")
429:                       settings = load_yaml(settings_text)
430: 
431:                       # For now we just load experiments. We can expand this if/when we add more settings
432:                       experiments = {}
433: 
434:                       for exp_name, exp_settings in settings.get(SETTING_EXPERIMENTS).items():
435:                           if not is_valid_experiment_name(exp_name):
436:                               # Exclude invalid experiments from the list. We log an error, but don't raise an exception so that other experiments can still be processed.
437:                               continue
438: 
439:                           valid_settings = {}
440:                           for setting in exp_settings:
441:                               if setting not in Experiment._fields:
442:                                   log.warning(
443:                                       f"Unexpected setting in experiment: {setting} = {exp_settings[setting]}"
444:                                   )
445:                               else:
446:                                   valid_settings[setting] = exp_settings[setting]
447: 
448:                           experiments[exp_name] = Experiment(**valid_settings)
449:                       return Settings(experiments)
450: 
451:               except Exception:
452:                   log.exception("Failed to parse settings")
453: 
454:               return Settings()
455: 
456: 
457:           def parse_settings(rollout_state: str) -> Settings:
458:               """
459:               Parse settings, if any, from the rollout state.
460: 
461:               If the issue body contains "---" then the text above that is the settings
462:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.
463: 
464:               If it doesn't contain "---" then the settings are empty and the default values are used.
465:               """
466:               settings_text, _ = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)
467:               return parse_settings_from_text(settings_text)
468: 
469: 
470:           def parse_users(rollout_state: str) -> UserOptins:
471:               """
472:               Parse users from the rollout state.
473: 
474:               """
475:               _, users_text = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)
476:               return parse_user_opt_in_from_text(users_text)
477: 
478: 
479:           def is_user_opted_in(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:
480:               """
481:               Check if a user is opted into an experiment
482:               """
483:               return experiment_name in user_optins.get(user, [])
484: 
485: 
486:           def is_user_opted_out(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:
487:               """
488:               Check if a user explicitly opted out of an experiment
489:               """
490:               # if the experiment is prefixed with a "-", then it's an opt-out
491:               experiment_optout = "-" + experiment_name
492:               if experiment_optout not in user_optins.get(user, []):
493:                   return False
494: 
495:               if is_user_opted_in(user, user_optins, experiment_name):
496:                   log.warning(
497:                       f"User {user} is opted into experiment {experiment_name}, but also opted out of it. Defaulting to opting out"
498:                   )
499: 
500:               return True
501: 
502: 
503:           def get_runner_prefix(
504:               rollout_state: str,
505:               workflow_requestors: Iterable[str],
506:               branch: str,
507:               eligible_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),
508:               opt_out_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),
509:               is_canary: bool = False,
510:           ) -> str:
511:               settings = parse_settings(rollout_state)
512:               user_optins = parse_users(rollout_state)
513: 
514:               fleet_prefix = ""
515:               prefixes = []
516:               for experiment_name, experiment_settings in settings.experiments.items():
517:                   if not experiment_settings.all_branches and is_exception_branch(branch):
518:                       log.info(
519:                           f"Branch {branch} is an exception branch. Not enabling experiment {experiment_name}."
520:                       )
521:                       continue
522: 
523:                   if opt_out_experiments:
524:                       if experiment_name in opt_out_experiments:
525:                           opt_out_exp_list = ", ".join(opt_out_experiments)
526:                           log.info(
527:                               f"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as this workflow has opted-out (opted out experiments are: {opt_out_exp_list})"
528:                           )
529:                           continue
530: 
531:                   if eligible_experiments:
532:                       if experiment_name not in eligible_experiments:
533:                           exp_list = ", ".join(eligible_experiments)
534:                           log.info(
535:                               f"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not in the eligible_experiments list: {exp_list}"
536:                           )
537:                           continue
538:                   elif not experiment_settings.default:
539:                       log.info(
540:                           f"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not a default experiment"
541:                       )
542:                       continue
543: 
544:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted out to this experiment?
545:                   opted_out_users = [
546:                       requestor
547:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors
548:                       if is_user_opted_out(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)
549:                   ]
550: 
551:                   if opted_out_users:
552:                       log.info(
553:                           f"{', '.join(opted_out_users)} have opted out of experiment {experiment_name}."
554:                       )
555:                       continue
556: 
557:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted in to this experiment?
558:                   opted_in_users = [
559:                       requestor
560:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors
561:                       if is_user_opted_in(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)
562:                   ]
563: 
564:                   enabled = False
565:                   if opted_in_users:
566:                       log.info(
567:                           f"{', '.join(opted_in_users)} have opted into experiment {experiment_name}."
568:                       )
569:                       enabled = True
570: 
571:                   elif experiment_settings.rollout_perc:
572:                       # If no user is opted in, then we randomly enable the experiment based on the rollout percentage
573:                       if random.uniform(0, 100) <= experiment_settings.rollout_perc:
574:                           log.info(
575:                               f"Based on rollout percentage of {experiment_settings.rollout_perc}%, enabling experiment {experiment_name}."
576:                           )
577:                           enabled = True
578: 
579:                   if enabled:
580:                       label = experiment_name
581:                       if experiment_name == LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT:
582:                           # We give some special treatment to the "lf" experiment since determines the fleet we use
583:                           #  - If it's enabled, then we always list it's prefix first
584:                           #  - If we're in the canary branch, then we append ".c" to the lf prefix
585:                           if is_canary:
586:                               label += CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX
587:                           fleet_prefix = label
588:                       else:
589:                           prefixes.append(label)
590: 
591:               if len(prefixes) > 1:
592:                   log.error(
593:                       f"Only a fleet and one other experiment can be enabled for a job at any time. Enabling {prefixes[0]} and ignoring the rest, which are {', '.join(prefixes[1:])}"
594:                   )
595:                   prefixes = prefixes[:1]
596: 
597:               # Fleet always comes first
598:               if fleet_prefix:
599:                   prefixes.insert(0, fleet_prefix)
600: 
601:               return ".".join(prefixes) + "." if prefixes else ""
602: 
603: 
604:           def get_rollout_state_from_issue(github_token: str, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> str:
605:               """
606:               Gets the first comment of the issue, which contains the desired rollout state.
607: 
608:               The default issue we use - https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132
609:               """
610:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)
611:               issue = get_issue(gh, repo, issue_num)
612:               return str(issue.get_comments()[0].body.strip("\n\t "))
613: 
614: 
615:           def download_json(url: str, headers: dict[str, str], num_retries: int = 3) -> Any:
616:               for _ in range(num_retries):
617:                   try:
618:                       req = Request(url=url, headers=headers)
619:                       content = urlopen(req, timeout=5).read().decode("utf-8")
620:                       return json.loads(content)
621:                   except Exception as e:
622:                       log.warning(f"Could not download {url}: {e}")
623: 
624:               log.warning(f"All {num_retries} retries exhausted, downloading {url} failed")
625:               return {}
626: 
627: 
628:           @cache
629:           def get_pr_info(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
630:               """
631:               Dynamically get PR information
632:               """
633:               github_api = f"https://api.github.com/repos/{github_repo}"
634:               headers = {
635:                   "Accept": "application/vnd.github.v3+json",
636:                   "Authorization": f"token {github_token}",
637:               }
638:               json_response: dict[str, Any] = download_json(
639:                   url=f"{github_api}/issues/{pr_number}",
640:                   headers=headers,
641:               )
642: 
643:               if not json_response:
644:                   log.warning(f"Failed to get the labels for #{pr_number}")
645:                   return {}
646: 
647:               return json_response
648: 
649: 
650:           def get_labels(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> set[str]:
651:               """
652:               Dynamically get the latest list of labels from the pull request
653:               """
654:               pr_info = get_pr_info(github_repo, github_token, pr_number)
655:               return {
656:                   label.get("name") for label in pr_info.get("labels", []) if label.get("name")
657:               }
658: 
659: 
660:           def main() -> None:
661:               args = parse_args()
662: 
663:               runner_label_prefix = DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX
664: 
665:               # Check if the PR is opt-out
666:               if args.pr_number:
667:                   labels = get_labels(args.github_repo, args.github_token, int(args.pr_number))
668:                   if OPT_OUT_LABEL in labels:
669:                       log.info(
670:                           f"Opt-out runner determinator because #{args.pr_number} has {OPT_OUT_LABEL} label"
671:                       )
672:                       set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)
673:                       sys.exit()
674: 
675:               try:
676:                   rollout_state = get_rollout_state_from_issue(
677:                       args.github_token, args.github_issue_repo, args.github_issue
678:                   )
679: 
680:                   username = get_potential_pr_author(
681:                       args.github_token,
682:                       args.github_repo,
683:                       args.github_actor,
684:                       args.github_ref_type,
685:                       args.github_branch,
686:                   )
687: 
688:                   is_canary = args.github_repo == "pytorch/pytorch-canary"
689: 
690:                   runner_label_prefix = get_runner_prefix(
691:                       rollout_state,
692:                       (args.github_issue_owner, username),
693:                       args.github_branch,
694:                       args.eligible_experiments,
695:                       args.opt_out_experiments,
696:                       is_canary,
697:                   )
698: 
699:               except Exception as e:
700:                   log.error(
701:                       f"Failed to get issue. Defaulting to Meta runners and no experiments. Exception: {e}"
702:                   )
703: 
704:               set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)
705: 
706: 
707:           if __name__ == "__main__":
708:               main()
709: 
710:           EOF
711: 
712:           cat runner_determinator.py
713: 
714:       - name: Install dependencies
715:         run: python3 -m pip install urllib3==1.26.18 PyGithub==2.3.0
716: 
717:       - name: Get the workflow type for the current user
718:         id: set-condition
719:         run: |
720:           curr_branch="${{ inputs.curr_branch }}"
721:           curr_ref_type="${{ inputs.curr_ref_type }}"
722:           echo "Current branch is '$curr_branch'"
723: 
724:           python3 runner_determinator.py \
725:             --github-token "$GITHUB_TOKEN" \
726:             --github-issue "$ISSUE_NUMBER" \
727:             --github-branch "$curr_branch" \
728:             --github-actor "$TRIGGERING_ACTOR" \
729:             --github-issue-owner "$ISSUE_OWNER" \
730:             --github-ref-type "$curr_ref_type" \
731:             --github-repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" \
732:             --eligible-experiments "$CHECK_EXPERIMENTS" \
733:             --opt-out-experiments "$OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS" \
734:             --pr-number "${PR_NUMBER}"

{"content":"1: name: windows-build
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       cuda-version:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: What CUDA version to build with, "cpu" for none.
14:       use-xpu:
15:         required: false
16:         type: boolean
17:         default: false
18:         description: If set, build with XPU support.
19:       xpu-version:
20:         required: false
21:         type: string
22:         description: The version of XPU support package.
23:       vc-year:
24:         required: false
25:         type: string
26:         default: "2022"
27:         description: The Visual Studio year to use for building.
28:       build-with-debug:
29:         required: false
30:         type: boolean
31:         default: false
32:         description: If set, build in debug mode.
33:       sync-tag:
34:         required: false
35:         type: string
36:         default: ""
37:         description: |
38:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
39:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
40:       test-matrix:
41:         required: false
42:         type: string
43:         description: |
44:           An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This
45:           is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter
46:           logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds
47:       runner:
48:         required: false
49:         type: string
50:         default: "windows.4xlarge.nonephemeral"
51:         description: |
52:           Label of the runner this job should run on.
53: 
54:     outputs:
55:       test-matrix:
56:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}
57:         description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.
58:       build-environment:
59:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}
60:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
61: 
62: env:
63:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
64: 
65: jobs:
66:   build:
67:     # Don't run on forked repos.
68:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
69:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner }}
70:     timeout-minutes: 240
71:     outputs:
72:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}
73:       build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
74:     defaults:
75:       run:
76:         shell: bash
77:     steps:
78:       # Duplicated in win-test because this MUST go before a checkout
79:       - name: Enable git long paths and symlinks on Windows and disable fsmonitor daemon
80:         shell: bash
81:         run: |
82:           git config --global core.longpaths true
83:           git config --global core.symlinks true
84:           git config --global core.ignorecase false
85: 
86:           # https://git-scm.com/docs/git-fsmonitor--daemon.  The daemon could lock
87:           # the directory on Windows and prevent GHA from checking out as reported
88:           # in https://github.com/actions/checkout/issues/1018
89:           git config --global core.fsmonitor false
90: 
91:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
92:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
93:         with:
94:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
95:           instructions: |
96:             To forward remote desktop on your local machine ssh as follows:
97:               ssh -L 3389:localhost:3389 %%username%%@%%hostname%%
98:             And then change password using `passwd` command.
99: 
100:             To start build locally, change working folder to \actions-runner\_work\pytorch\pytorch,
101:             Activate miniconda and Visual Studio environment, by running:
102:               call C:\Jenkins\Miniconda3\Scripts\activate.bat C:\Jenkins\Miniconda3
103:               call "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools\VC\Auxiliary\Build\vcvarsall.bat" x64
104: 
105:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
106:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
107:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
108:         with:
109:           no-sudo: true
110: 
111:       - name: Setup Windows
112:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-win
113:         with:
114:           cuda-version: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}
115: 
116:       - name: Parse ref
117:         id: parse-ref
118:         shell: bash
119:         run: python3 .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
120: 
121:       - name: Get workflow job id
122:         id: get-job-id
123:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
124:         if: always()
125:         with:
126:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
127: 
128:       # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there
129:       - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)
130:         id: filter
131:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
132:         with:
133:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
134:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
135:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
136: 
137:       - name: Download pytest cache
138:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download
139:         continue-on-error: true
140:         with:
141:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache
142:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}
143: 
144:       - name: Build
145:         if: steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == ''
146:         id: build
147:         shell: bash
148:         env:
149:           PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/
150:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
151:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
152:           BUILD_WHEEL: 1
153:           MAX_JOBS: 8
154:           CUDA_VERSION: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}
155:           PYTHON_VERSION: "3.10"
156:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: "ossci-compiler-cache"
157:           SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX: ${{ github.workflow }}
158:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1
159:           VC_PRODUCT: "BuildTools"
160:           VC_VERSION: ""
161:           VC_YEAR: "${{ inputs.vc-year }}"
162:           ALPINE_IMAGE: "308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine"
163:           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
164:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
165:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
166:           DEBUG: ${{ inputs.build-with-debug && '1' || '0' }}
167:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: "8.6"
168:           USE_CUDA: ${{ inputs.cuda-version != 'cpu' && '1' || '0' }}
169:           USE_XPU: ${{ inputs.use-xpu == true && '1' || '0' }}
170:           XPU_VERSION: "${{ inputs.xpu-version }}"
171:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
172:         run: |
173:           .ci/pytorch/win-build.sh
174: 
175:       # Collect Windows torch libs and CUDA libs for cross-compilation
176:       - name: Collect Windows CUDA libs for cross-compilation
177:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && inputs.cuda-version != 'cpu'
178:         shell: bash
179:         run: |
180:           set -ex
181: 
182:           # Create directory structure if does not exist
183:           mkdir -p /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results
184: 
185:           # Copy CUDA libs
186:           CUDA_PATH="/c/Program Files/NVIDIA GPU Computing Toolkit/CUDA/v${{ inputs.cuda-version }}"
187: 
188:           if [ -f "${CUDA_PATH}/lib/x64/cuda.lib" ]; then
189:             cp "${CUDA_PATH}/lib/x64/cuda.lib" /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/
190:           fi
191: 
192:           if [ -f "${CUDA_PATH}/lib/x64/cudart.lib" ]; then
193:             cp "${CUDA_PATH}/lib/x64/cudart.lib" /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/
194:           fi
195: 
196:           # List collected files
197:           echo "Collected CUDA libs:"
198:           ls -lah /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/*.lib
199: 
200:       # Upload to github so that people can click and download artifacts
201:       - name: Upload artifacts to s3
202:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'
203:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
204:         with:
205:           retention-days: 14
206:           if-no-files-found: error
207:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
208:           path: C:\${{ github.run_id }}\build-results
209: 
210:       - name: Upload sccache stats
211:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'
212:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-sccache-stats
213:         with:
214:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
215: 
216:       - name: Teardown Windows
217:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-win
218:         if: always()
219:         timeout-minutes: 120
220:         with:
221:           extra-delete-dir: /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/
222: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_win-build.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: win-test
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       cuda-version:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: What CUDA version to build with, "cpu" for none.
14:       test-matrix:
15:         required: true
16:         type: string
17:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.
18:       sync-tag:
19:         required: false
20:         type: string
21:         default: ""
22:         description: |
23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
25:       timeout-minutes:
26:         required: false
27:         type: number
28:         default: 240
29:         description: |
30:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
31:       disable-monitor:
32:         description: |
33:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.
34:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,
35:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.
36:         required: false
37:         type: boolean
38:         default: true
39:       monitor-log-interval:
40:         description: |
41:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
42:         required: false
43:         type: number
44:         default: 5
45:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
46:         description: |
47:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
48:         required: false
49:         type: number
50:         default: 1
51: env:
52:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
53: 
54: jobs:
55:   test:
56:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix
57:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'
58:     strategy:
59:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}
60:       fail-fast: false
61:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
62:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
63:     defaults:
64:       run:
65:         shell: bash
66:     steps:
67:       # Duplicated in win-build because this MUST go before a checkout
68:       - name: Enable git long paths and symlinks on Windows and disable fsmonitor daemon
69:         shell: bash
70:         run: |
71:           git config --global core.longpaths true
72:           git config --global core.symlinks true
73:           git config --global core.ignorecase false
74: 
75:           # https://git-scm.com/docs/git-fsmonitor--daemon.  The daemon could lock
76:           # the directory on Windows and prevent GHA from checking out as reported
77:           # in https://github.com/actions/checkout/issues/1018
78:           git config --global core.fsmonitor false
79: 
80:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
81:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
82:         with:
83:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
84:           instructions: |
85:             To forward remote desktop on your local machine ssh as follows:
86:               ssh -L 3389:localhost:3389 %%username%%@%%hostname%%
87:             And then change password using `passwd` command.
88: 
89:             To start tests locally, change working folder to \actions-runner\_work\pytorch\pytorch\test,
90:             Activate miniconda and Visual Studio environment and set PYTHON_PATH, by running:
91:               call C:\Jenkins\Miniconda3\Scripts\activate.bat C:\Jenkins\Miniconda3
92:               call "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools\VC\Auxiliary\Build\vcvarsall.bat" x64
93:               set PYTHONPATH=C:\actions-runner\_work\pytorch\pytorch\build\win_tmp\build
94: 
95:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
96:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
97:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
98:         with:
99:           no-sudo: true
100: 
101:       - name: Setup Windows
102:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-win
103:         with:
104:           cuda-version: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}
105: 
106:       - name: Get workflow job id
107:         id: get-job-id
108:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
109:         if: always()
110:         with:
111:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
112: 
113:       - name: Start monitoring script
114:         id: monitor-script
115:         env:
116:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
117:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
118:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
119:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
120:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
121:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
122:         shell: bash
123:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
124:         continue-on-error: true
125:         run: |
126:           # Windows conda doesn't have python3 binary, only python, but it's python3
127:           ${CONDA_RUN} python -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84
128:           ${CONDA_RUN} python -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &
129:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
130: 
131:       - name: Download PyTorch Build Artifacts
132:         uses: seemethere/download-artifact-s3@1da556a7aa0a088e3153970611f6c432d58e80e6 # v4.2.0
133:         with:
134:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
135:           path: C:\${{ github.run_id }}\build-results
136: 
137:       - name: Check build-results folder
138:         shell: powershell
139:         run: |
140:           tree /F C:\$Env:GITHUB_RUN_ID\build-results
141: 
142:       - name: Download TD artifacts
143:         continue-on-error: true
144:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts
145: 
146:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues
147:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently
148:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do
149:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.
150:         id: keep-going
151:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
152:         with:
153:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
154:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
155:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
156: 
157:       - name: Set Test step time
158:         id: test-timeout
159:         shell: bash
160:         env:
161:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
162:         run: |
163:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
164: 
165:       - name: Test
166:         id: test
167:         shell: bash
168:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
169:         env:
170:           USE_CUDA: ${{ inputs.cuda-version != 'cpu' && '1' || '0' }}
171:           INSTALL_WINDOWS_SDK: 1
172:           PYTHON_VERSION: "3.10"
173:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}
174:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}
175:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}
176:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}
177:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}
178:           VC_PRODUCT: "BuildTools"
179:           VC_VERSION: ""
180:           VS_VERSION: "17.4.1"
181:           VC_YEAR: "2022"
182:           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
183:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
184:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
185:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
186:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
187:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
188:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
189:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
190:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
191:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
192:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
193:           CUDA_VERSION: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}
194:           PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/
195:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
196:           ALPINE_IMAGE: "308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine"
197:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}
198:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}
199:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}
200:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ github.event.pull_request.reenabled-issues }}
201:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: "8.6"
202:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}
203:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}
204:         run: |
205:           pushd "${PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR}"
206:           # shellcheck disable=SC2046,SC2102
207:           python3 -mpip install $(echo *.whl)[opt-einsum,optree] optree==0.13.0
208:           popd
209: 
210:           .ci/pytorch/win-test.sh
211: 
212:       - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed
213:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload
214:         continue-on-error: true
215:         if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure'
216:         with:
217:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache
218:           shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}
219:           sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
220:           test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}
221:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}
222: 
223:       - name: Print remaining test logs
224:         shell: bash
225:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion
226:         run: |
227:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true
228: 
229:       - name: Stop monitoring script
230:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
231:         shell: bash
232:         continue-on-error: true
233:         env:
234:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
235:         run: |
236:           kill "$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID"
237: 
238:       - name: Upload test artifacts
239:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts
240:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'
241:         with:
242:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
243: 
244:       - name: Upload utilization stats
245:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}
246:         continue-on-error: true
247:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
248:         with:
249:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
250:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
251:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
252:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
253:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
254: 
255:       - name: Parse ref
256:         id: parse-ref
257:         shell: bash
258:         run: python3 .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
259: 
260:       - name: Teardown Windows
261:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-win
262:         if: always()
263:         timeout-minutes: 120
