{"content":"1: # TODO: this looks sort of similar to _linux-test, but there are like a dozen
2: # places where you would have to insert an if statement. Probably it's better to
3: # just use a different workflow altogether
4: 
5: name: xpu-test
6: 
7: on:
8:   workflow_call:
9:     inputs:
10:       build-environment:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
14:       test-matrix:
15:         required: true
16:         type: string
17:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.
18:       docker-image:
19:         required: true
20:         type: string
21:         description: Docker image to run in.
22:       sync-tag:
23:         required: false
24:         type: string
25:         default: ""
26:         description: |
27:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
28:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
29:       timeout-minutes:
30:         required: false
31:         type: number
32:         default: 390
33:         description: |
34:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
35:       tests-to-include:
36:         required: false
37:         type: string
38:         default: ""
39:         description: |
40:           List of tests to include (empty string implies default list)
41:       dashboard-tag:
42:         required: false
43:         type: string
44:         default: ""
45:       disable-monitor:
46:         description: |
47:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.
48:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,
49:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.
50:         required: false
51:         type: boolean
52:         default: true
53:       monitor-log-interval:
54:         description: |
55:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
56:         required: false
57:         type: number
58:         default: 5
59:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
60:         description: |
61:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
62:         required: false
63:         type: number
64:         default: 1
65:     secrets:
66:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
67:         required: false
68:         description: |
69:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
70: permissions:
71:   id-token: write
72:   contents: read
73: 
74: env:
75:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
76: 
77: jobs:
78:   test:
79:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix
80:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'
81:     strategy:
82:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}
83:       fail-fast: false
84:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
85:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
86:     steps:
87:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
88:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
89:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
90: 
91:       - name: Setup XPU
92:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-xpu
93: 
94:       - name: configure aws credentials
95:         id: aws_creds
96:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
97:         with:
98:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only
99:           aws-region: us-east-1
100: 
101:       - name: Login to Amazon ECR
102:         id: login-ecr
103:         uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1
104: 
105:       - name: Calculate docker image
106:         id: calculate-docker-image
107:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
108:         with:
109:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
110: 
111:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
112:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
113:         env:
114:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
115:         shell: bash
116:         run: |
117:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
118:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
119: 
120:       - name: Pull docker image
121:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
122:         with:
123:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
124: 
125:       - name: Get workflow job id
126:         id: get-job-id
127:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
128:         if: always()
129:         with:
130:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
131: 
132:       - name: Start monitoring script
133:         id: monitor-script
134:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
135:         shell: bash
136:         continue-on-error: true
137:         env:
138:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
139:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
140:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
141:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
142:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
143:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
144:         run: |
145:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84
146:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &
147:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
148: 
149:       - name: Download build artifacts
150:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
151:         with:
152:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
153: 
154:       - name: Parse ref
155:         id: parse-ref
156:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
157: 
158:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues
159:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently
160:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do
161:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.
162:         id: keep-going
163:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
164:         with:
165:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
166:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
167:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
168: 
169:       - name: Set Test step time
170:         id: test-timeout
171:         shell: bash
172:         env:
173:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}
174:         run: |
175:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
176: 
177:       - name: Test
178:         id: test
179:         env:
180:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
181:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
182:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
183:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
184:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
185:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
186:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
187:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
188:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
189:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
190:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
191:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
192:           PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES: 1
193:           PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL: 1
194:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}
195:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}
196:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}
197:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}
198:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}
199:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}
200:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}
201:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}
202:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}
203:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
204:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla
205:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}
206:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}
207:           TESTS_TO_INCLUDE: ${{ inputs.tests-to-include }}
208:           DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}
209:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
210:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
211:         run: |
212:           # Fetch aws credential from IMDs
213:           eval "$(python3 .github/scripts/get_aws_session_tokens.py)"
214:           set -x
215: 
216:           TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh
217: 
218:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
219:           # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice
220:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090
221:           container_name=$(docker run \
222:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \
223:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
224:             -e PR_NUMBER \
225:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
226:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \
227:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \
228:             -e GITHUB_JOB \
229:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \
230:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \
231:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
232:             -e JOB_ID \
233:             -e BRANCH \
234:             -e SHA1 \
235:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \
236:             -e AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID \
237:             -e AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY \
238:             -e AWS_SESSION_TOKEN \
239:             -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \
240:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \
241:             -e TEST_CONFIG \
242:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \
243:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \
244:             -e PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES \
245:             -e PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL \
246:             -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \
247:             -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \
248:             -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \
249:             -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \
250:             -e NO_TD \
251:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
252:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \
253:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \
254:             -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \
255:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \
256:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \
257:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \
258:             -e TESTS_TO_INCLUDE \
259:             -e ZE_AFFINITY_MASK \
260:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \
261:             -e DASHBOARD_TAG \
262:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
263:             --ulimit stack=10485760:83886080 \
264:             --ulimit core=0 \
265:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
266:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
267:             --shm-size="8g" \
268:             --tty \
269:             --detach \
270:             --name="${container_name}" \
271:             --user jenkins \
272:             --privileged \
273:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
274:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
275:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
276:           )
277:           # save container name for later step
278:           echo "CONTAINER_NAME=${container_name}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
279:           # jenkins user does not have write permission to mounted workspace; work-around by copying within container to jenkins home
280:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "cd .. && cp -R workspace pytorch && cd pytorch && pip install dist/*.whl && ${TEST_COMMAND}"
281: 
282:       - name: Save test results
283:         if: always()
284:         run: |
285:           # copy test results back to the mounted workspace, needed sudo, resulting permissions were correct
286:           docker exec -t "${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}" sh -c "cd ../pytorch && sudo cp -R test/test-reports ../workspace/test"
287: 
288:       - name: Change permissions
289:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion }}
290:         run: |
291:           docker exec -t "${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}" sh -c "sudo chown -R 1000:1000 test"
292: 
293:       - name: Print remaining test logs
294:         shell: bash
295:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion
296:         run: |
297:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true
298: 
299:       - name: Stop monitoring script
300:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
301:         shell: bash
302:         continue-on-error: true
303:         env:
304:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
305:         run: |
306:           kill "$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID"
307: 
308:       - name: Upload test artifacts
309:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts
310:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'
311:         with:
312:           use-gha: true
313:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
314: 
315:       - name: Upload utilization stats
316:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}
317:         continue-on-error: true
318:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
319:         with:
320:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
321:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
322:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
323:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
324:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
325: 
326:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)
327:         if: always()
328:         run: |
329:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156
330:           find . -iname "core.[1-9]*" -exec docker exec "${CONTAINER_NAME}" sh -c "gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'" \;
331: 
332:       - name: Stop container before exit
333:         if: always()
334:         run: |
335:           # Workaround for multiple runners on same IDC node
336:           docker stop "${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}"
337: 
338:       - name: Store Core dumps on GitHub
339:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
340:         if: failure()
341:         with:
342:           name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}
343:           retention-days: 14
344:           if-no-files-found: ignore
345:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*
346: 
347:       - name: Authenticate with AWS
348:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
349:         with:
350:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results
351:           # The max duration enforced by the server side
352:           role-duration-seconds: 18000
353:           aws-region: us-east-1
354: 
355:       - name: Upload the benchmark results
356:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@main
357:         with:
358:           benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports
359:           dry-run: false
360:           schema-version: v3
361:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
362: 
363:       - name: Teardown XPU
364:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-xpu
365: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: CI for distributed tests on B200
2: 
3: on:
4:   pull_request:
5:     paths:
6:       - .github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml
7:   workflow_dispatch:
8:   push:
9:     tags:
10:       - ciflow/b200-distributed/*

{"content":"1: name: Build almalinux docker images
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_dispatch:
5:   push:
6:     branches:
7:       - main
8:       - release/*
9:     tags:
10:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate or nightly builds
11:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1
12:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+
13:     paths:
14:       - .ci/docker/**
15:       - .github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml
16:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**
17:   pull_request:
18:     paths:
19:       - .ci/docker/**
20:       - .github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml
21:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**
22: 
23: env:
24:   DOCKER_REGISTRY: "docker.io"
25:   DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1
26:   WITH_PUSH: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) }}
27: 
28: concurrency:
29:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}
30:   cancel-in-progress: true
31: 
32: jobs:
33:   build-docker:
34:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
35:     environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'docker-build') || '' }}
36:     runs-on: linux.9xlarge.ephemeral
37:     strategy:
38:       matrix:
39:         tag: ["cuda12.6", "cuda12.8", "cuda12.9", "cuda13.0", "rocm7.0", "rocm7.1", "cpu"]
40:     steps:
41:       - name: Build docker image
42:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/binary-docker-build@main
43:         with:
44:           docker-image-name: almalinux-builder
45:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{matrix.tag}}
46:           docker-build-dir: almalinux
47:           DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_TOKEN }}
48:           DOCKER_ID: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_ID }}
49: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: Build libtorch docker images
2: 
3: on:
4:   push:
5:     branches:
6:       - main
7:       - release/*
8:     tags:
9:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate or nightly builds
10:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1
11:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+
12:     paths:
13:       - .ci/docker/**
14:       - .github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml
15:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**
16:   pull_request:
17:     paths:
18:       - .ci/docker/**
19:       - .github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml
20:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**
21: 
22: env:
23:   DOCKER_REGISTRY: "docker.io"
24:   DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1
25:   WITH_PUSH: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) }}
26: 
27: concurrency:
28:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}
29:   cancel-in-progress: true
30: 
31: jobs:
32:   get-label-type:
33:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
34:     name: get-label-type
35:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@main
36:     with:
37:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
38:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
39:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
40:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
41: 
42:   build:
43:     environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'docker-build') || '' }}
44:     needs: get-label-type
45:     runs-on: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.9xlarge.ephemeral
46:     name: libtorch-cxx11-builder:${{ matrix.tag }}
47:     strategy:
48:       fail-fast: false
49:       matrix:
50:         include: [
51:           { tag: "cuda13.0" },
52:           { tag: "cuda12.9" },
53:           { tag: "cuda12.8" },
54:           { tag: "cuda12.6" },
55:           { tag: "rocm7.0"  },
56:           { tag: "rocm7.1"  },
57:           { tag: "cpu"      },
58:         ]
59:     steps:
60:       - name: Build docker image
61:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/binary-docker-build@main
62:         with:
63:           docker-image-name: libtorch-cxx11-builder
64:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ matrix.tag }}
65:           docker-build-dir: libtorch
66:           DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_TOKEN }}
67:           DOCKER_ID: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_ID }}
68: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml"}

import os, re

def strip_ln(content):
    lines = content.split('\n')
    result = []
    for line in lines:
        m = re.match(r'^\d+: (.*)$', line)
        if m:
            result.append(m.group(1))
        elif line == '':
            result.append('')
        else:
            result.append(line)
    # Remove trailing empty line if the original ends with \n (the last element is empty string)
    if result and result[-1] == '':

    return '\n'.join(result) + '\n'

{"content":"1: name: linux-binary-build
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build_name:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: The build's name
10:       build_environment:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: The build environment
14:       runner_prefix:
15:         required: false
16:         default: ""
17:         type: string
18:         description: prefix for runner label
19:       runs_on:
20:         required: false
21:         default: linux.12xlarge.memory.ephemeral
22:         type: string
23:         description: Hardware to run this "build" job on, linux.12xlarge or linux.arm64.2xlarge.
24:       timeout-minutes:
25:         required: false
26:         default: 240
27:         type: number
28:         description: timeout for the job
29:       ALPINE_IMAGE:
30:         required: false
31:         type: string
32:         default: "308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine"
33:         description: Alpine image to use
34:       PYTORCH_ROOT:
35:         required: true
36:         type: string
37:         description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository
38:       PACKAGE_TYPE:
39:         required: true
40:         type: string
41:         description: Package type
42:       DESIRED_CUDA:
43:         required: true
44:         type: string
45:         description: Desired Cuda version
46:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION:
47:         required: false
48:         type: string
49:         description: GPU Arch version
50:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE:
51:         required: true
52:         type: string
53:         description: GPU Arch type
54:       DOCKER_IMAGE:
55:         required: true
56:         type: string
57:         description: Docker image to use
58:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:
59:         required: true
60:         type: string
61:         description: Docker image tag to use
62:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG:
63:         required: false
64:         type: string
65:         description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)
66:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT:
67:         required: false
68:         type: string
69:         description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)
70:       DESIRED_PYTHON:
71:         required: false
72:         type: string
73:         description: Desired python version
74:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS:
75:         required: false
76:         type: string
77:         description: Extra install requirements
78:         default: ""
79:     secrets:
80:       github-token:
81:         required: true
82:         description: Github Token
83: 
84: permissions:
85:   id-token: write
86: 
87: jobs:
88:   build:
89:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runs_on }}
90:     timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}
91:     env:
92:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_ROOT }}
93:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}
94:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in
95:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION
96:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}
97:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}
98:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}
99:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
100:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1
101:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}
102:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}
103:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}
104:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}
105:       ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
106:       AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
107:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env
108:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build_environment }}
109:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
110:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
111:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts
112:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
113:     steps:
114:       - name: Make the env permanent during this workflow (but not the secrets)
115:         shell: bash
116:         run: |
117:           {
118:             echo "PYTORCH_ROOT=${{ env.PYTORCH_ROOT }}"
119:             echo "PACKAGE_TYPE=${{ env.PACKAGE_TYPE }}"
120:             echo "DESIRED_CUDA=${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}"
121:             echo "GPU_ARCH_VERSION=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}"
122:             echo "GPU_ARCH_TYPE=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}"
123:             echo "DOCKER_IMAGE=${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}"
124:             echo "SKIP_ALL_TESTS=${{ env.SKIP_ALL_TESTS }}"
125:             echo "LIBTORCH_CONFIG=${{ env.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}"
126:             echo "LIBTORCH_VARIANT=${{ env.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}"
127:             echo "DESIRED_PYTHON=${{ env.DESIRED_PYTHON }}"
128:             echo "PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS=${{ env.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}"
129:             echo "ALPINE_IMAGE=${{ env.ALPINE_IMAGE }}"
130:             echo "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${{ env.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION }}"
131:             echo "BINARY_ENV_FILE=${{ env.BINARY_ENV_FILE }}"
132:             echo "BUILD_ENVIRONMENT=${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}"
133:             echo "BUILD_NAME=${{ env.BUILD_NAME }}"
134:             echo "PR_NUMBER=${{ env.PR_NUMBER }}"
135:             echo "PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}"
136:             echo "SHA1=${{ env.SHA1 }}"
137:           } >> "${GITHUB_ENV} }}"
138: 
139:       - name: List the env
140:         shell: bash
141:         run: env
142: 
143:       - name: "[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)"
144:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
145:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
146:         continue-on-error: true
147:         with:
148:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
149: 
150:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
151:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
152:         with:
153:           no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
154: 
155:       - name: Setup Linux
156:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
157:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
158: 
159:       - name: Chown workspace
160:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
161:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace
162:         with:
163:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
164: 
165:       - name: Clean workspace
166:         shell: bash
167:         run: |
168:           set -eux
169: 
170:           rm -rf "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
171:           mkdir "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
172: 
173:           if [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' ]] || [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' ]] ; then
174:             rm -rf "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts"
175:             mkdir "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts"
176:           fi
177: 
178:       - name: Checkout PyTorch to pytorch dir
179:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
180:         with:
181:           ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
182:           submodules: recursive
183:           path: pytorch
184:           show-progress: false
185: 
186:       - name: Clean PyTorch checkout
187:         run: |
188:           # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts
189:           git clean -fxd
190:         working-directory: pytorch
191: 
192:       - name: Check if the job is disabled
193:         id: filter
194:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs
195:         with:
196:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
197:           # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the
198:           # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled
199:           test-matrix: |
200:             { include: [
201:               { config: "default" },
202:             ]}
203: 
204:       - name: configure aws credentials
205:         id: aws_creds
206:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}
207:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
208:         with:
209:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only
210:           aws-region: us-east-1
211:           role-duration-seconds: 18000
212: 
213:       - name: Calculate docker image
214:         id: calculate-docker-image
215:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
216:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
217:         with:
218:           # If doing this in main or release branch, use docker.io. Otherwise
219:           # use ECR
220:           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}
221:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
222:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}
223:           # The build.sh script in this folder is not actually the correct one,
224:           # this is just needed for sha calculation
225:           docker-build-dir: .ci/docker
226:           working-directory: pytorch
227: 
228:       - name: Pull Docker image
229:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
230:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
231:         with:
232:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
233: 
234:       - name: Build PyTorch binary
235:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
236:         env:
237:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image || format('{0}:{1}', inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE, inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX) }}
238:         run: |
239:           set -x
240:           mkdir -p artifacts/
241:           container_name=$(docker run \
242:             -e BINARY_ENV_FILE \
243:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
244:             -e DESIRED_CUDA \
245:             -e DESIRED_PYTHON \
246:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
247:             -e GPU_ARCH_TYPE \
248:             -e GPU_ARCH_VERSION \
249:             -e LIBTORCH_VARIANT \
250:             -e PACKAGE_TYPE \
251:             -e PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR \
252:             -e PYTORCH_ROOT \
253:             -e SKIP_ALL_TESTS \
254:             -e PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS \
255:             --tty \
256:             --detach \
257:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/pytorch:/pytorch" \
258:             -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/artifacts" \
259:             -w / \
260:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
261:           )
262:           docker exec -t -w "${PYTORCH_ROOT}" "${container_name}" bash -c "bash .circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh"
263:           # Unified build script for all architectures (x86_64, aarch64, s390x)
264:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" bash -c "source ${BINARY_ENV_FILE} && bash /pytorch/.ci/${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}/build.sh"
265: 
266:       - name: Chown artifacts
267:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
268:         shell: bash
269:         run: |
270:           # Ensure the working directory gets chowned back to the current user
271:           docker run --rm -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/v" -w /v "${ALPINE_IMAGE}" chown -R "$(id -u):$(id -g)" .
272: 
273:       - uses: actions/upload-artifact@50769540e7f4bd5e21e526ee35c689e35e0d6874 # v4.4.0
274:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
275:         with:
276:           name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}
277:           if-no-files-found: error
278:           path:
279:             ${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/*
280: 
281:       - name: Teardown Linux
282:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
283:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
284: 
285:       - name: Chown workspace
286:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
287:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/chown-workspace
288:         with:
289:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
290: 
291:       - name: Cleanup docker
292:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
293:         shell: bash
294:         run: |
295:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop
296:           # ignore expansion of "docker ps -q" since it could be empty
297:           # shellcheck disable=SC2046
298:           docker stop $(docker ps -q) || true
299: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml"}

64:         required: true
65:         type: string
66:         description: Hardware to run this job on. Valid values are linux.4xlarge, linux.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu, linux.arm64.2xlarge, and linux.rocm.gpu
67:     secrets:
68:       github-token:
69:         required: true
70:         description: Github Token
71: 
72: permissions:
73:   id-token: write
74: 
75: jobs:
76:   test:
77:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runs_on }}
78:     timeout-minutes: 240
79:     env:
80:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_ROOT }}
81:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}
82:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in
83:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION
84:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}
85:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}
86:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}
87:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
88:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1
89:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}
90:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}
91:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}
92:       ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
93:       AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1
94:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env
95:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build_environment }}
96:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
97:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
98:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts
99:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
100:     steps:
101:       - name: Make the env permanent during this workflow (but not the secrets)
102:         shell: bash
103:         run: |
104:           {
105:             echo "PYTORCH_ROOT=${{ env.PYTORCH_ROOT }}"
106:             echo "PACKAGE_TYPE=${{ env.PACKAGE_TYPE }}"
107: 
108:             echo "DESIRED_CUDA=${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}"
109:             echo "GPU_ARCH_VERSION=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}"
110:             echo "GPU_ARCH_TYPE=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}"
111:             echo "DOCKER_IMAGE=${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}"
112:             echo "SKIP_ALL_TESTS=${{ env.SKIP_ALL_TESTS }}"
113:             echo "LIBTORCH_CONFIG=${{ env.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}"
114:             echo "LIBTORCH_VARIANT=${{ env.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}"
115:             echo "DESIRED_PYTHON=${{ env.DESIRED_PYTHON }}"
116: 
117:             echo "ALPINE_IMAGE=${{ env.ALPINE_IMAGE }}"
118:             echo "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${{ env.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION }}"
119:             echo "BINARY_ENV_FILE=${{ env.BINARY_ENV_FILE }}"
120:             echo "BUILD_ENVIRONMENT=${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}"
121:             echo "PR_NUMBER=${{ env.PR_NUMBER }}"
122:             echo "PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}"
123:             echo "SHA1=${{ env.SHA1 }}"
124:           } >> "${GITHUB_ENV} }}"
125: 
126:       - name: "[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)"
127:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
128:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
129:         continue-on-error: true
130:         with:
131:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
132: 
133:         # Setup the environment
134:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
135:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
136:         with:
137:           no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
138: 
139:       - name: Setup Linux
140:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
141:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
142: 
143:       - name: Chown workspace
144:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
145:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace
146:         with:
147:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
148: 
149:       - name: Clean workspace
150:         shell: bash
151:         run: |
152:           rm -rf "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
153:           mkdir "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
154: 
155:       - name: Checkout PyTorch to pytorch dir
156:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
157:         with:
158:           ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
159:           submodules: recursive
160:           show-progress: false
161:           path: pytorch
162: 
163:       - name: Clean PyTorch checkout
164:         run: |
165:           # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts
166:           git clean -fxd
167:         working-directory: pytorch
168: 
169:       - name: Check if the job is disabled
170:         id: filter
171:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs
172:         with:
173:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
174:           # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the
175:           # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled
176:           test-matrix: |
177:             { include: [
178:               { config: "default" },
179:             ]}
180: 
181:       - name: Download Build Artifacts
182:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
183:         uses: actions/download-artifact@65a9edc5881444af0b9093a5e628f2fe47ea3b2e # v4.1.7
184:         with:
185:           name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}
186:           path: "${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/"
187: 
188:       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG
189:         id: install-nvidia-driver
190:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main
191:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
192: 
193:       - name: configure aws credentials
194:         id: aws_creds
195:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}
196:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
197:         with:
198:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only
199:           aws-region: us-east-1
200:           role-duration-seconds: 18000
201: 
202:       - name: Calculate docker image
203:         id: calculate-docker-image
204:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
205:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
206:         with:
207:           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}
208:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
209:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}
210:           docker-build-dir: .ci/docker
211:           working-directory: pytorch
212: 
213:       - name: Pull Docker image
214:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
215:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
216:         with:
217:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
218: 
219:       - name: Test Pytorch binary
220:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}
221:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/test-pytorch-binary
222:         env:
223:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image || format('{0}:{1}', inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE, inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX) }}
224: 
225:       - name: Teardown Linux
226:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
227:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
228: 
229:       - name: Chown workspace
230:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
231:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/chown-workspace
232:         with:
233:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}
234: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_binary-test-linux.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: upload
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build_name:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: The build's name
10:       use_s3:
11:         type: boolean
12:         default: true
13:         description: If true, will download artifacts from s3. Otherwise will use the default GitHub artifact download action
14:       PYTORCH_ROOT:
15:         required: false
16:         type: string
17:         description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository. Not actually needed, but currently passing it in since we pass in the same inputs to the reusable workflows of all binary builds
18:       PACKAGE_TYPE:
19:         required: true
20:         type: string
21:         description: Package type
22:       DESIRED_CUDA:
23:         required: true
24:         type: string
25:         description: Desired CUDA version
26:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION:
27:         required: false
28:         type: string
29:         description: GPU Arch version
30:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE:
31:         required: true
32:         type: string
33:         description: GPU Arch type
34:       DOCKER_IMAGE:
35:         required: false
36:         type: string
37:         description: Docker image to use
38:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:
39:         required: false
40:         type: string
41:         description: Docker image tag to use
42:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG:
43:         required: false
44:         type: string
45:         description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)
46:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT:
47:         required: false
48:         type: string
49:         description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)
50:       DESIRED_PYTHON:
51:         required: false
52:         type: string
53:         description: Desired python version
54:     secrets:
55:       github-token:
56:         required: true
57:         description: Github Token
58: 
59: jobs:
60:   upload:
61:     runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
62:     container:
63:       image: continuumio/miniconda3:4.12.0
64:     env:
65:       PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch
66:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}
67:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in
68:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION
69:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}
70:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}
71:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}
72:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}
73:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1
74:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}
75:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}
76:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}
77:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env
78:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}
79:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
80:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts
81:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
82:     steps:
83:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
84:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
85:         with:
86:           no-sudo: true
87: 
88:       - name: Configure AWS credentials(PyTorch account) for nightly
89:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.event.ref == 'refs/heads/nightly' }}
90:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
91:         with:
92:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::749337293305:role/gha_workflow_nightly_build_wheels
93:           aws-region: us-east-1
94: 
95:       - name: Configure AWS credentials(PyTorch account) for RC builds
96:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' &&  (startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/')) }}
97:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
98:         with:
99:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::749337293305:role/gha_workflow_test_build_wheels
100:           aws-region: us-east-1
101: 
102:       - name: Download Build Artifacts
103:         id: download-artifacts
104:         # NB: When the previous build job is skipped, there won't be any artifacts and
105:         # this step will fail. Binary build jobs can only be skipped on CI, not nightly
106:         continue-on-error: true
107:         uses: actions/download-artifact@65a9edc5881444af0b9093a5e628f2fe47ea3b2e # v4.1.7
108:         with:
109:           name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}
110:           path: "${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/"
111: 
112:       - name: Set DRY_RUN (only for tagged pushes)
113:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.event.ref == 'refs/heads/nightly' || (startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/'))) }}
114:         run: |
115:           echo "DRY_RUN=disabled" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
116: 
117:       - name: Set UPLOAD_CHANNEL (only for tagged pushes)
118:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}
119:         shell: bash -e -l {0}
120:         run: |
121:           # reference ends with an RC suffix
122:           if [[ "${GITHUB_REF_NAME}" = *-rc[0-9]* ]]; then
123:             echo "UPLOAD_CHANNEL=test" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
124:           fi
125: 
126:       - name: Upload binaries
127:         if: steps.download-artifacts.outcome && steps.download-artifacts.outcome == 'success'
128:         shell: bash
129:         env:
130:           PKG_DIR: "${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts"
131:           UPLOAD_SUBFOLDER: "${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}"
132:           BUILD_NAME: ${{ inputs.build_name }}
133:         run: |
134:             set -ex
135:             bash .circleci/scripts/binary_upload.sh
136: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml"}

{"content":"1: name: build docs
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       docker-image:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Docker image to run in.
14:       push:
15:         required: false
16:         type: boolean
17:         default: false
18:         description: If set, push the docs to the docs website.
19:       run-doxygen:
20:         required: false
21:         type: boolean
22:         default: false
23:         description: If set, will enable C++ API doc generation using doxygen / breathe / exhale.
24:       sync-tag:
25:         required: false
26:         type: string
27:         default: ""
28:         description: |
29:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
30:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
31:       s3-bucket:
32:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
33:         required: false
34:         type: string
35:         default: "gha-artifacts"
36:       aws-role-to-assume:
37:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts
38:         required: false
39:         type: string
40:         default: ""
41:       upload-aws-role-to-assume:
42:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts
43:         required: false
44:         type: string
45:         default: ""
46:       runner_prefix:
47:         description: prefix for runner label
48:         type: string
49:         default: ""
50:     secrets:
51:       GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN:
52:         required: false
53:         description: Permissions for pushing to the docs site.
54: 
55: jobs:
56:   build-docs:
57:     # Don't run on forked repos.
58:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
59:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
60:     environment: ${{ (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'pytorchbot-env' || '' }}
61:     strategy:
62:       fail-fast: false
63:       matrix:
64:         include:
65:           - docs_type: cpp
66:             # We recently seeing lots of exit code 137 running this in Docker indicating
67:             # an OOM issue when running the job, so this upgrades the runner from 4xlarge
68:             # to the next available tier of 12xlarge. So much memory just to generate cpp
69:             # doc
70:             runner: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix }}linux.12xlarge.memory
71:             # TODO: Nightly cpp docs take longer and longer to finish (more than 3h now)
72:             # Let's try to figure out how this can be improved
73:             timeout-minutes: 360
74:           - docs_type: python
75:             runner: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix }}linux.c7i.2xlarge
76:             # It takes less than 30m to finish python docs unless there are issues
77:             timeout-minutes: 30
78:     # Set a fixed name for this job instead of using the current matrix-generated name, i.e. build-docs (cpp, linux.12xlarge, 180)
79:     # The current name requires updating the database last docs push query from test-infra every time the matrix is updated
80:     name: build-docs-${{ matrix.docs_type }}-${{ inputs.push }}
81:     steps:
82:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
83:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
84:         with:
85:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
86:           instructions: |
87:             All builds are done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:
88:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash
89:             To start Python docs build type:
90:               cd docs && make html && make coverage
91: 
92:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]
93:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
94:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
95: 
96:       - name: Setup Linux
97:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
98: 
99:       - name: configure aws credentials
100:         if : ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' }}
101:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
102:         with:
103:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}
104:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test
105:           aws-region: us-east-1
106: 
107:       - name: Calculate docker image
108:         id: calculate-docker-image
109:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
110:         with:
111:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
112: 
113:       - name: Pull docker image
114:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
115:         with:
116:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
117: 
118:       - name: Download build artifacts
119:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
120:         with:
121:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
122:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
123: 
124:       - name: Generate netrc (only for docs-push)
125:         if: inputs.push
126:         env:
127:           GITHUB_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN }}
128:         run: |
129:           # sometimes .netrc exists as a directory even though this is the temp folder
130:           rm -rf "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc"
131:           # set credentials for https pushing
132:           echo "machine github.com" > "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc"
133:           echo "login pytorchbot" >> "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc"
134:           echo "password ${GITHUB_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN}" >> "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc"
135: 
136:       - name: Build ${{ matrix.docs_type }} docs
137:         timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.timeout-minutes }}
138:         id: build-docs
139:         env:
140:           # After https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/88373, pull workflow can now be run periodically,
141:           # so using a schedule event to determine if the docs should be pushed or not doesn't hold true
142:           # anymore
143:           WITH_PUSH: ${{ inputs.push }}
144:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
145:           DOCS_TYPE: ${{ matrix.docs_type }}
146:           RUN_DOXYGEN: ${{ inputs.run-doxygen }}
147:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
148:         run: |
149:           set -ex
150:           # Convert refs/tags/v1.12.0rc3 into 1.12
151:           if [[ "${GITHUB_REF}" =~ ^refs/tags/v([0-9]+\.[0-9]+)\.* ]]; then
152:             target="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
153:           else
154:             target="main"
155:           fi
156:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
157:           container_name=$(docker run \
158:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
159:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
160:             -e SHA1="$GITHUB_SHA" \
161:             -e DOCS_VERSION="${target}" \
162:             -e DOCS_TYPE \
163:             -e RUN_DOXYGEN \
164:             -e WITH_PUSH \
165:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
166:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
167:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
168:             --tty \
169:             --detach \
170:             --user jenkins \
171:             -v "${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc":/var/lib/jenkins/.netrc \
172:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
173:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
174:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
175:           )
176:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" bash -c "sudo chown -R jenkins . && pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ./.ci/pytorch/${DOCS_TYPE}_doc_push_script.sh"
177: 
178:       - name: Chown workspace
179:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace
180:         if: always()
181: 
182:       - name: configure aws credentials
183:         if : ${{ inputs.upload-aws-role-to-assume != '' }}
184:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
185:         with:
186:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.upload-aws-role-to-assume }}
187:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test
188:           aws-region: us-east-1
189: 
190:       - name: Upload Python Docs Preview
191:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
192:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && matrix.docs_type == 'python' && steps.build-docs.outcome == 'success' }}
193:         with:
194:           retention-days: 14
195:           s3-bucket: doc-previews
196:           if-no-files-found: error
197:           path: pytorch_docs/main/
198:           s3-prefix: pytorch/pytorch/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
199: 
200:       - name: Upload C++ Docs Preview
201:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
202:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && matrix.docs_type == 'cpp' && steps.build-docs.outcome == 'success' }}
203:         with:
204:           retention-days: 14
205:           if-no-files-found: error
206:           s3-bucket: doc-previews
207:           path: cppdocs/
208:           s3-prefix: pytorch/pytorch/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/cppdocs
209: 
210:       - name: Teardown Linux
211:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
212:         if: always()

{"content":"1: name: Get Changed Files
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       all_files:
7:         description: "Whether to return all files instead of just changed files"
8:         required: false
9:         type: boolean
10:         default: false
11:     outputs:
12:       changed-files:
13:         description: "List of changed files (space-separated) or '*' if not in a PR"
14:         value: ${{ jobs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}
15: 
16: jobs:
17:   get-changed-files:
18:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19:     outputs:
20:       changed-files: ${{ steps.get-files.outputs.changed-files }}
21: 
22:     steps:
23:       - name: Get changed files
24:         id: get-files
25:         env:
26:           GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
27:         run: |
28:           # Check if we're in a pull request context
29:           if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ] || [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request_target" ]; then
30:             echo "Running in PR context"
31: 
32:             # Get the PR number from the github context
33:             PR_NUMBER="${{ github.event.number }}"
34: 
35:             # Check if all_files is requested
36:             if [ "${{ inputs.all_files }}" = "true" ]; then
37:               echo "all_files input is true, returning all files"
38:               echo "changed-files=*" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
39:             else
40:               # Use gh CLI to get changed files in the PR with explicit repo
41:               CHANGED_FILES=$(gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/pulls/$PR_NUMBER/files --paginate --jq '.[] | select(.status != "removed") | .filename' | tr '\n' ' ' | sed 's/ $//')
42: 
43:               # See https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/134215#issuecomment-2332128790
44:               PYI_FILES_TO_ADD=""
45:               for file in ${CHANGED_FILES}; do
46:                 if [[ "${file}" == *".pyi.in" ]]; then
47:                   PYI_FILES_TO_ADD="${PYI_FILES_TO_ADD} ${file//.in/}"
48:                 fi
49:               done
50:               CHANGED_FILES="${CHANGED_FILES}${PYI_FILES_TO_ADD}"
51: 
52:               if [ -z "$CHANGED_FILES" ]; then
53:                 echo "No changed files found, setting to '*'"
54:                 CHANGED_FILES="*"
55:               fi
56: 
57:               echo "Changed files: $CHANGED_FILES"
58:               echo "changed-files=$CHANGED_FILES" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
59:             fi
60: 
61:           else
62:             echo "Not in PR context, setting changed files to '*'"
63:             echo "changed-files=*" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
64:           fi
65: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@76b8fdafad537579cd97b5ec2502c6ac3bc473e0/-/blob/.github/workflows/_get-changed-files.yml"}

{"content":"1: on:
2:   workflow_call:
3:     inputs:
4:       runner:
5:         type: string
6:         required: true
7:       ref:
8:         type: string
9:         required: true
10: 
11: jobs:
12:   lint-urls:
13:     if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' || !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-url-lint') }}
14:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
15:     with:
16:       job-name: lint-urls
17:       timeout: 120
18:       runner: ${{ inputs.runner }}linux.2xlarge
19:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
20:       fetch-depth: 0
21:       submodules: false
22:       ref: ${{ inputs.ref }}
23:       script: |
24:         ./scripts/lint_urls.sh $(
25:           if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
26:             echo "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}" "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"
27:           else
28:             echo "${{ github.event.before }}" "${{ github.sha }}"
29:           fi
30:         ) || {
31:           echo
32:           echo "URL lint failed."
33:           echo "If this is a transient outage, you can bypass it by adding the \`skip-url-lint\` label to your PR."
34:           echo "Or add \`@lint-ignore\` somewhere on the same line as the URL you want to skip checking."
35:           exit 1
36:         }
37: 
38:   lint-xrefs:
39:     if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' || !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-xref-lint') }}
40:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@main
41:     with:
42:       job-name: lint-xrefs
43:       timeout: 60
44:       runner: ${{ inputs.runner }}linux.2xlarge
45:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
46:       fetch-depth: 0
47:       submodules: false
48:       ref: ${{ inputs.ref }}
49:       script: |
50:         ./scripts/lint_xrefs.sh $(
51:           if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
52:             echo "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}" "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"
53:           else
54:             echo "${{ github.event.before }}" "${{ github.sha }}"
55:           fi
56:         ) || {
57:           echo
58:           echo "Xref lint failed."
59:           echo "If this is a transient outage, you can bypass it by adding the \`skip-xref-lint\` label to your PR."
60:           echo "Or add \`@lint-ignore\` somewhere on the same line as the reference you want to skip checking."
61:           exit 1
62:         }
