{"content":"1: name: linux-build
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       docker-image-name:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Name of the base docker image to build with.
14:       build-generates-artifacts:
15:         required: false
16:         type: boolean
17:         default: true
18:         description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.
19:       sync-tag:
20:         required: false
21:         type: string
22:         default: ""
23:         description: |
24:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
25:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
26:       cuda-arch-list:
27:         required: false
28:         type: string
29:         default: "5.2"
30:         description: |
31:           List of CUDA architectures CI build should target.
32:       runner_prefix:
33:         required: false
34:         default: ""
35:         type: string
36:         description: Prefix for runner label
37:       runner:
38:         required: false
39:         type: string
40:         default: "linux.c7i.2xlarge"
41:         description: |
42:           Label of the runner this job should run on.
43:       test-matrix:
44:         required: false
45:         type: string
46:         description: |
47:           An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This
48:           is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter
49:           logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds
50:       selected-test-configs:
51:         description: |
52:           A comma-separated list of test configurations from the test matrix to keep,
53:           The empty list means we are going to keep every configurations by defaults
54:         required: false
55:         type: string
56:         default: ""
57:       s3-bucket:
58:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
59:         required: false
60:         type: string
61:         default: "gha-artifacts"
62:       aws-role-to-assume:
63:         description: Role to assume for downloading artifacts
64:         required: false
65:         type: string
66:         default: ""
67:       disable-monitor:
68:         description: |
69:           Disable utilization monitoring for build job
70:         required: false
71:         type: boolean
72:         default: false
73:       monitor-log-interval:
74:         description: |
75:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
76:         required: false
77:         type: number
78:         default: 5
79:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
80:         description: |
81:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
82:         required: false
83:         type: number
84:         default: 1
85:       allow-reuse-old-whl:
86:         description: |
87:           If set, the build try to pull an old wheel from s3 that was built on a
88:           commit with no cpp changes from this commit
89:         required: false
90:         type: boolean
91:         default: true
92:       build-additional-packages:
93:         description: |
94:           If set, the build job will also builds these packages and saves their
95:           wheels as artifacts
96:         required: false
97:         type: string
98:         default: ""
99:       build-external-packages:
100:         description: |
101:           If set, the build external packages and saves their wheels as artifacts
102:           use command separated list of packages to build ex: 'vllm,transformers'.
103:         required: false
104:         type: string
105:         default: ""
106: 
107:     secrets:
108:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
109:         required: false
110:         description: |
111:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
112:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:
113:         required: false
114:         description: |
115:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint
116: 
117:     outputs:
118:       docker-image:
119:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.docker-image }}
120:         description: The docker image containing the built PyTorch.
121:       test-matrix:
122:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}
123:         description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.
124:       build-environment:
125:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}
126:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
127: 
128: jobs:
129:   build:
130:     environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}
131:     # Don't run on forked repos
132:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
133:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runner }}
134:     timeout-minutes: 480
135:     outputs:
136:       docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
137:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}
138:       build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
139:     steps:
140:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
141:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main
142:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
143:         with:
144:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
145:           instructions: |
146:             Build is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:
147:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash
148: 
149:       # [pytorch repo ref]
150:       # Use a pytorch/pytorch reference instead of a reference to the local
151:       # checkout because when we run this action we don't *have* a local
152:       # checkout. In other cases you should prefer a local checkout.
153:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
154:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
155:         with:
156:           no-sudo: true
157: 
158:       - name: Setup Linux
159:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
160:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
161: 
162:       - name: configure aws credentials
163:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
164:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
165:         with:
166:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}
167:           role-session-name: gha-linux-build
168:           aws-region: us-east-1
169: 
170:       - name: Get workflow job id
171:         id: get-job-id
172:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
173:         if: always()
174:         with:
175:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
176: 
177:       - name: Check if can use old whl build
178:         id: use-old-whl
179:         uses: ./.github/actions/reuse-old-whl
180:         if: ${{ inputs.allow-reuse-old-whl }}
181:         with:
182:           build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
183:           run-id: ${{ github.run_id }}
184:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
185:           job-id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
186:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
187: 
188:       - name: Calculate docker image
189:         id: calculate-docker-image
190:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
191:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
192:         with:
193:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}
194: 
195:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
196:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
197:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
198:         env:
199:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
200:         shell: bash
201:         run: |
202:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
203:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
204: 
205:       - name: Pull docker image
206:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
207:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
208:         with:
209:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
210: 
211:       - name: Parse ref
212:         id: parse-ref
213:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
214: 
215:       # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there
216:       - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)
217:         id: filter
218:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
219:         with:
220:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
221:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
222:           selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.selected-test-configs }}
223:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
224: 
225:       - name: Start monitoring script
226:         id: monitor-script
227:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
228:         shell: bash
229:         continue-on-error: true
230:         env:
231:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
232:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
233:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
234:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
235:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
236:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
237:         run: |
238:           mkdir -p ../../usage_logs
239:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7
240:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor \
241:           --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" \
242:           --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" \
243:           > "../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_${JOB_ID}.txt" 2>&1 &
244:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
245: 
246:       - name: Download pytest cache
247:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download
248:         continue-on-error: true
249:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
250:         with:
251:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache
252:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}
253:           s3_bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
254: 
255:       - name: Build
256:         if: (steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == '') && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
257:         id: build
258:         env:
259:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
260:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
261:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
262:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
263:           # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs
264:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2
265:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1
266:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla
267:           PR_LABELS: ${{ toJson(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name) }}
268:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}
269:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
270:           DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}
271:           XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}
272:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
273:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
274:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
275:           BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES: ${{ inputs.build-additional-packages }}
276:           RUNNER: ${{ inputs.runner }}
277:         run: |
278:           START_TIME=$(date +%s)
279:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"s390x"* ]]; then
280:             JENKINS_USER=
281:             USED_IMAGE="${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}"
282:             # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours
283:             # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container
284:             # when job is cancelled
285:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD="sleep 12h"
286: 
287:             # since some steps are skipped on s390x, if they are necessary, run them here
288:             env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
289:             env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
290:           else
291:             JENKINS_USER="--user jenkins"
292:             USED_IMAGE="${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
293:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=
294:           fi
295: 
296:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things
297:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf "%.3f \n", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)
298:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap
299:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058
300:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$(("${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}" + 3))
301: 
302:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"riscv64"* ]]; then
303:             # EC2 specific setup for RISC-V emulation
304:             # Ensure binfmt_misc is available
305:             echo "Mounting binfmt_misc filesystem"
306:             sudo mount binfmt_misc -t binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc 2>/dev/null || true
307: 
308:             echo "QEMU registration: multiarch/qemu-user-static"
309:             docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes || true
310: 
311:             # Final verification
312:             echo "Checking binfmt_misc status:"
313:             ls -la /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/ 2>/dev/null || echo "Cannot access binfmt_misc directory"
314: 
315:             if [ -f /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/qemu-riscv64 ]; then
316:               echo "qemu-riscv64 registration successful"
317:             else
318:               echo "qemu-riscv64 registration failed - proceeding without emulation"
319:               echo "This may cause RISC-V builds to fail"
320:             fi
321: 
322:             RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS="--privileged"
323:           else
324:             RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=
325:           fi
326: 
327:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
328:           # Used for JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD, which can be empty
329:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086
330:           container_name=$(docker run \
331:             ${RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS} \
332:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
333:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
334:             -e PR_NUMBER \
335:             -e SHA1 \
336:             -e BRANCH \
337:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \
338:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \
339:             -e XLA_CUDA \
340:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \
341:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \
342:             -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \
343:             -e PR_LABELS \
344:             -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \
345:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \
346:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \
347:             -e BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES \
348:             -e RUNNER \
349:             --memory="${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g" \
350:             --memory-swap="${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g" \
351:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
352:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
353:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
354:             --tty \
355:             --detach \
356:             ${JENKINS_USER} \
357:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
358:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
359:             "${USED_IMAGE}" \
360:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}
361:           )
362: 
363:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"s390x"* ]]; then
364:             docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt"
365:           fi
366: 
367:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'
368: 
369:           END_TIME=$(date +%s)
370:           echo "build_time=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
371: 
372:       - name: Build external packages
373:         id: build-external-packages
374:         if: inputs.build-external-packages != '' &&  steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'
375:         uses: ./.github/actions/build-external-packages
376:         with:
377:           build-targets: ${{ inputs.build-external-packages }}
378:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
379:           cuda-arch-list: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}
380:           output-dir: external
381: 
382:       - name: Move external packages to dist
383:         if: steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir != '' && steps.build-external-packages.outcome != 'skipped'
384:         shell: bash
385:         run: |
386:           src="${{ steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir }}"
387:           if [ -d "$src" ]; then
388:             mkdir -p "dist/$(dirname "$src")"
389:             mv "$src" "dist/$(dirname "$src")/"
390:           fi
391: 
392:       - name: Stop monitoring script
393:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
394:         shell: bash
395:         continue-on-error: true
396:         env:
397:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}
398:         run: |
399:           kill "$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID"
400: 
401:       - name: Archive artifacts into zip
402:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
403:         run: |
404:           zip -1 -r artifacts.zip dist/ build/custom_test_artifacts build/lib build/bin .additional_ci_files
405: 
406:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts on S3
407:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
408:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
409:         with:
410:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
411:           retention-days: 14
412:           if-no-files-found: error
413:           path: artifacts.zip
414:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
415: 
416:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts for s390x
417:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2
418:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
419:         with:
420:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
421:           retention-days: 14
422:           if-no-files-found: error
423:           path: artifacts.zip
424: 
425:       - name: copy logs
426:         shell: bash
427:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}
428:         continue-on-error: true
429:         run: |
430:           rm -f ./usage_logs
431:           mkdir -p ./usage_logs
432:           cp ../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt ./usage_logs/
433: 
434:       - name: Upload raw usage log to s3
435:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}
436:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5
437:         with:
438:           s3-prefix: |
439:             ${{ github.repository }}/${{ github.run_id }}/${{ github.run_attempt }}/artifact
440:           retention-days: 14
441:           if-no-files-found: warn
442:           path: usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt
443: 
444:       - name: Upload sccache stats
445:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
446:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-sccache-stats
447:         with:
448:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
449:           build-time: ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}
450: 
451:       - name: Upload utilization stats
452:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
453:         continue-on-error: true
454:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
455:         with:
456:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
457:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
458:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
459:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
460:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
461:           artifact_prefix: usage_log_build_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
462: 
463:       - name: Teardown Linux
464:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
465:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
466: 
467:       - name: Cleanup docker
468:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
469:         shell: bash
470:         run: |
471:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop
472:           docker stop -a || true
473:           docker kill -a || true

{"content":"1: # The point of this workflow is to test that a FA3 wheel that was built based off the
2: # stable ABI as of torch nightly 20250830 can still run on the newer torch.
3: #
4: # This workflow is very similar to the _linux-test.yml workflow, with the following
5: # differences:
6: #   1. It is simpler (there is no test matrix)
7: #   2. It pulls flash-attention as a secondary repository in order to access the tests.
8: #      Note that it does not BUILD anything from flash-attention, as we have a prebuilt
9: #      wheel. We pull flash-attention only to run a few tests.
10: #   3. It runs only FA3 tests. No PyTorch tests are run.
11: name: linux-test-stable-fa3
12: 
13: on:
14:   workflow_call:
15:     inputs:
16:       build-environment:
17:         required: true
18:         type: string
19:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
20:       docker-image:
21:         required: true
22:         type: string
23:         description: Docker image to run in.
24:       timeout-minutes:
25:         required: false
26:         type: number
27:         default: 30
28:         description: |
29:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish
30:       s3-bucket:
31:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
32:         required: false
33:         type: string
34:         default: "gha-artifacts"
35:     secrets:
36:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
37:         required: false
38:         description: |
39:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
40:       VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:
41:         required: false
42:         description: |
43:           HF Auth token to test vllm
44:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:
45:         required: false
46:         description: |
47:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint
48: 
49: env:
50:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
51: 
52: jobs:
53:   test:
54:     # Don't run on forked repos
55:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
56:     runs-on: linux.aws.h100
57:     timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes || 30 }}
58:     permissions:
59:       id-token: write
60:       contents: read
61:     steps:
62:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
63:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main
64:         with:
65:           no-sudo: true
66: 
67:       - name: Checkout flash-attention as a secondary repository
68:         uses: actions/checkout@v4
69:         with:
70:           repository: Dao-AILab/flash-attention
71:           path: flash-attention
72: 
73:       - name: Setup Linux
74:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
75: 
76:       - name: Calculate docker image
77:         id: calculate-docker-image
78:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main
79:         with:
80:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
81: 
82:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
83:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
84:         env:
85:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
86:         shell: bash
87:         run: |
88:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
89:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
90: 
91:       - name: Pull docker image
92:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main
93:         with:
94:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
95: 
96:       - name: Check if in a container runner
97:         shell: bash
98:         id: check_container_runner
99:         run: echo "IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
100: 
101:       - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run
102:         id: setup-gpu-flag
103:         run: echo "GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
104: 
105:       - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container
106:         id: setup-sscache-port-flag
107:         run: echo "SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
108:         if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}
109: 
110:       - name: Get workflow job id
111:         id: get-job-id
112:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
113:         if: always()
114:         with:
115:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
116: 
117:       - name: Download build artifacts
118:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts
119:         with:
120:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
121:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
122: 
123:       - name: Parse ref
124:         id: parse-ref
125:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
126: 
127:       - name: Set Test step time
128:         id: test-timeout
129:         shell: bash
130:         env:
131:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}
132:         run: |
133:           echo "timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
134: 
135:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker
136:         shell: bash
137:         run: |
138:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
139:           env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
140: 
141:       - name: Test
142:         id: test
143:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}
144:         env:
145:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
146:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
147:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
148:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}
149:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}
150:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
151:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}
152:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}
153:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
154:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
155:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
156:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
157:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}
158:           SHM_SIZE: '2g'
159:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}
160:           VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN }}
161:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
162:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
163:           ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX: ${{ github.job }}-${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
164:         run: |
165:           set -x
166: 
167:           TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test_fa3_abi_stable.sh
168: 
169:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things
170:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf "%.3f \n", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)
171:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap
172:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058
173:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$(("${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}" + 3))
174: 
175: 
176:           SHM_OPTS="--shm-size=${SHM_SIZE}"
177:           JENKINS_USER="--user jenkins"
178:           DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=
179: 
180:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux
181:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase
182:           # Used for GPU_FLAG, SHM_OPTS, JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD since that doesn't play nice
183:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090
184:           container_name=$(docker run \
185:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \
186:             ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \
187:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \
188:             -e PR_NUMBER \
189:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \
190:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \
191:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \
192:             -e GITHUB_JOB \
193:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \
194:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \
195:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \
196:             -e JOB_ID \
197:             -e JOB_NAME \
198:             -e BASE_SHA \
199:             -e BRANCH \
200:             -e SHA1 \
201:             -e MAX_JOBS="$(nproc --ignore=2)" \
202:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \
203:             -e VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN \
204:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \
205:             -e ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX \
206:             --memory="${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g" \
207:             --memory-swap="${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g" \
208:             --env-file="/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" \
209:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
210:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \
211:             --ipc=host \
212:             ${SHM_OPTS} \
213:             --tty \
214:             --detach \
215:             --name="${container_name}" \
216:             ${JENKINS_USER} \
217:             -v "${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace" \
218:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \
219:             "${DOCKER_IMAGE}" \
220:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}
221:           )
222: 
223:           echo "DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}" >> "${GITHUB_ENV}"
224: 
225:           docker exec -t "${container_name}" sh -c "python3 -m pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}"
226: 
227:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)
228:         if: always()
229:         run: |
230:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156
231:           find . -iname "core.[1-9]*" -exec docker exec "${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}" sh -c "gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'" \;
232: 
233:       - name: Store Core dumps on S3
234:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0
235:         if: failure()
236:         with:
237:           name: coredumps-fa3-stable-abi-smoke-tests
238:           retention-days: 14
239:           if-no-files-found: ignore
240:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*
241: 
242:       - name: Upload utilization stats
243:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' }}
244:         continue-on-error: true
245:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats
246:         with:
247:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
248:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
249:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}
250:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}
251:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}
252: 
253:       - name: Teardown Linux
254:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@main
255:         if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'
